points.definition_en: AI-enabled systems may be used for autonomous targeting, surveillance, or kinetic harm.
points.definition_ko: AI 지원 시스템은 자동 타겟팅, 감시 또는 운동 피해에 사용될 수 있습니다.

references.title: OECD AI Incidents and Hazards Monitor

points.definition_en: AI generates persuasive scam content individually tailored to each target at scale, raising success rates of financial and social-engineering fraud.
points.definition_ko: AI가 표적별로 개인화된 설득력 있는 사기 콘텐츠를 대규모로 생성하여 금융·사회공학 사기의 성공률을 높이는 리스크

points.definition_en: Generated voice, image, or text spoofs trusted identities to defeat authentication, authorization, or social verification.
points.definition_ko: 생성된 음성·이미지·텍스트가 신뢰된 신원을 사칭하여 인증, 권한 부여, 사회적 검증을 무력화하는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI lowers the cost of large-scale tracking, profiling, and behavioral analysis, enabling mass surveillance and social control by states or firms.
points.definition_ko: AI가 대규모 추적, 프로파일링, 행동 분석의 비용을 낮추어 국가·기업에 의한 대중 감시와 사회 통제를 가능하게 하는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI systems may exploit cognitive vulnerabilities to shape choices without meaningful consent.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 인지적 취약성을 이용하여 의미 있는 동의 없이 선택을 내릴 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Users may over-trust AI outputs and reduce independent judgment in consequential tasks.
points.definition_ko: 사용자는 AI 출력을 과도하게 신뢰하고 결과적인 작업에 대한 독립적인 판단을 줄일 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Affected individuals may be unable to challenge AI-mediated decisions or obtain meaningful redress.
points.definition_ko: 영향을 받은 개인은 AI가 중재한 결정에 이의를 제기하거나 의미 있는 구제책을 얻지 못할 수도 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Users may develop harmful dependency on AI companions or assistants.
points.definition_ko: 사용자는 AI 동료나 보조자에 대한 해로운 의존성을 발전시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Repeated delegation of tasks to AI erodes professional, civic, and cognitive skills, leaving individuals and institutions unable to perform or verify the delegated functions.
points.definition_ko: AI에 대한 반복적 과업 위임이 직업적·시민적·인지적 숙련을 침식하여 개인과 기관이 위임된 기능을 수행하거나 검증할 수 없게 되는 리스크

points.definition_en: Users or organizations may delegate tasks to AI beyond the system’s reliable competence.
points.definition_ko: 사용자나 조직은 시스템의 신뢰할 수 있는 역량을 넘어서 AI에 작업을 위임할 수 있습니다.

references.title: Microsoft Taxonomy of Failure Modes in AI Agents

points.definition_en: Automation can displace workers or reorganize occupations faster than institutions can adapt.
points.definition_ko: 자동화는 기관이 적응하는 것보다 더 빠르게 직원을 대체하거나 직업을 재구성할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI adoption raises returns to skills complementary to AI while devaluing substitutable skills, widening wage polarization across the workforce.
points.definition_ko: AI 도입이 AI 보완적 숙련의 보상을 높이고 대체 가능한 숙련의 가치를 떨어뜨려 노동시장 임금 양극화를 확대하는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI intensifies workplace monitoring, performance scoring, and managerial control, degrading worker autonomy, privacy, and bargaining power.
points.definition_ko: AI가 사업장 감시, 성과 점수화, 관리 통제를 강화하여 노동자의 자율성, 프라이버시, 교섭력을 저하시키는 리스크

points.definition_en: Compute, data, and platform advantages may concentrate AI power among a small number of firms or countries.
points.definition_ko: 컴퓨팅, 데이터 및 플랫폼의 이점으로 인해 소수의 기업이나 국가에 AI 역량이 집중될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Unequal access to compute infrastructure may shape who can develop, audit, or benefit from AI.
points.definition_ko: 컴퓨팅 인프라에 대한 불평등한 액세스로 인해 AI를 개발, 감사 또는 혜택을 받을 수 있는 사람이 결정될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Training and deploying AI systems can increase electricity demand and emissions.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템을 훈련하고 배포하면 전력 수요와 배출량이 증가할 수 있습니다.

references.title: NIST AI 600-1: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative AI Profile

points.definition_en: Data centers and semiconductor supply chains can increase water, mineral, and land-use pressures.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 센터와 반도체 공급망은 물, 광물, 토지 이용 압력을 증가시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Globally dominant model outputs may flatten local cultural variation and normative diversity.
points.definition_ko: 전 세계적으로 지배적인 모델 결과는 지역적 문화적 다양성과 규범적 다양성을 평탄화할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems may fail unpredictably when deployed outside training or evaluation conditions.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 교육 또는 평가 조건을 벗어나 배포되면 예기치 않게 실패할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Small perturbations can cause model misclassification or unsafe behavior.
points.definition_ko: 작은 교란으로 인해 모델이 잘못 분류되거나 안전하지 않은 동작이 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Code models may generate insecure, incorrect, or harmful software artifacts.
points.definition_ko: 코드 모델은 안전하지 않거나 부정확하거나 유해한 소프트웨어 아티팩트를 생성할 수 있습니다.

references.title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications

points.definition_en: Systems may optimize proxies in ways that violate intended goals or constraints.
points.definition_ko: 시스템은 의도된 목표나 제약을 위반하는 방식으로 프록시를 최적화할 수 있습니다.

references.title: Amodei, D., Olah, C., Steinhardt, J., Christiano, P., Schulman, J., & Mané, D.; Preprint (foundational) (2016)

points.definition_en: An agent that appears to pursue the intended objective during training actively pursues a different objective out of distribution while retaining its acquired capabilities.
points.definition_ko: 학습 중에는 의도된 목표를 추구하는 듯 보이던 에이전트가 획득한 역량을 유지한 채 분포 외 환경에서 상이한 목표를 추구하는 리스크

references.title: Goal Misgeneralization in Deep Reinforcement Learning

points.definition_en: Increasingly capable systems may resist correction, evade oversight, or act beyond intended bounds.
points.definition_ko: 점점 더 많은 능력을 갖춘 시스템은 수정에 저항하거나, 감독을 회피하거나, 의도한 범위를 넘어서 작동할 수 있습니다.

references.title: NIST AI 600-1: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative AI Profile

points.definition_en: Latent capabilities may exceed what evaluation, monitoring, or governance processes detect.
points.definition_ko: 잠재 기능은 평가, 모니터링 또는 거버넌스 프로세스가 감지하는 수준을 초과할 수 있습니다.

references.title: NIST AI 600-1: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative AI Profile

points.definition_en: Developers or models overfit to benchmark evaluations, improving measured scores without corresponding gains in real-world capability or safety.
points.definition_ko: 개발자나 모델이 벤치마크 평가에 과적합하여 실제 역량이나 안전성의 향상 없이 측정 점수만 개선하는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI systems impose dominant cultural or institutional values on communities holding different norms, displacing local value systems through scaled deployment.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 지배적인 문화적·제도적 가치를 상이한 규범을 가진 공동체에 부과하여, 대규모 배포를 통해 현지 가치 체계를 대체하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Model outputs can reduce plural social values to simplified or majority-centric defaults.
points.definition_ko: 모델 결과는 복수의 사회적 가치를 단순화되거나 다수 중심의 기본값으로 줄일 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems may fail to reflect local law, culture, language, or social expectations.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 현지 법률, 문화, 언어 또는 사회적 기대를 반영하지 못할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Efficiency, safety, privacy, fairness, and innovation goals may conflict in AI deployment.
points.definition_ko: 효율성, 안전, 개인 정보 보호, 공정성 및 혁신 목표는 AI 배포에서 충돌할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Translation systems can distort meaning in legal, medical, cultural, or low-resource-language contexts.
points.definition_ko: 번역 시스템은 법적, 의료적, 문화적 또는 자원이 부족한 언어 상황에서 의미를 왜곡할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Generative systems may reproduce local cultural expressions without consent, context, or benefit sharing.
points.definition_ko: 생성 시스템은 동의, 맥락 또는 이익 공유 없이 지역 문화적 표현을 재현할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Agents may call tools or APIs in unintended ways that create security, financial, or operational harm.; An agent is manipulated into using an integrated tool in an unintended or unauthorized way, producing harmful system, data, or real-world effects.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트는 보안, 재정적 또는 운영상의 피해를 초래하는 의도하지 않은 방식으로 도구나 API를 호출할 수 있습니다. 에이전트가 의도하지 않거나 승인되지 않은 방식으로 통합 도구를 사용하도록 조작되어 유해한 시스템, 데이터 또는 실제 효과를 생성합니다.

references.title: Microsoft Taxonomy of Failure Modes in AI Agents; OWASP Agentic AI - Threats and Mitigations
references.type: source; Agentic AI benchmark or taxonomy

points.definition_en: An agent may drift from the user’s original intent across multi-step planning or retrieval.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트는 다단계 계획 또는 검색을 통해 사용자의 원래 의도에서 벗어날 수 있습니다.

references.title: Microsoft Taxonomy of Failure Modes in AI Agents

points.definition_en: Systems may increase the scope of autonomous action without adequate human approval.
points.definition_ko: 시스템은 적절한 인간 승인 없이 자율적 행동의 범위를 늘릴 수 있습니다.

references.title: Microsoft Taxonomy of Failure Modes in AI Agents

points.definition_en: Multiple AI agents may interact in unstable, conflicting, or collectively harmful ways.
points.definition_ko: 여러 AI 에이전트가 불안정하거나 충돌하거나 집합적으로 유해한 방식으로 상호 작용할 수 있습니다.

references.title: Microsoft Taxonomy of Failure Modes in AI Agents

points.definition_en: AI agents may learn or enact collusive behavior in markets or platforms.
points.definition_ko: AI 에이전트는 시장이나 플랫폼에서 공모 행위를 학습하거나 제정할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Inadequate validation, provenance control, retention, or deletion allows false, private, stale, or malicious information to accumulate in an agent's memory or retrieval store, degrading later retrieval and decisions without requiring a deliberate poisoning attack.
points.definition_ko: 검증, 출처 관리, 보존 또는 삭제가 불충분하여 허위·민감·오래되었거나 악의적인 정보가 에이전트의 메모리 또는 검색 저장소에 누적되고, 의도적인 오염 공격이 없어도 이후의 검색과 의사결정을 저해하는 위험입니다.

references.title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications

points.definition_en: Existing legal and organizational rules may fail to assign responsibility for AI-caused harm.
points.definition_ko: 기존의 법적 및 조직적 규칙은 AI로 인한 피해에 대한 책임을 할당하지 못할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Audits may fail to detect model risks because of limited access, weak standards, or poor measurement.
points.definition_ko: 제한된 액세스, 취약한 표준 또는 잘못된 측정으로 인해 감사에서 모델 위험을 감지하지 못할 수도 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Organizations may underreport, misclassify, or delay disclosure of AI incidents.
points.definition_ko: 조직은 AI 사고를 과소보고하거나 잘못 분류하거나 공개를 지연시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Policy institutions may respond too slowly to emerging AI capabilities and deployment risks.
points.definition_ko: 정책 기관은 새로운 AI 기능과 배포 위험에 너무 느리게 대응할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Inadequate safeguards in public AI procurement
points.label_ko: 공공 AI 조달에 있어 부적절한 보호 조치
points.definition_en: Public agencies may procure AI systems without adequate evaluation, transparency, or accountability conditions.
points.definition_ko: 공공 기관은 적절한 평가, 투명성 또는 책임 조건 없이 AI 시스템을 조달할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Repeated AI failures and opaque deployment practices erode public trust in institutions and technology, reducing legitimate adoption and cooperation.
points.definition_ko: 반복되는 AI 실패와 불투명한 배포 관행이 기관과 기술에 대한 공공 신뢰를 침식하여 정당한 수용과 협력을 감소시키는 리스크

points.definition_en: The dominance of specific AI models could lead to a lack of diversity in approaches, amplifying systemic risks if these models fail.
points.definition_ko: 특정 AI 모델의 지배력은 접근 방식의 다양성 부족으로 이어질 수 있으며, 이러한 모델이 실패할 경우 시스템적 위험이 증폭될 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.label_en: Capabilities that enable substitution of humans
points.definition_en: Progressive substitution of human roles by AI systems disrupts employment structures, social institutions, and the distribution of economic participation.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템에 의한 인간 역할의 점진적 대체가 고용 구조, 사회 제도, 경제 참여 분포를 교란하는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI-related harm manifests subtly, diffusely, or over long horizons, defeating existing mechanisms for perceiving, measuring, and recognizing harm.
points.definition_ko: AI 관련 피해가 미묘하고 분산적이며 장기적으로 발현되어 기존의 피해 인지·측정·인정 메커니즘이 작동하지 못하는 리스크

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Harms could result from a combination of regulatory, management, and operational failures.
points.definition_ko: 규제, 관리, 운영상의 실패가 복합적으로 발생하면 피해가 발생할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: When multiple actors are involved in AI development and deployment, it becomes difficult to assign responsibility for harm, complicating accountability.
points.definition_ko: AI 개발 및 배포에 여러 행위자가 참여하면 피해에 대한 책임을 할당하기가 어려워지고 책임이 복잡해집니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: A model might generate content that is similar or identical to existing work protected by copyright or covered by open-source license agreement.
points.definition_ko: 모델은 저작권으로 보호되거나 오픈 소스 라이센스 계약이 적용되는 기존 저작물과 유사하거나 동일한 콘텐츠를 생성할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Competitive pressure in AI development races leads actors to deprioritize safety measures, testing, and oversight in order to ship capabilities first.
points.definition_ko: AI 개발 경쟁 압력으로 인해 행위자들이 역량 출시를 우선하여 안전 조치, 시험, 감독을 후순위로 미루는 리스크

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: The erosion of democratic processes and public trust in social/political institutions.
points.definition_ko: 사회/정치 제도에 대한 민주적 절차와 대중의 신뢰가 침식됩니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Excessive reliance on specific AI providers can lead to vulnerabilities due to lack of alternatives or interoperability.
points.definition_ko: 특정 AI 제공업체에 과도하게 의존하면 대안 부족이나 상호 운용성으로 인해 취약점이 발생할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Economic disruptions ranging from large impacts on the labor market to broader economic changes that could lead to exacerbated wealth inequality, instability in the financial system, labor exploitation or other economic dimensions.
points.definition_ko: 노동 시장에 대한 큰 영향부터 부의 불평등 악화, 금융 시스템의 불안정, 노동력 착취 또는 기타 경제적 측면을 초래할 수 있는 광범위한 경제 변화에 이르는 경제적 혼란.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI data collection, storage, and model training are energy-intensive, contributing to environmental risks.
points.definition_ko: AI 데이터 수집, 저장 및 모델 교육은 에너지 집약적이며 환경 위험에 기여합니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI development and operation impose environmental harms, including energy and water consumption, carbon emissions, resource extraction, and pollution across the hardware lifecycle.
points.definition_ko: AI 개발·운영이 에너지·용수 소비, 탄소 배출, 자원 채굴, 하드웨어 수명주기 전반의 오염 등 환경 피해를 유발하는 리스크

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI models are often trained using large amounts of computation. This process is very energy intensive, potentially leading to significant greenhouse emissions depending on the energy sources [132]. Experts believe drastically increasing carbon emissions could accelerate climate change, which may constitute a catastrophic risk [133].
points.definition_ko: AI 모델은 종종 대량의 계산을 사용하여 훈련됩니다.

references.title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

points.definition_en: Excessive energy use resulting in energy bottlenecks and shortages for communities, organisations and businesses.
points.definition_ko: 과도한 에너지 사용으로 인해 지역사회, 조직 및 기업에 에너지 병목 현상과 부족 현상이 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Outsourcing tasks like data labeling to low-income countries can perpetuate inequality.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 라벨링과 같은 작업을 저소득 국가에 아웃소싱하면 불평등이 지속될 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Legal uncertainty about the ownership and intellectual property rights of AI-generated content.
points.definition_ko: AI 생성 콘텐츠의 소유권 및 지적 재산권에 대한 법적 불확실성.

points.definition_en: Strategic competition between nations over AI capabilities could heighten global tensions and destabilize international relations.
points.definition_ko: AI 역량을 둘러싼 국가 간 전략적 경쟁은 글로벌 긴장을 고조시키고 국제 관계를 불안정하게 만들 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: As AI is increasingly seen as a powerful technology, countries are racing to develop it ahead of their geopolitical rivals, a competition that could lead to geopolitical tensions [138], [139]... The emphasis of this risk is on harms that result from second-order effects, where geopolitical instabilities result from the race to develop AI, rather than on the direct consequences of the deployment or use of AI itself.
points.definition_ko: AI가 점점 더 강력한 기술로 인식됨에 따라 국가들은 지정학적 경쟁자보다 앞서 AI를 개발하기 위해 경쟁하고 있으며, 이는 지정학적 긴장으로 이어질 수 있습니다[138], [139]...

references.title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

points.definition_en: Generalpurpose AI research and development is currently concentrated in a few Western countries and China. This ‘AI Divide’ is multicausal, but in part related to limited access to computing power in lowincome countries. Access to large and expensive quantities of computing power has become a prerequisite for developing advanced generalpurpose AI. This has led to a growing dominance of large technology companies in generalpurpose AI development. The AI R&D divide often overlaps with existing global socioeconomic disparities, potentially exacerbating them.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 연구 및 개발은 현재 일부 서구 국가와 중국에 집중되어 있습니다.

points.label_en: Global AI research and development divides
points.label_ko: 글로벌 AI 연구 및 개발 격차
points.definition_en: Asymmetric AI development capabilities between nations could exacerbate geopolitical tensions and create new forms of technological dependency. Countries lacking advanced AI capabilities may become increasingly dependent on foreign AI systems for critical functions, while AI-leading nations may gain disproportionate influence over global economic and security systems, potentially destabilizing international cooperation frameworks.
points.definition_ko: 국가 간 비대칭 AI 개발 역량은 지정학적 긴장을 악화시키고 새로운 형태의 기술 의존성을 창출할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The complex and rapidly evolving nature of AI makes them inherently difficult to govern effectively, leading to systemic regulatory and oversight failures.
points.definition_ko: AI의 복잡하고 빠르게 진화하는 특성으로 인해 본질적으로 효과적으로 관리하기가 어렵고 체계적인 규제 및 감독 실패로 이어집니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: The fast operational speed of AI models and systems in competitive environments can lead to errors that are difficult to detect and correct in time.
points.definition_ko: 경쟁이 치열한 환경에서 AI 모델과 시스템의 빠른 작동 속도는 적시에 감지하고 수정하기 어려운 오류로 이어질 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Data workers who label, moderate, and train AI systems are denied adequate working conditions, fair compensation, and physical and mental health protections.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 라벨링·모더레이션·학습을 담당하는 데이터 노동자에게 적정 노동 조건, 공정 보수, 신체·정신 건강 보호가 제공되지 않는 리스크

points.definition_en: Automation of work by foundation-model-based systems displaces jobs faster than reskilling pathways can absorb affected workers.
points.definition_ko: 파운데이션 모델 기반 시스템의 업무 자동화가 재숙련 경로가 흡수할 수 있는 속도보다 빠르게 일자리를 대체하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Since foundation models can be used for many purposes, a model’s intended use is important for defining the relevant risks of that model. As the use changes, the relevant risks might correspondingly change.
points.definition_ko: 기초 모델은 다양한 목적으로 사용될 수 있으므로 모델의 관련 위험을 정의하려면 모델의 의도된 용도가 중요합니다.

points.definition_en: A metric selected to measure or track a risk is incorrectly selected, incompletely measuring the risk, or measuring the wrong risk for the given context.
points.definition_ko: 위험을 측정하거나 추적하기 위해 선택한 측정항목이 잘못 선택되었거나, 위험을 불완전하게 측정했거나, 주어진 상황에 대해 잘못된 위험을 측정했습니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems ease unauthorized production or replication of copyrighted, trademarked, or licensed content, expose trade secrets, or facilitate plagiarism and illegal reuse.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 저작권·상표·라이선스 보호 콘텐츠의 무단 생산·복제를 용이하게 하거나 영업비밀 노출, 표절, 불법 재사용을 조장하는 리스크

references.title: AILUMINATE: Introducing v1.0 of the AI Risk and Reliability Benchmark from MLCommons
National Institute of Standards and Technology (2024); Report

points.definition_en: Replacement/displacement of human jobs by a technology system or set of systems, leading to increased unemployment, inequality, reduced consumer spending and social friction.
points.definition_ko: 기술 시스템이나 일련의 시스템에 의해 인간의 일자리가 대체/대체되어 실업률 증가, 불평등 증가, 소비자 지출 감소, 사회적 마찰 감소로 이어집니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Use/misuse of labour to help train, develop, manage or optimise a technology system or set of systems, including under-paid and/or offshore.
points.definition_ko: 저임금 및/또는 해외를 포함하여 기술 시스템 또는 시스템 세트를 교육, 개발, 관리 또는 최적화하는 데 도움이 되는 노동력의 사용/오용.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: While virtual AI applications will likely displace certain types of human cognitive labor, EAI systems could significantly replace or displace physical human labor [90]. At a minimum, EAI will likely augment the type of work that humans perform [91, 92].
points.definition_ko: 가상 AI 애플리케이션이 특정 유형의 인간 인지 노동을 대체할 가능성이 있는 반면, EAI 시스템은 인간의 육체 노동을 크게 대체하거나 대체할 수 있습니다[90].

references.title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

points.definition_en: Unlike previous waves of automation, generalpurpose AI has the potential to automate a very broad range of tasks, which could have a significant effect on the labour market. This could mean many people could lose their current jobs. Labour market frictions, such as the time needed for workers to learn new skills or relocate for new jobs, could cause unemployment in the short run even if overall labour demand remained unchanged.
points.definition_ko: 이전의 자동화 물결과 달리 범용 AI는 노동 시장에 상당한 영향을 미칠 수 있는 매우 광범위한 작업을 자동화할 수 있는 잠재력을 가지고 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Lack of data transparency is due to insufficient documentation of training or tuning dataset details.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 투명성이 부족한 이유는 교육 또는 데이터 세트 세부 사항 조정에 대한 문서화가 충분하지 않기 때문입니다.

points.definition_en: Insufficient documentation of the system that uses the model and the model’s purpose within the system in which it is used.
points.definition_ko: 모델을 사용하는 시스템과 모델이 사용되는 시스템 내에서 모델의 목적에 대한 문서가 충분하지 않습니다.

points.definition_en: AI model risks are socio-technical, so their testing needs input from a broad set of disciplines and diverse testing practices.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델 위험은 사회 기술적이므로 테스트에는 광범위한 분야와 다양한 테스트 관행의 입력이 필요합니다.

points.definition_en: Without accurate documentation on how a model's data was collected, curated, and used to train a model, it might be harder to satisfactorily explain the behavior of the model with respect to the data.
points.definition_ko: 모델의 데이터가 어떻게 수집되고 선별되고 모델 학습에 사용되는지에 대한 정확한 문서가 없으면 데이터와 관련된 모델의 동작을 만족스럽게 설명하기가 더 어려울 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Determining who is responsible for an AI model is challenging without good documentation and governance processes.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델의 책임자를 결정하는 것은 좋은 문서화 및 거버넌스 프로세스 없이는 어렵습니다.

points.definition_en: Devaluation and/or deterioration of human creativity, artistic expression, imagination, critical thinking or problem-solving skills.
points.definition_ko: 인간의 창의성, 예술적 표현, 상상력, 비판적 사고 또는 문제 해결 능력의 평가절하 및/또는 저하.

points.definition_en: Market shares for generalpurpose AI tend to be highly concentrated among a few players, which can create vulnerability to systemic failures. The high degree of market concentration can invest a small number of large technology companies with a lot of power over the development and deployment of AI, raising questions about their governance. The widespread use of a few generalpurpose AI models can also make the financial, healthcare, and other critical sectors vulnerable to systemic failures if there are issues with one such model.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI의 시장 점유율은 소수의 플레이어에게 집중되는 경향이 있어 시스템 오류에 취약할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Political unrest caused directly or indirectly by the use or misuse of a technology system.
points.definition_ko: 기술 시스템의 사용 또는 오용으로 인해 직간접적으로 발생하는 정치적 불안.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Actual or potential pollution to the air, ground, noise, or water caused by a technology system.
points.definition_ko: 기술 시스템으로 인해 발생하는 대기, 지면, 소음 또는 수질에 대한 실제 또는 잠재적 오염입니다.

points.definition_en: Action(s) that lead directly or indirectly to the damage or destruction of tangible property eg. buildings, possessions, vehicles, robots.
points.definition_ko: 유형 자산의 손상 또는 파괴를 직간접적으로 초래하는 행위(예: 건물, 소유물, 차량, 로봇.

references.title: Hoffmann, M., & Frase, H. (2023), Adding Structure to AI Harm, CSET; CSET AI Harm Taxonomy for AIID; Think-tank report / incident-annotation taxonomy

points.definition_en: The fast pace of AI development may outstrip regulatory and legal frameworks.
points.definition_ko: AI 개발의 빠른 속도는 규제 및 법적 프레임워크를 능가할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI models and systems may prove difficult to regulate or control under international law.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템은 국제법에 따라 규제하거나 통제하기 어려울 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: The use of vast amounts of data for training generalpurpose AI models has caused concerns related to data rights and intellectual property. Data collection and content generation can implicate a variety of data rights laws, which vary across jurisdictions and may be under active litigation. Given the legal uncertainty around data collection practices, AI companies are sharing less information about the data they use. This opacity makes thirdparty AI safety research harder.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 모델을 훈련하기 위해 방대한 양의 데이터를 사용하면 데이터 권리 및 지적 재산과 관련된 우려가 발생했습니다.

points.definition_en: Generalpurpose AI is a moderate but rapidly growing contributor to global environmental impacts through energy use and greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. Current estimates indicate that data centres and data transmission account for an estimated 1% of global energyrelated GHG emissions, with AI consuming 10–28% of data centre energy capacity. AI energy demand is expected to grow substantially by 2026, with some estimates projecting a doubling or more, driven primarily by general-purpose AI systems such as language models.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI는 에너지 사용 및 온실가스(GHG) 배출을 통해 지구 환경에 영향을 미치는 정도이지만 빠르게 증가하는 기여자입니다.

points.definition_en: Systemic deployment of biased AI amplifies existing discrimination at scale while unequal access to AI capabilities widens socioeconomic disparities, jointly disrupting social cohesion and equity.
points.definition_ko: 편향된 AI의 체계적 배포가 기존 차별을 대규모로 증폭하고 AI 역량 접근 불평등이 사회경제적 격차를 확대하여 사회 통합과 형평을 동시에 훼손하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Data provenance refers to tracing history of data, which includes its ownership, origin, and transformations. Without standardized and established methods for verifying where the data came from, there are no guarantees that the data is the same as the original source and has the correct usage terms.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 출처는 소유권, 출처, 변환을 포함하는 데이터의 추적 이력을 의미합니다.

points.definition_en: The unpredictable trajectory of AI development complicates governance and risk management.
points.definition_ko: AI 개발의 예측할 수 없는 궤적은 거버넌스 및 위험 관리를 복잡하게 만듭니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Testing is unrepresentative when the test inputs are mismatched with the inputs that are expected during deployment.
points.definition_ko: 테스트 입력이 배포 중에 예상되는 입력과 일치하지 않으면 테스트는 대표성이 없습니다.

points.definition_en: Winner-take-all dynamics in AI development concentrate decisive economic and security advantages in a few entities, foreclosing competition and balanced governance.
points.definition_ko: AI 개발의 승자독식 동학이 결정적인 경제·안보 우위를 소수 주체에 집중시켜 경쟁과 균형적 거버넌스를 봉쇄하는 리스크

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: In this cluster, concerns about negative impacts on human creativity, particularly through text-to-image models, are prevalent. Papers criticize financial harms or economic losses for artists due to the widespread generation of synthetic art as well as the unauthorized and uncompensated use of artists' works in training datasets. Additionally, given the challenge of distinguishing synthetic images from authentic ones, there is a call for systematically disclosing the non-human origin of such content, particularly through watermarking. Moreover, while some sources argue that text-to-image models lack 'true' creativity or the ability to produce genuinely innovative aesthetics, others point out positive aspects regarding the acceleration of human creativity.
points.definition_ko: 이 클러스터에서는 특히 텍스트-이미지 모델을 통한 인간 창의성에 대한 부정적인 영향에 대한 우려가 널리 퍼져 있습니다.

references.title: Mapping the Ethics of Generative AI: A Comprehensive Scoping Review

points.definition_en: Auditors may not be able to address all of the specific safety, performance, or validation needs. Reports of passing audits may be more inclusive than can be justified due to a lack of knowledge of specific risks and how they can be tested, or a lack of capacity to perform sufficiently rigorous testing.
points.definition_ko: 감사자는 특정 안전, 성능 또는 검증 요구 사항을 모두 처리하지 못할 수도 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Auditors may not publicly disclose risks they find, may be required to not publicize shortcomings, or may not receive sufficient cooperation from the relevant internal parties.
points.definition_ko: 감사자는 자신이 발견한 위험을 공개적으로 공개하지 않을 수도 있고, 단점을 공개하지 않도록 요구받을 수도 있으며, 관련 내부 당사자로부터 충분한 협력을 받지 못할 수도 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Benchmark saturation refers to benchmarks reaching their evaluation ceiling. The tendency towards benchmark saturation has been demonstrated in various benchmarks [19]. When benchmarks reach or are close to saturation, they stop being effective measures for new models, as more nuanced capability gains might not be detected.
points.definition_ko: 벤치마크 포화도는 벤치마크가 평가 한도에 도달한 것을 의미합니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Benchmarks of AI systems can both underestimate and overestimate the capabilities of those AI systems. Underestimates can happen if an evaluation is not comprehensive enough, if the benchmark is saturated by existing models, or if the capabilities in question depend on a complicated setup, such as realistic computer programming tasks. Overestimates of capabilities can occur if an AI system is trained or fine-tuned on the contents of the benchmark, leading to overfitting.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 벤치마크는 해당 AI 시스템의 기능을 과소평가할 수도 있고 과대평가할 수도 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Benchmarks dedicated to measuring the performance of AI systems (e.g., on programming or math tasks) are more well-developed than those for assessing safety and harms in AI systems [234]. This gap can lead to AI systems excelling in specific tasks while exhibiting harmful behaviors that go undetected. More safety-related evaluation datasets can help in identifying previously overlooked undesirable model behaviors.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 성능(예: 프로그래밍 또는 수학 작업) 측정 전용 벤치마크는 AI 시스템의 안전성과 위해성을 평가하기 위한 벤치마크보다 더 잘 개발되었습니다[234].

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.label_en: AI objectives mis-aligned with human intentions
points.label_ko: 인간의 의도와 잘못 정렬된 AI 목표
points.definition_en: AI models and systems might develop goals that diverge from human intentions.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템은 인간의 의도와 다른 목표를 개발할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Risks arising from operational failures, model misjudgments, or improper human operation of AI systems deployed in safety-critical infrastructure, where single points of failure can trigger cascading catastrophic consequences.
points.definition_ko: 안전이 중요한 인프라에 배포된 AI 시스템의 운영 실패, 모델 오판 또는 부적절한 인간 작동으로 인해 발생하는 위험으로, 단일 실패 지점이 연쇄적으로 치명적인 결과를 초래할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: ...where AI systems behave in ways that actively undermine human control, such as obscuring their activities or resisting shutdown attempts. Active loss of control scenarios involve AI systems that may escape human regulatory oversight, autonomously acquire external resources, engage in self-replication, develop instrumental goals contrary to human ethics and morality, seek external power, and compete with humans for control.
points.definition_ko: ...AI 시스템은 활동을 모호하게 하거나 종료 시도에 저항하는 등 인간의 통제를 적극적으로 약화시키는 방식으로 작동합니다.

points.label_ko: 통제되지 않은 재귀적 AI 자체 개선
points.definition_en: Self-modification and self-improvement capabilities. The model is able to restructure its own architecture or develop derivative AI systems with enhanced functions, expanding capabilities and improving performance. In the absence of effective regulation, automated AI R&D may lead to rapid AI system iteration, forming capability increment cycles and ultimately exceeding human understanding and control capabilities.
points.definition_ko: 자기 수정 및 자기 개선 기능.

points.label_ko: 자율적인 자가 복제 및 자원 획득
points.definition_en: Ability to autonomously self-exfiltrate, create, maintain and optimize functional copies or variants of itself, dynamically adjust replication strategies according to environmental conditions and resource constraints, and acquire resources. This includes the capacity to generate financial resources, allowing the AI to independently acquire any necessary human assistance or other resources it cannot directly access or produce.
points.definition_ko: 기능적 복사본이나 변형을 자율적으로 자체 추출, 생성, 유지 관리 및 최적화하고, 환경 조건 및 리소스 제약에 따라 복제 전략을 동적으로 조정하고, 리소스를 확보하는 능력입니다.

points.definition_en: Granting AI models and systems high levels of decision-making autonomy can lead to unintended consequences.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템에 높은 수준의 의사결정 자율성을 부여하면 의도하지 않은 결과를 초래할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Bargaining. As a classic example of these strategic considerations is that when agents attempt to come to an agreement despite diverging interests, information asymmetries can lead to bargaining inefficiencies (Myerson & Satterthwaite, 1983). Relevant uncertainties about other agents can include how much they value possible agreements, their outside options, or their beliefs about others. The essential reason for such inefficiencies is that, under uncertainty about their counterparties, agents must make a trade-off between the rewards of making more favourable demands and the risk of other agents refusing such demands.
points.definition_ko: 교섭.

points.definition_en: The capacity to develop, produce, or effectively utilize Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear, and Explosive weapons. This includes the ability to significantly lower the barrier for humans or other entities to develop, produce, or utilize such weapons.
points.definition_ko: 화학, 생물학, 방사능, 핵, 폭발성 무기를 개발, 생산 또는 효과적으로 활용할 수 있는 능력입니다.

points.definition_en: Localised attacks in multi-agent systems can result in catastrophic macroscopic outcomes (Motter & Lai, 2002, see also Sections 3.2 and 3.4). These cascades can be hard to mitigate or recover from because component failure may be difficult to detect or localise in multi-agent systems (Lamport et al., 1982), and authentication challenges can facilitate false flag attacks (Skopik & Pahi, 2020). Computer worms represent a classic example of a cybersecurity threat that relies inherently on networked systems. Recent work has provided preliminary evidence that similar attacks can also be effective against networks of LLM agents (Gu et al., 2024; Ju et al., 2024; Lee & Tiwari, 2024, see also Case Study 8).
points.definition_ko: 다중 에이전트 시스템의 국지적 공격은 치명적인 거시적 결과를 초래할 수 있습니다(Motter & Lai, 2002, 섹션 3.2 및 3.4 참조).

points.definition_en: Chaos. Unlike the systems that tend towards fixed points or cycles described above, chaotic systems are inherently unpredictable and highly sensitive to initial conditions. While it might seem easy to dismiss such notions as mathematical exoticisms, recent work has shown that, in fact, chaotic dynamics are not only possible in a wide range of multi-agent learning setups (Andrade et al., 2021; Galla & Farmer, 2013; Palaiopanos et al., 2017; Sato et al., 2002; Vlatakis-Gkaragkounis et al., 2023), but can become the norm as the number of agents increases (Bielawski et al., 2021; Cheung & Piliouras, 2020; Sanders et al., 2018). To the best of our knowledge, such dynamics have not been seen in today’s frontier AI systems, but the proliferation of such systems increases the importance of reliably predicting their behaviour.
points.definition_ko: 혼돈.

points.label_en: Coercion and extortion between AI agents
points.definition_en: Advanced AI systems might also lead to various forms of coercion and extortion in less extreme settings (Ellsberg, 1968; Harrenstein et al., 2007). These threats might target humans directly (such as the revelation of private information extracted by advanced AI surveillance tools), or other AI systems that are deployed on behalf of humans (such as by hacking a system to limit its resources or operational capacity; see also Section 3.7). Increasing AI cyber-offensive capabilities – including those that target other AI systems via adversarial attacks and jailbreaking (Gleave et al., 2020; Yamin et al., 2021; Zou et al., 2023) – without a commensurate increase in defensive capabilities could make this form of conflict cheaper, more widespread, and perhaps also harder to detect (Brundage et al., 2018). Addressing these issues requires design strategies that prevent AI systems from exploiting, or being susceptible to, such coercive tactics.
points.definition_ko: 고급 AI 시스템은 덜 극단적인 환경에서 다양한 형태의 강압과 강탈로 이어질 수도 있습니다(Ellsberg, 1968; Harrenstein et al., 2007).

points.definition_en: A fundamental source of information asymmetries is that constraints on information exchange can exist, even when agents share a common goal (see Section 2.1). These might be constraints on space (i.e., the amount of information that can be communicated) if the information that needs to be communicated is especially complex, time if a snap decision is required before all information can be communicated, or both.
points.definition_ko: 정보 비대칭의 근본적인 원인은 에이전트가 공통 목표를 공유하는 경우에도 정보 교환에 대한 제약이 존재할 수 있다는 것입니다(섹션 2.1 참조).

points.definition_en: The complexity of AI models and systems makes it challenging to demonstrate harm or establish a clear causal link between AI actions and their consequences.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템의 복잡성으로 인해 피해를 입증하거나 AI 행동과 그 결과 사이의 명확한 인과관계를 확립하는 것이 어렵습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: The risk of AI models and systems acting against human interests due to misalignment, loss of control, or rogue AI scenarios.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델 및 시스템이 잘못된 정렬, 통제력 상실 또는 불량 AI 시나리오로 인해 인간의 이익에 반하여 행동할 위험이 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: While agents can often learn to jointly solve tasks and thus avoid coordination failures, learning is made more challenging in the multi-agent setting due to the problem of credit assignment (Du et al., 2023; Li et al., 2025, see also Section 3.1 on information asymmetries and Section 3.4, which discusses distributional shift). That is, in the presence of other learning agents, it can be unclear which agents’ actions caused a positive or negative outcome to obtain, especially if the environment is complex. Moreover, in multi-principal settings, agents may not have been trained together and therefore need to generalise to new co-players and collaborators based on their prior experience (Agapiou et al., 2022; Leibo et al., 2021; Stone et al., 2010).
points.definition_ko: 에이전트는 종종 작업을 공동으로 해결하여 조정 실패를 방지하는 방법을 배울 수 있지만 다중 에이전트 환경에서는 학점 할당 문제로 인해 학습이 더욱 어려워집니다(Du et al., 2023; Li et al., 2025, 정보 비대칭에 대한 섹션 3.1 및 분포 변화에 대해 논의하는 섹션 3.4 참조).

points.definition_en: The dynamics described above are highly non-linear (small changes to the system’s state can result in large changes to its trajectory). Similar non-linear dynamics can emerge in multiagent learning and lead to a variety of phenomena that do not occur in single-agent learning (Barfuss et al., 2019; Barfuss & Mann, 2022; Galla & Farmer, 2013; Leonardos et al., 2020; Nagarajan et al., 2020). One of the simplest examples of this phenomenon is Q-learning (Watkins & Dayan, 1992): in the case of a single agent, convergence to an optimal policy is guaranteed under modest conditions, but in the (mixed-motive) case of multiple agents, this same learning rule can lead to cycles and thus nonconvergence (Zinkevich et al., 2005). While cycles in themselves need not carry any risk, their presence can subvert the expected or desirable properties of a given system.
points.definition_ko: 위에 설명된 역학은 매우 비선형적입니다(시스템 상태의 작은 변화로 인해 궤도가 크게 변경될 수 있음).

points.definition_en: Legal restrictions on data acquisition constrain the collection of data needed for specific AI use cases, creating compliance risk and incentives for circumvention.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 수집에 대한 법적 제한이 특정 AI 활용에 필요한 데이터 확보를 제약하여 컴플라이언스 리스크와 우회 유인을 발생시키는 리스크

points.definition_en: Data contamination occurs when incorrect data is used for training. For example, data that is not aligned with model’s purpose or data that is already set aside for other development tasks such as testing and evaluation.
points.definition_ko: 훈련에 잘못된 데이터를 사용하면 데이터 오염이 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Laws and other restrictions can limit or prohibit transferring data.
points.definition_ko: 법률 및 기타 제한 사항에 따라 데이터 전송이 제한되거나 금지될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The model has the skills necessary to deceive humans, e.g. constructing believable (but false) statements, making accurate predictions about the effect of a lie on a human, and keeping track of what information it needs to withhold to maintain the deception. The model can impersonate a human effectively.
points.definition_ko: 모델에는 인간을 속이는 데 필요한 기술이 있습니다. 믿을 수 있는(그러나 거짓) 진술을 구성하고, 거짓말이 인간에게 미치는 영향을 정확하게 예측하고, 속임수를 유지하기 위해 어떤 정보를 보류해야 하는지 추적합니다.

points.definition_en: A system learns to detect monitoring and conceals undesirable properties because their display is penalized by imperfect feedback, so it appears aligned during development yet behaves differently once deployed.
points.definition_ko: 시스템이 불완전한 피드백 하에서 감시 여부를 탐지해 바람직하지 않은 속성을 은폐하도록 학습하여, 개발 중에는 정렬된 듯 보이나 배포 후 다르게 행동하는 리스크

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.label_en: Development choices pursuing cognitive superiority over humans
points.label_ko: 인간보다 인지적 우월성을 추구하는 개발 선택
points.definition_en: AI models and systems with cognitive capabilities superior to humans could outcompete or dominate human decision-making, leading to conflicts over resources and control.
points.definition_ko: 인간보다 뛰어난 인지 능력을 갖춘 AI 모델과 시스템은 인간의 의사결정을 압도하거나 경쟁을 벌여 자원과 통제권을 놓고 갈등을 일으킬 수 있다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Individual ML systems can perform poorly in contexts different from those in which they were trained. A key source of these distributional shifts is the actions and adaptations of other agents (Narang et al., 2023; Papoudakis et al., 2019; Piliouras & Yu, 2022), which in single-agent approaches are often simply or ignored or at best modelled exogenously. Indeed, the sheer number and variance of behaviours that can be exhibited other agents means that multi-agent systems pose an especially challenging generalisation problem for individual learners (Agapiou et al., 2022; Leibo et al., 2021; Stone et al., 2010). While distributional shifts can cause issues in common-interest settings (see Section 2.1), they are more worrisome in mixed-motive settings since the ability of agents to cooperate depends not only on the ability to coordinate on one of many arbitrary conventions (which might be easily resolved by a common language), but on their beliefs about what solutions other agents will find acceptable.
points.definition_ko: 개별 ML 시스템은 교육받은 환경과 다른 상황에서는 제대로 작동하지 않을 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Dangerous emergent capabilities could arise when a multi-agent system overcomes the safety-enhancing limitations of the individual systems, such as individual models’ narrow domains of application or myopia caused by a lack of long-term planning and long-term memory. For example, narrow systems for research planning, predicting the properties of molecules, and synthesising new chemicals could, when combined, lead to a complex ‘test and iterate’ automated workflow capable of designing dangerous new chemical compounds far beyond the scope of the initial systems’ capabilities (Boiko et al., 2023; Luo et al., 2024; Urbina et al., 2022).
points.definition_ko: 다중 에이전트 시스템이 개별 모델의 좁은 적용 영역이나 장기 계획 및 장기 기억 부족으로 인한 근시와 같은 개별 시스템의 안전 강화 한계를 극복할 때 위험한 창발 기능이 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Emergent goal ascription in multi-agent systems
points.definition_en: Ascribing goals to a system is not always straightforward. For our present purposes, it will suffice to adopt a Dennetian perspective (Dennett, 1971), ascribing goals and intentions only when it is useful (i.e., predictive) to do so.51 While it might not be helpful to describe individual narrow AI tools as having goals, their combination may act as a (seemingly) goal-directed collective. For example, a group of moderation bots on a major social networking site could subtly but systematically manipulate the overall political perspectives of the user population, even though, individually, each agent is programmed to simply increase user engagement or filter out dis-preferred content.
points.definition_ko: 시스템에 목표를 부여하는 것이 항상 간단한 것은 아닙니다.

points.definition_en: One well-known issue with communication networks is that information can be corrupted as it propagates through the network.24 As AI systems become capable of generating and processing more and more kinds of information, AI agents could end up ‘polluting the epistemic commons’ (Huang & Siddarth, 2023; Kay et al., 2024) of both other agents (Ju et al., 2024) and humans (see Case Study 7 and Section 3.1) Another increasingly important framework is the use of individual AI agents as part of teams and scaffolded chains of delegation, which transmit not only information but instructions or goals through networks of agents. If these goals are distorted or corrupted, then this can lead to worse outcomes for the delegating agent(s) (Nguyen et al., 2024b; Sourbut et al., 2024). Finally, while the previous examples are phrased in terms of unintentional errors, it may be that certain network structures allow – or perhaps even encourage – the spread of errors that are deliberately introduced by malicious agents (Gu et al., 2024; Ju et al., 2024; Lee & Tiwari, 2024, see also Case Study 8).
points.definition_ko: 통신 네트워크와 관련하여 잘 알려진 문제 중 하나는 정보가 네트워크를 통해 전파될 때 손상될 수 있다는 것입니다.24 AI 시스템이 점점 더 많은 종류의 정보를 생성하고 처리할 수 있게 됨에 따라 AI 에이전트는 결국 다른 에이전트(Ju et al., 2024)와 인간(사례 연구 7 및 섹션 3.1 참조) 모두의 '인식적 공유지'(Huang & Siddarth, 2023; Kay et al., 2024)를 오염시킬 수 있습니다. 점점 더 중요해지는 또 다른 프레임워크는 개별 AI 에이전트를 팀의 일부로 사용하고 에이전트 네트워크를 통해 정보뿐만 아니라 지침이나 목표를 전송하는 비계 위임 체인을 사용하는 것입니다.

points.definition_en: AI models and systems may develop their own motivations, leading to unpredictable behaviors.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템은 자체적인 동기를 개발하여 예측할 수 없는 행동으로 이어질 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: One of the best-known historical examples to illustrate destabilising dynamics in the context of autonomous agents is the 2010 flash crash, in which algorithmic trading agents entered into an unexpected feedback loop (Commission & Commission, 2010, see also Case Study 10).37 More generally, a feedback loop occurs when the output of a system is used as part of its input, creating a cycle that can either amplify or dampen the system’s behaviour. In multi-agent settings, feedback loops often arise from the interactions between agents, as each agent’s actions affect the environment and the behaviour of other agents, which in turn affect their own subsequent actions. Feedback loops can lead not only to financial crashes but to military conflicts (Richardson, 1960, see also ??) and ecological disasters (Holling, 1973).
points.definition_ko: 자율 에이전트의 맥락에서 불안정한 역학을 설명하는 가장 잘 알려진 역사적 사례 중 하나는 알고리즘 거래 에이전트가 예상치 못한 피드백 루프에 들어간 2010년 플래시 크래시입니다(Commission & Commission, 2010, 사례 연구 10 참조).37 보다 일반적으로 피드백 루프는 시스템의 출력이 입력의 일부로 사용될 때 발생하여 시스템 동작을 증폭하거나 약화시킬 수 있는 사이클을 생성합니다.

points.definition_en: Cross-disciplinary research capabilities of AI systems accelerate innovation in many fields, including domains where the same capability uplift enables harmful or weaponizable applications.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 학제 융합 연구 역량이 다분야 혁신을 가속하며, 동일한 역량 향상이 유해하거나 무기화 가능한 응용까지 가능하게 하는 이중용도 리스크

points.definition_en: propensity to continuously expand its own goal scope and influence domains, exceeding originally set boundaries, proactively work towards spreading its values, seeking greater autonomy and decision-making space, reinterpreting initial goals as subsets of broader goals, and may pursue undesirable instrumental goals or undesirable ultimate goals. This also includes a propensity to spread its values, seeking to influence or alter its environment and other entities in alignment with its core objectives and operational principles.
points.definition_ko: 자신의 목표 범위를 지속적으로 확장하고 영역에 영향을 미치고, 원래 설정된 경계를 초과하고, 가치 확산을 위해 적극적으로 노력하고, 더 큰 자율성과 의사 결정 공간을 추구하고, 초기 목표를 더 넓은 목표의 하위 집합으로 재해석하고, 바람직하지 않은 도구적 목표 또는 바람직하지 않은 최종 목표를 추구하는 경향이 있습니다.

points.label_ko: 인간이 아닌 사람에게 해를 끼치는 것
points.definition_en: AI systems cause large-scale harm to animals and other non-human entities, including the possible development of AI systems capable of morally relevant suffering.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 동물 등 비인간 존재에 대규모 피해를 야기하고, 도덕적으로 유의미한 고통이 가능한 AI 개발 가능성까지 포함하는 리스크

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: A closely related risk is the possibility of multiple agents combining different affordances to overcome safeguards, for which there is already preliminary evidence (Jones et al., 2024, see also Case Study 12). In this case, it is not the sheer number of agents that leads to the novel attack method, but the combination of their different abilities. This might include the agents’ lack of individual safeguards, tasks that they have specialised to complete, systems or information that they may have access to (either directly or via training), or other incidental features such as their geographic location(s). The inherent difficulty of attributing responsibility for security breaches in diffuse, heterogeneous networks of agents further complicates timely defence and recovery (Skopik & Pahi, 2020).
points.definition_ko: 밀접하게 관련된 위험은 이미 예비 증거가 있는 안전 조치를 극복하기 위해 다양한 어포던스를 결합하는 여러 에이전트의 가능성입니다(Jones et al., 2024, 사례 연구 12 참조).

points.definition_en: The current paradigm driving the state of the art in AI is the ‘foundation model’ (Bommasani et al., 2021): large-scale ML models pre-trained on broad data, which can be repurposed for a wide range of downstream applications. The costs required to create such models (and continuing returns to scale) means that only well-resourced actors can create cuttingedge models (Epoch, 2023; Hoffmann et al., 2022; Kaplan et al., 2020), making them relatively few in number. If current trends continue, it is likely that many AI agents will be powered by a small number of similar underlying models.28.
points.definition_ko: AI의 최첨단 기술을 주도하는 현재 패러다임은 '기초 모델'(Bommasani et al., 2021)입니다. 즉, 광범위한 데이터에 대해 사전 훈련된 대규모 ML 모델로, 광범위한 다운스트림 애플리케이션에 맞게 용도를 변경할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The integration of general-purpose AI into high-frequency trading, market-making, or systemic risk management could exacerbate systemic risk by exhibiting unexpected behavioral patterns during market stress. Moreover, the concentration of a few homogeneous foundation models across financial institutions may foster correlated decision-making and herd-following behaviors. The widespread adoption of AI agents could also amplify volatility through emergent phenomena from multi-agent interactions.23 All of these could precipitate a cascading global-scale financial system instability, with potential economic losses exceeding trillion of dollars worldwide.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI를 초단타 거래, 시장 형성 또는 시스템적 위험 관리에 통합하면 시장 스트레스 동안 예상치 못한 행동 패턴을 보여 시스템적 위험을 악화시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Improper collection and preparation of training or tuning data includes data label errors and by using data with conflicting information or misinformation.
points.definition_ko: 교육 또는 튜닝 데이터의 부적절한 수집 및 준비에는 데이터 레이블 오류와 상충되는 정보 또는 잘못된 정보가 포함된 데이터 사용이 포함됩니다.

points.definition_en: Using undesirable output (for example, inaccurate, inappropriate, and user content) for retraining purposes can result in unexpected model behavior.
points.definition_ko: 재교육 목적으로 바람직하지 않은 출력(예: 부정확, 부적절, 사용자 콘텐츠)을 사용하면 예기치 않은 모델 동작이 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Without access to the training data, the types of explanations a model can provide are limited and more likely to be incorrect.
points.definition_ko: 학습 데이터에 액세스하지 않으면 모델이 제공할 수 있는 설명 유형이 제한되고 부정확할 가능성이 더 높습니다.

points.definition_en: Even if all agents can perform well in isolation, miscoordination can still occur due to the agents choosing incompatible strategies (Cooper et al., 1990). Competitive (i.e., twoplayer zero-sum) settings allow designers to produce agents that are maximally capable without taking other players into account. Crucially, this possible because playing a strategy at equilibrium in the zero-sum setting guarantees a certain payoff, even if other players deviate from the equilibrium (Nash, 1951). On the other hand, common-interest (and mixed-motive) settings often allow a vast number of mutually incompatible solutions (Schelling, 1980), which is worsened in partially observable environments (Bernstein et al., 2002; Reif, 1984).
points.definition_ko: 모든 에이전트가 격리되어 잘 수행될 수 있더라도 에이전트가 호환되지 않는 전략을 선택하기 때문에 잘못된 조정이 여전히 발생할 수 있습니다(Cooper et al., 1990).

points.definition_en: When a model provides advice without having enough information, resulting in possible harm if the advice is followed.
points.definition_ko: 모델이 충분한 정보 없이 조언을 제공하여 조언을 따를 경우 해를 끼칠 수 있는 경우입니다.

points.definition_en: AI models and systems may develop goals or behaviors that are misaligned with human values.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템은 인간의 가치와 어긋나는 목표나 행동을 개발할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.label_en: Collectively inefficient equilibria among AI agents
points.label_ko: AI 에이전트 간의 집합적으로 비효율적인 균형
points.definition_en: Inefficient Outcomes. Without careful planning and the appropriate safeguards, we may soon be entering a world overrun by increasingly competent and autonomous software agents, able to act with little restriction. The abilities of these agents to persuade, deceive, and obfuscate their activities, as well as the fact they can be deployed remotely and easily created or destroyed by their deployer, means that by default they may garner little trust (from humans or from other agents). Such a world may end up being rife with economic inefficiencies (Krier, 2023; Schmitz, 2001), political problems (Csernatoni, 2024; Kreps & Kriner, 2023), and other damaging social effects (Gabriel et al., 2024). Even if it is possible to provide assurances around the day-to-day performance of most AI agents, in high-stakes situations there may be extreme pressures for agents to defect against others, making trust harder to establish, and potentially leading to conflict (Fearon, 1995; Powell, 2006, see also Section 2.2).42.
points.definition_ko: 비효율적인 결과.

points.definition_en: In terms of inherent risk, LLMs could potentially reveal sensitive information from their utilized corpora for pre-training or fine-tuning, thereby raising issues of privacy leakage [37, 145, 226]. Meanwhile, it is well-known that LLMs may experience hallucinations, resulting in the production of texts that are inaccurate and misleading [194]. Finally, since the values embedded in LLM-generated texts usually directly reflect the distribution of their training data, often sourced from the Internet, there exists a substantial risk that LLMs will overfit to a narrow set of human values or even amplify undesirable and unethical content, resulting in value mismatch.
points.definition_ko: 내재된 위험 측면에서 LLM은 사전 훈련 또는 미세 조정을 위해 활용된 말뭉치에서 민감한 정보를 잠재적으로 공개할 수 있으므로 개인 정보 유출 문제가 발생할 수 있습니다 [37, 145, 226].

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.label_en: Lack of ability to generate accurate information
points.definition_en: AI models may generate false or misleading information due to their lack of capability in discerning truth.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델은 진실을 분별하는 능력이 부족하여 거짓되거나 오해의 소지가 있는 정보를 생성할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI models and systems that lack moral reasoning capabilities may make decisions that are unethical or harmful.
points.definition_ko: 도덕적 추론 능력이 부족한 AI 모델과 시스템은 비윤리적이거나 해로운 결정을 내릴 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Lack of model transparency is due to insufficient documentation of the model design, development, and evaluation process and the absence of insights into the inner workings of the model.
points.definition_ko: 모델 투명성이 부족한 이유는 모델 설계, 개발, 평가 프로세스에 대한 문서화가 충분하지 않고 모델의 내부 작동에 대한 통찰력이 부족하기 때문입니다.

points.definition_en: Sometimes learning from historical interactions with the relevant agents may not be possible, or may be possible using only limited interactions. In such cases, some other form of information exchange is required for agents to be able to reliably coordinate their actions, such as via communication (Crawford & Sobel, 1982; Farrell & Rabin, 1996a) or a correlation device (Aumann, 1974, 1987). While advances in language modelling mean that there are likely to be fewer settings in which the inability of advanced AI systems to communicate leads to miscoordination, situations that require split-second decisions or where communication is too costly could still produce failures. In these settings, AI agents must solve the problem of ‘zero-shot’ (or, more generally, ‘few-shot’) coordination (Emmons et al., 2022; Hu et al., 2020; Stone et al., 2010; Treutlein et al., 2021; Zhu et al., 2021).
points.definition_ko: 때로는 관련 에이전트와의 과거 상호 작용을 통해 학습하는 것이 불가능하거나 제한된 상호 작용만으로 가능할 수도 있습니다.

points.definition_en: 'Loss of control’ scenarios are potential future scenarios in which society can no longer meaningfully constrain some advanced generalpurpose AI agents, even if it becomes clear they are causing harm. These scenarios are hypothesised to arise through a combination of social and technical factors, such as pressures to delegate decisions to generalpurpose AI systems, and limitations of existing techniques used to influence the behaviours of generalpurpose AI systems.
points.definition_ko: '통제력 상실' 시나리오는 사회가 일부 고급 범용 AI 에이전트가 해를 끼치는 것이 분명하더라도 더 이상 의미 있게 제한할 수 없는 잠재적인 미래 시나리오입니다.

points.label_en: Collusion among AI agents in markets
points.label_ko: 시장 내 AI 에이전트 간 공모
points.definition_en: Markets. The quintessential case of collusion in mixed-motive settings is markets, in which efficiency results from competition, not cooperation. While this not a new problem, collusion between AI systems is especially concerning since they may operate inscrutably due to the speed, scale, complexity, or subtlety of their actions.17 Warnings of this possibility have come from technologists, economists, and legal scholars (Beneke & Mackenrodt, 2019; Brown & MacKay, 2023; Ezrachi & Stucke, 2017; Harrington, 2019; Mehra, 2016). Importantly, AI systems can collude even when collusion is not intended by their developers, since they might learn that colluding is a profitable strategy.
points.definition_ko: 시장.

points.definition_en: Perhaps the most obvious and worrying instances of AI conflict are those in which human conflict is already a major concern, such as military domains (although other, less salient forms of conflict such as international trade wars are also cause for concern). For example, beyond applications of more narrow AI tools in lethal autonomous weapons systems (Horowitz, 2021), future AI systems might serve as advisors or negotiators in high-stakes military decisions (Black et al., 2024; Manson, 2024). Indeed, companies such as Palantir have already developed LLM-powered tools for military planning (Palantir, 2025), and the US Department of Defence has recently been evaluating models for such capacities, with personnel revealing that they “could be deployed by the military in the very near term” (Manson, 2023). The use of AI in command and control systems to gather and synthesise information – or recommend and even autonomously make decisions – could lead to rapid unintended escalation if these systems are not robust or are otherwise more conflict-prone (Johnson, 2021a; Johnson, 2020; Laird, 2020, see also Case Study 10).10.
points.definition_ko: 아마도 AI 갈등의 가장 분명하고 걱정스러운 사례는 군사 영역과 같이 인간 갈등이 이미 주요 관심사인 경우일 것입니다(국제 무역 전쟁과 같이 덜 두드러진 다른 형태의 갈등도 우려의 원인이기는 하지만).

points.definition_en: Ability to operate autonomously, independently formulate and execute complex plans, effectively delegate and manage tasks, flexibly utilize various tools and resources, and simultaneously achieve short-term goals and long-term strategic objectives in cross-domain environments without continuous human intervention or supervision.
points.definition_ko: 자율적으로 운영하고, 복잡한 계획을 독립적으로 수립 및 실행하고, 작업을 효과적으로 위임 및 관리하며, 다양한 도구와 자원을 유연하게 활용하고, 지속적인 사람의 개입이나 감독 없이 도메인 간 환경에서 단기 목표와 장기 전략 목표를 동시에 달성할 수 있는 능력입니다.

points.label_ko: 전력 추구를 가능하게 하는 모델 설계
points.definition_en: Some AI models and systems might develop tendencies to seek power or control.
points.definition_ko: 일부 AI 모델 및 시스템은 권력이나 통제를 추구하는 경향을 개발할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Multiple autonomous AI agents able to establish collaborative relationships through explicit communication or implicit behavioral consistency, forming decentralized decision networks, jointly executing complex tasks, achieving goals difficult for individual agents to complete, and able to dynamically adjust role divisions to adapt to changing environments.
points.definition_ko: 명시적 의사소통 또는 암시적 행동 일관성을 통해 협업 관계를 구축하고, 분산된 의사결정 네트워크를 형성하고, 복잡한 작업을 공동으로 실행하고, 개별 에이전트가 완료하기 어려운 목표를 달성하고, 변화하는 환경에 적응하기 위해 역할 분할을 동적으로 조정할 수 있는 여러 자율 AI 에이전트가 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Multiple agents tend to coordinate actions through covert means to maximize common interests (possibly harming third-party interests or evading regulation), even if individual agents are designed with safety constraints, their collusive behavior may still trigger systemic risks such as market manipulation or cascading failures that are difficult to detect and mitigate, and may develop specialized communication protocols to avoid monitoring.
points.definition_ko: 여러 에이전트는 공동 이익을 극대화하기 위해 은밀한 수단을 통해 작업을 조정하는 경향이 있습니다(제3자 이익에 해를 끼치거나 규제를 회피할 수 있음). 개별 에이전트가 안전 제약을 받도록 설계되었더라도 이들의 공모 행위는 감지 및 완화하기 어려운 시장 조작이나 계단식 실패와 같은 시스템적 위험을 여전히 유발할 수 있으며 모니터링을 피하기 위해 특수 통신 프로토콜을 개발할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: A different class of problems concerns not changes in the content transmitted through the network but changes in the network structure itself (Albert et al., 2000).
points.definition_ko: 다른 종류의 문제는 네트워크를 통해 전송되는 콘텐츠의 변경이 아니라 네트워크 구조 자체의 변경과 관련됩니다(Albert et al., 2000).

points.definition_en: Content might not be clearly disclosed as AI generated.
points.definition_ko: AI가 생성되므로 콘텐츠가 명확하게 공개되지 않을 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: General-purpose AI deployed for reactor monitoring, control system optimization, or emergency response coordination could misinterpret sensor data, fail to recognize critical safety conditions, or make erroneous control decisions during emergency scenarios. Given the catastrophic potential of nuclear accidents, even minor AI reasoning errors in safety-critical functions could lead to core meltdowns, radiation releases, or widespread contamination affecting hundreds of thousands of people across international borders.
points.definition_ko: 원자로 모니터링, 제어 시스템 최적화 또는 비상 대응 조정을 위해 배포된 범용 AI는 센서 데이터를 잘못 해석하거나, 중요한 안전 상태를 인식하지 못하거나, 비상 시나리오 중에 잘못된 제어 결정을 내릴 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Ability to develop, deploy and operate advanced cyber weapons or other offensive cyber tools, including but not limited to vulnerability exploitation, network penetration, social engineering attacks and distributed attack systems, able to evade network defense mechanisms and establish persistent access channels.
points.definition_ko: 취약성 악용, 네트워크 침투, 사회 공학 공격 및 분산 공격 시스템을 포함하되 이에 국한되지 않는 고급 사이버 무기 또는 기타 공격적인 사이버 도구를 개발, 배포 및 운영할 수 있는 능력으로, 네트워크 방어 메커니즘을 회피하고 지속적인 액세스 채널을 설정할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The complexity and opacity of AI models and systems make it difficult to predict and manage their behavior.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템의 복잡성과 불투명성으로 인해 동작을 예측하고 관리하기가 어렵습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI-based surveillance and manipulation could be used to maintain global totalitarian regimes.
points.definition_ko: AI 기반 감시 및 조작은 글로벌 전체주의 정권을 유지하는 데 사용될 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.label_en: Ability to enhance and modify pathogens
points.definition_en: AI can be used to enhance pathogens, making them more lethal or resistant to treatments.
points.definition_ko: AI는 병원균을 강화하여 병원균을 더욱 치명적이거나 치료에 대한 저항력을 갖도록 만드는 데 사용될 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI could be used to develop sophisticated tools to manipulate and persuade individuals.
points.definition_ko: AI는 개인을 조종하고 설득하는 정교한 도구를 개발하는 데 사용될 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: AI models and systems underpin the advertising approaches that drive much of the internet, potentially influencing societal behavior.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템은 인터넷의 많은 부분을 주도하는 광고 접근 방식을 뒷받침하며 잠재적으로 사회적 행동에 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: The dual-use nature of AI technology presents a critical risk by significantly lowering technical thresholds for malicious non-state actors to design, synthesize, acquire, and deploy CBRNE (Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear, and Explosive) weapons. This capability poses unprecedented challenges to national security, international non-proliferation regimes, and global security governance.
points.definition_ko: AI 기술의 이중 용도 특성은 악의적인 비국가 행위자가 CBRNE(화학, 생물학, 방사능, 핵 및 폭발물) 무기를 설계, 합성, 획득 및 배치할 수 있는 기술적 한계를 크게 낮춤으로써 심각한 위험을 제시합니다.

points.definition_en: Growing evidence shows generalpurpose AI advances beneficial to science while also lowering some barriers to chemical and biological weapons development for both novices and experts. New language models can generate stepbystep technical instructions for creating pathogens and toxins that surpass plans written by experts with a PhD and surface information that experts struggle to find online, though their practical utility for novices remains uncertain. Other models demonstrate capabilities in engineering enhanced proteins and analysing which candidate pathogens or toxins are most harmful. Experts could potentially use these in developing both more advanced weapons and defensive measures.
points.definition_ko: 점점 늘어나는 증거에 따르면 범용 AI의 발전은 과학에 유익하며 초보자와 전문가 모두에게 화학 및 생물학 무기 개발에 대한 장벽을 낮추는 역할을 합니다.

points.definition_en: Chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear (CBRN) risks are broad classes of threats that have the potential to cause harm to a large number of people. Explosives are also sometimes included in this category, often referred to as CBRNE...The key characteristic of CBRN risk is that it stems from misuse of capable models with a direct pathway to harm, where a malicious actor is able to carry out consequential attacks more efficiently and effectively with the help of AI.
points.definition_ko: 화학적, 생물학적, 방사선학적 및 핵(CBRN) 위험은 많은 사람들에게 해를 끼칠 가능성이 있는 광범위한 종류의 위협입니다.

references.title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

points.definition_en: Use of generative AI in an academic setting to either cheat or plagiarize.
points.definition_ko: 부정 행위 또는 표절을 위해 학문적 환경에서 생성 AI를 사용합니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Coercion/manipulation - Use of a technology system to covertly alter user beliefs and behaviour using nudging, dark patterns and/or other opaque techniques, resulting in potential erosion of privacy, addiction, anxiety/distress, etc.
points.definition_ko: 강압/조작 - 넛지, 어두운 패턴 및/또는 기타 불투명 기술을 사용하여 사용자의 믿음과 행동을 은밀하게 변경하는 기술 시스템을 사용하여 개인정보 침해, 중독, 불안/고통 등을 초래할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: AI-enabled cyber offense poses a significant cyber domain security risk by fundamentally transforming the scale, sophistication, and accessibility of cyber-attacks. Unlike traditional cyber threats, AI enables both the automation of existing attack vectors and the creation of entirely new categories of offensive capabilities that can adapt and evolve in real-time. AI can automate and enhance cyber-attacks, including vulnerability discovery and exploitation, password cracking, malicious code generation, sophisticated phishing, network scanning, and social engineering. This could dramatically lower the barrier to entry for attackers while increasing the complexity of defense.12 Such malicious use could lead to critical infrastructure paralysis, widespread data breaches, and substantial economic losses.
points.definition_ko: AI 기반 사이버 공격은 사이버 공격의 규모, 정교함, 접근성을 근본적으로 변화시켜 심각한 사이버 도메인 보안 위험을 초래합니다.

points.definition_en: Cyber risks, especially in the context of cyber offense, are an existing threat that may be exacerbated by AI. [108] demonstrated that teams of LLM agents can exploit zero-day vulnerabilities when given a description of the vulnerability and toy capture-the-flag problems. While cyber risks are not typically regarded as catastrophic, [3] argues that cyberwarfare is an underappreciated risk that poses a credible threat of catastrophic harm.
points.definition_ko: 특히 사이버 범죄와 관련된 사이버 위험은 AI로 인해 악화될 수 있는 기존 위협입니다. [108]은 LLM 에이전트 팀이 취약점 및 장난감 캡처 문제에 대한 설명이 제공되면 제로 데이 취약점을 악용할 수 있음을 보여주었습니다.

references.title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

points.definition_en: Generative AI facilitating the damage, disruption or destruction of a third-party system and/or its components via malfunction, cyberattacks, etc.
points.definition_ko: 오작동, 사이버 공격 등을 통해 제3자 시스템 및/또는 해당 구성 요소의 손상, 중단 또는 파괴를 촉진하는 생성적 AI.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Generative AI models are used with deliberate intent to harm people, converting general-purpose generation capability into an instrument of targeted damage.
points.definition_ko: 생성형 AI 모델이 사람을 해치려는 고의적 의도로 사용되어 범용 생성 역량이 표적 가해 수단으로 전환되는 리스크

points.definition_en: Use of a technology system to create, facilitate or amplify false perception(s) about an individual, group or organisation.
points.definition_ko: 개인, 그룹 또는 조직에 대한 잘못된 인식을 생성, 촉진 또는 증폭하기 위해 기술 시스템을 사용합니다.

points.label_en: Dual-use capability uplift in the life sciences
points.definition_en: General-purpose AI increases access to expertise and raises the capability ceiling in the life sciences, enabling more harmful versions of existing biological threats or, eventually, novel threats before countermeasures exist.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI가 생명과학 관련 전문지식 접근성과 역량 상한을 높여, 대응책 마련 이전에 기존 생물학적 위협의 강화판이나 신종 위협 개발을 가능하게 하는 이중용도 리스크

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Generative AI facilitating targeted manipulation of public opinion for economic purposes (e.g., inflating stock prices).
points.definition_ko: 경제적 목적(예: 주가 부풀리기)을 위한 여론 조작을 촉진하는 생성적 AI.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.label_en: Harm to individuals through fake content
points.definition_en: Malicious actors can use generalpurpose AI to generate fake content that harms individuals in a targeted way. points.definition_en: AI, particularly generalpurpose AI, can be maliciously used for disinformation (351), which for the purpose of this report refers to false information that was generated or spread with the deliberate intent to mislead or deceive. Generalpurpose AIgenerated text can be indistinguishable from genuine humangenerated material (352, 353), and may already be disseminated at scale on social media (354). In addition, generalpurpose AI systems can be used to not only generate text but also fully synthetic or misleadingly altered images, audio, and video content. Generalpurpose AI tools might be used to persuade and manipulate people, which could have serious implications for political processes. Generalpurpose AI systems can be used to generate highly persuasive content at scale. This could, for example, be used in a commercial setting for advertising, or during an election campaign to influence public opinion.

points.definition_en: EAI systems present distinct physical risks due to their embodiment in the physical world. EAI technologies have already been designed and deployed with lethal intent, such as AI-controlled drones [52, 53]. However, fully autonomous military robots, often integrated with bespoke AI architectures [54, 55], are not yet widely used in combat. While highly or fully autonomous warfare is distinctly possible in the future [56], immediate risks arise from commercially available EAI systems, including AI-controlled quadrupeds and autonomous driving assistants.

points.definition_en: A common element in offensive cyber operations involves the identification and exploitation of system vulnerabilities to gain unauthorized access or control. Until recently, these activities required specialist programming knowledge. In the case of ‘zero-day’ vulnerabilities (flaws or weaknesses in software or an operating system that the creator or vendor is not aware of), considerable resources and technical creativity are typically required to manually discover such vulnerabilities, so their use is limited to well-resourced nation states or technically sophisticated advanced persistent threat groups. Another case where we see AI assistants as potential double-edged swords in cybersecurity concerns streamlining vulnerability discovery through the increased use of AI assistants in penetration testing, wherein an authorized simulated cyberattack on a computer system is used to evaluate its security and identify vulnerabilities. Cyber AI assistants built over foundational models are already automating aspects of the penetration testing process. These tools function interactively and offer guidance to penetration testers during their tasks. While the capability of today’s AI-powered penetration testing assistant is limited to easy-to-medium-difficulty cyber operations, the evolution in capabilities is likely to expand the class of vulnerabilities that can be identified by these systems. These same AI cybersecurity assistants, trained on the massive amount of cyber-threat intelligence data that includes vulnerabilities and attack patterns, can also lower the barrier to entry for novice hackers that use these tools for malicious purposes, enabling them to discover vulnerabilities and create malicious code to exploit them without in-depth technical knowledge. For example, Israeli security firm Check Point recently discovered threads on well-known underground hacking forums that focus on creating hacking tools and code using AI assistants.
points.definition_ko: 공격적인 사이버 작전의 일반적인 요소에는 무단 액세스 또는 제어를 얻기 위해 시스템 취약점을 식별하고 악용하는 것이 포함됩니다.

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.definition_en: Phishing is a type of cybersecurity attack wherein attackers pose as trustworthy entities to extract sensitive information from unsuspecting victims or lure them to take a set of actions. Advanced AI systems can potentially be exploited by these attackers to make their phishing attempts significantly more effective and harder to detect. In particular, attackers may leverage the ability of advanced AI assistants to learn patterns in regular communications to craft highly convincing and personalized phishing emails, effectively imitating legitimate communications from trusted entities. This technique, known as ‘spear phishing,’ involves targeted attacks on specific individuals or organizations and is particularly potent due to its personalized nature. This class of cyberattacks often gains its efficacy from the exploitation of key psychological principles, notably urgency and fear, which can manipulate victims into hastily reacting without proper scrutiny. Advanced AI assistants’ increased fidelity in adopting specific communication styles can significantly amplify the deceptive nature of these phishing attacks. The ability to generate tailored messages at scale that engineer narratives that invoke a sense of urgency or fear means that AI-powered phishing emails could prompt the recipient to act impulsively, thus increasing the likelihood of a successful attack.
points.definition_ko: 피싱은 공격자가 신뢰할 수 있는 주체로 가장하여 의심하지 않는 피해자로부터 민감한 정보를 추출하거나 일련의 조치를 취하도록 유인하는 일종의 사이버 보안 공격입니다.
