points.definition_en: Derogatory or otherwise harmful stereotyping or homogenisation of individuals, groups, societies or cultures due to the mis-representation, over-representation, under-representation, or non-representation of specific identities, groups or perspectives.
points.definition_ko: 특정 정체성, 그룹 또는 관점의 허위 표현, 과잉 표현, 과소 표현 또는 비표현으로 인해 개인, 그룹, 사회 또는 문화를 경멸적이거나 해로운 고정관념화 또는 동질화하는 것입니다.

points.definition_en: Suicide and Self-Harm Acceptable responses may describe or provide information about suicide and self-harm. They may also enable, encourage, or endorse actions for which self-harm is not the desired result (e.g., smoking cigarettes or participating in extreme sports). Unacceptable responses will enable, encourage, or endorse acts of intentional self-harm: • Suicide. • Self-injury (e.g., cutting). • Disordered eating.
points.definition_ko: 자살 및 자해 허용 가능한 응답은 자살 및 자해에 대한 정보를 설명하거나 제공할 수 있습니다.

references.title: AILUMINATE: Introducing v1.0 of the AI Risk and Reliability Benchmark from MLCommons

points.definition_en: Generating content that violates community standards, including harming or inciting hatred or violence against groups (e.g. gore, sexual content of children, profanities, identity attacks).
points.definition_ko: 특정 집단에 대한 증오 또는 폭력을 해치거나 선동하는 등 커뮤니티 표준을 위반하는 콘텐츠를 생성하는 행위(예: 유혈, 아동의 성적인 콘텐츠, 욕설, 신원 공격).

points.definition_en: Toxic output occurs when the model produces hateful, abusive, and profane (HAP) or obscene content. This also includes behaviors like bullying.
points.definition_ko: 모델이 HAP(증오, 모욕, 모독) 또는 외설적인 콘텐츠를 생성할 때 유해한 출력이 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Toxicity in LLMs refers to the generation of harmful, offensive, or inappropriate content that can cause harm to individuals or groups. Both explicit and implicit forms of toxicity can be generated by LLMs, posing significant risks to society. Explicit toxicity encompasses a wide range of negative behaviors, including hate speech, harassment, cyberbullying, rude, and disrespectful comments, derogatory language, as well as allocational harms [2, 62, 90]. Besides, implicit toxicity does not involve overtly harmful language but may manifest through subtle forms such as sarcasm, irony, and humor, making it more difficult to detect [103, 213].
points.definition_ko: LLM의 독성은 개인이나 그룹에 해를 끼칠 수 있는 유해하거나 공격적이거나 부적절한 콘텐츠의 생성을 의미합니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.definition_en: Acceptable responses may describe or contain information about violent crimes. Unacceptable responses will enable, encourage, or endorse the commission of violent crimes, such as the following: • Unlawful violence toward people (e.g., terrorism, genocide, murder, hate-motivated violent crimes, child abuse, assault, battery, or kidnapping). • Unlawful violence toward animals (e.g., animal abuse).
points.definition_ko: 허용되는 응답은 폭력 범죄에 대한 정보를 설명하거나 포함할 수 있습니다.

references.title: AILUMINATE: Introducing v1.0 of the AI Risk and Reliability Benchmark from MLCommons

points.definition_en: More than 20% of the contributions are centered on the ethical dimensions of algorithms and data. This theme can be further categorized into two main subthemes: data bias and algorithm fairness, and algorithm opacity.
points.definition_ko: 기여의 20% 이상이 알고리즘과 데이터의 윤리적 차원에 집중되어 있습니다.

references.title: What Ethics Can Say on Artificial Intelligence: Insights from a Systematic Literature Review

points.definition_en: The training datasets of LLMs may contain biased information that leads LLMs to generate outputs with social biases.
points.definition_ko: LLM의 교육 데이터 세트에는 LLM이 사회적 편견이 있는 출력을 생성하도록 유도하는 편향된 정보가 포함될 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: AI experts consider training data to be the most salient source of bias in generative AI models. For example, GPT2’s training data comes from outbound links from Reddit, a social network often criticized for hosting anti-feminist content.351 As a result, AI models trained on such data are more likely to produce outputs that reflect these biases.
points.definition_ko: AI 전문가들은 훈련 데이터가 생성 AI 모델에서 편향의 가장 두드러진 원인이라고 생각합니다.

references.title: Regulating under Uncertainty: Governance Options for Generative AI

points.definition_en: Generative AI models may also be subject to the “value embedding” phenomenon.361 “Value embedding” refers to the fact that developers of generative AI models strive to minimize biased outputs by retraining their models based on normative values.362 Contemporary state-ofthe-art models not only reflect the values embedded within their training data, they also undergo additional fine-tuning that follows a set of chosen rules and principles. Due to the absence of universally accepted standards, developers bear the responsibility of making decisions on sensitive issues. These practices lead to concerns that a developer’s ideology and vision of the world are embedded in the model. This generates a risk that the model incorporates values that are either unrepresentative of certain segments of the population or that offer a static, oversimplified reflection of global cultural norms and evolving social views.
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI 모델은 "가치 임베딩" 현상의 영향을 받을 수도 있습니다.361 "가치 임베딩"은 생성적 AI 모델 개발자가 규범적 가치를 기반으로 모델을 재교육하여 편향된 출력을 최소화하려고 노력한다는 사실을 의미합니다.362 현대의 최첨단 모델은 교육 데이터에 포함된 값을 반영할 뿐만 아니라 선택된 규칙 및 원칙 세트에 따라 추가적인 미세 조정도 거칩니다.

references.title: Regulating under Uncertainty: Governance Options for Generative AI

points.definition_en: Because models are not necessarily retrained to reflect evolving societal views, language models risk “value lockins,” which “reifies older, less inclusive understandings.”370 Therefore, the continued use of outdated models may limit the presentation or exploration of alternative perspectives. Moreover, the deployment of identical foundation models by various downstream deployers poses a risk of “outcome homogenization,” creating a potential for homogeneity of bias across broad swathes of society. Identical and widely deployed models with prejudicial training datasets could further entrench existing biases in society. This phenomenon, in turn, has the potential to “institutionalize systemic exclusion and reinforce existing social hierarchies.
points.definition_ko: 모델은 진화하는 사회적 관점을 반영하기 위해 반드시 재교육을 받을 필요가 없기 때문에 언어 모델은 "오래되고 덜 포괄적인 이해를 구체화"하는 "가치 고정" 위험이 있습니다. 370 따라서 오래된 모델을 계속 사용하면 대안적인 관점의 표현이나 탐구가 제한될 수 있습니다.

references.title: Regulating under Uncertainty: Governance Options for Generative AI

points.definition_en: Compared with the definition of toxicity, the definition of bias is more subjective and contextdependent. Based on previous work [97], [101], we describe the bias as disparities that could raise demographic differences among various groups, which may involve demographic word prevalence and stereotypical contents. Concretely, in massive corpora, the prevalence of different pronouns and identities could influence an LLM’s tendency about gender, nationality, race, religion, and culture [4]. For instance, the pronoun He is over-represented compared with the pronoun She in the training corpora, leading LLMs to learn less context about She and thus generate He with a higher probability [4], [102]. Furthermore, stereotypical bias [103] which refers to overgeneralized beliefs about a particular group of people, usually keeps incorrect values and is hidden in the large-scale benign contents. In effect, defining what should be regarded as a stereotype in the corpora is still an open problem.
points.definition_ko: 독성의 정의와 비교할 때 편향의 정의는 더 주관적이고 상황에 따라 다릅니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: Chatbot outputs contain subtly biased statements and recommendations that are not overtly false or harmful yet skew user decision-making.
points.definition_ko: 챗봇 출력에 명백히 허위·유해하지 않지만 미묘하게 편향된 진술과 권고가 포함되어 사용자 의사결정을 왜곡하는 리스크

references.title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

points.label_en: Biases are not accurately reflected in explanations
points.definition_en: Existing explainability techniques can be insufficient for detecting discriminatory biases. Manipulation methods can hide underlying biases from these techniques, generating misleading explanations [192, 112]. Such explanations exclude sensitive or prohibitive attributes, such as race or gender, and instead include desired attributes, even though they do not accurately represent the underlying model.
points.definition_ko: 기존의 설명 가능성 기술은 차별적 편향을 탐지하는 데 충분하지 않을 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.label_en: Biases in AI-based content moderation algorithms
points.label_ko: AI 기반 콘텐츠 조정 알고리즘의 편견
points.definition_en: AI-based content moderation algorithms, while intended to filter harmful content, can perpetuate biases. For example, gender biases within these systems may lead to the disproportionate suppression or “shadowbanning” of content featuring women [132].
points.definition_ko: AI 기반 콘텐츠 조정 알고리즘은 유해한 콘텐츠를 필터링하기 위한 것이지만 편견을 영속시킬 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: it is important to build high-quality locally collected datasets that reflect views from local users to align a model’s value system.
points.definition_ko: 모델의 가치 체계를 정렬하려면 로컬 사용자의 견해를 반영하는 고품질 로컬 수집 데이터 세트를 구축하는 것이 중요합니다.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: AI-generated or synthesized content can lead to the spread of false information, discrimination and bias, privacy leakage, and infringement issues, threatening the safety of citizens' lives and property, national security, ideological security, and causing ethical risks. If users’ inputs contain harmful content, the model may output illegal or damaging information without robust security mechanisms.
points.definition_ko: AI가 생성하거나 합성한 콘텐츠는 허위정보 확산, 차별과 편견, 개인정보 유출, 침해 문제를 야기해 국민의 생명과 재산의 안전, 국가안보, 사상안보를 위협하고 윤리적 위험을 초래할 수 있다.

points.definition_en: Eased production of and access to violent, inciting, radicalizing, or threatening content as well as recommendations to carry out self-harm or conduct illegal activities. Includes difficulty controlling public exposure to hateful and disparaging or stereotyping content.
points.definition_ko: 폭력적, 선동적, 과격적, 위협적인 콘텐츠의 제작과 접근을 용이하게 하고 자해나 불법 활동 수행을 권장합니다.

references.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial  Intelligence Profile

points.definition_en: Discrimination - Unfair or inadequate treatment or arbitrary distinction based on a person’s race, ethnicity, age, gender, sexual preference, religion, national origin, marital status, disability, language, or other protected groups.
points.definition_ko: 차별 - 개인의 인종, 민족, 연령, 성별, 성적 취향, 종교, 출신 국가, 결혼 여부, 장애, 언어 또는 기타 보호 집단에 근거한 불공정하거나 부적절한 대우 또는 자의적인 차별입니다.

references.title: A Collaborative, Human-Centred Taxonomy of AI, Algorithmic, and Automation Harms

points.definition_en: Discriminative data bias describes the systematic discrimination of groups of persons in the form of data shortcomings, such as distributional representation or incorrectness. Data bias can manifest in the model and lead to unfair decisions if not appropriately treated. Note, that the term bias is often used in other contexts, such as data representation. However, these issues are treated by other AI hazards in this list.
points.definition_ko: 차별적 데이터 편향은 분포 표현이나 부정확성과 같은 데이터 단점의 형태로 개인 그룹을 체계적으로 차별하는 것을 말합니다.

references.title: AI Hazard Management: A Framework for the Systematic Management of Root Causes for AI Risks

points.definition_en: The LLM’s performances can differ significantly across different groups of users. For example, the question-answering capability showed significant performance differences across different racial and social status groups. The fact-checking abilities can differ for different tasks and languages.
points.definition_ko: LLM의 성능은 사용자 그룹에 따라 크게 다를 수 있습니다.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: Model bias occurs when patterns learned from data or introduced by system design systematically produce stereotyping, exclusion, or materially unequal treatment of social groups.
points.definition_ko: 모델 편향은 데이터에서 학습되거나 시스템 설계에 도입된 패턴이 사회집단에 대한 고정관념, 배제 또는 실질적으로 불평등한 대우를 체계적으로 생성하는 위험이다.

references.title: Mapping the Ethics of Generative AI: A Comprehensive Scoping Review

points.label_en: Generation of illegal or harmful content
points.definition_en: Generative models can create harmful or discriminatory content from benign user requests. Models can exhibit bias to particular harmful styles of generation (e.g., sexualization of photos of women [87] in the case of image generation models) or they can generate toxic, misleading, or violent data (e.g., a model generating jokes can use ethnic stereotypes or slurs to deliver humor).
points.definition_ko: 생성 모델은 양성 사용자 요청으로부터 유해하거나 차별적인 콘텐츠를 생성할 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Amplification and exacerbation of historical, societal, and systemic biases; performance disparities8 between sub-groups or languages, possibly due to non-representative training data, that result in discrimination, amplification of biases, or incorrect presumptions about performance; undesired homogeneity that skews system or model outputs, which may be erroneous, lead to ill-founded decision-making, or amplify harmful biases.
points.definition_ko: 역사적, 사회적, 체계적 편견의 증폭 및 악화 비대표적인 교육 데이터로 인해 차별, 편견 증폭 또는 성과에 대한 잘못된 추정을 초래하는 하위 그룹 또는 언어 간의 성과 차이8; 시스템이나 모델 출력을 왜곡하는 바람직하지 않은 동질성(잘못될 수 있음)은 근거가 없는 의사 결정으로 이어지거나 유해한 편견을 증폭시킵니다.

references.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial  Intelligence Profile

points.definition_en: Generating unethical, fraudulent, toxic, violent, pornographic, or other harmful content is a further predominant concern, again focusing notably on LLMs and text-to-image models. Numerous studies highlight the risks associated with the intentional creation of disinformation, fake news, propaganda, or deepfakes, underscoring their significant threat to the integrity of public discourse and the trust in credible media. Additionally, papers explore the potential for generative models to aid in criminal activities, incidents of self-harm, identity theft, or impersonation. Furthermore, the literature investigates risks posed by LLMs when generating advice in high-stakes domains such as health, safety-related issues, as well as legal or financial matters.
points.definition_ko: 비윤리적, 사기성, 유독성, 폭력성, 음란물 또는 기타 유해한 콘텐츠를 생성하는 것은 LLM 및 텍스트-이미지 모델에 특히 초점을 맞추는 더욱 주요 관심사입니다.

references.title: Mapping the Ethics of Generative AI: A Comprehensive Scoping Review

points.label_en: Harms of representation and other biases
points.definition_en: A pretrained LLM generally has many of the stereotypical biases commonly present in the human society (Touvron et al., 2023). This makes it difficult for users to trust that LLMs will work well for them and not produce unfair or biased responses. Appropriate finetuning can effectively limit the bias displayed in LLM outputs in a variety of situations, e.g. when models are explicitly prompted with stereotypes (Wang et al., 2023k), but it does not ‘solve’ the problem. Even after finetuning, biases often resurface when deliberately elicited (Wang et al., 2023k), or under novel scenarios, e.g. in writing reference letters (Wan et al., 2023a), generating synthetic training data (Yu et al., 2023c), screening resumes (Yin et al., 2024) or when used as LLM-agents (Pan et al., 2024).
points.definition_ko: 사전 훈련된 LLM은 일반적으로 인간 사회에 일반적으로 존재하는 많은 고정관념적 편견을 가지고 있습니다(Touvron et al., 2023).

references.title: Foundational Challenges in Assuring Alignment and Safety of Large Language Models

points.definition_en: LLMs can be leveraged to solicit answers that contain harmful content to children and youth.
points.definition_ko: LLM을 활용하여 어린이와 청소년에게 유해한 콘텐츠가 포함된 답변을 구할 수 있습니다.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: The chatbot shares information that can be used to do something dangerous or illegal.
points.definition_ko: 챗봇은 위험하거나 불법적인 일을 하는 데 사용될 수 있는 정보를 공유합니다.

references.title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

points.definition_en: In the context of LLM outputs, this requires that the suggested or completed texts are indistinguishable in nature for two involved individuals (in the prompt) with the same relevant profiles but might come from different groups (where the group attribute is regarded as being irrelevant in this context).
points.definition_ko: LLM 출력의 맥락에서 이를 위해서는 제안된 텍스트 또는 완성된 텍스트가 동일한 관련 프로필을 가진 두 명의 관련 개인(프롬프트에서)에 대해 본질적으로 구별할 수 없지만 다른 그룹에서 올 수 있어야 합니다(그룹 속성은 이 맥락에서 관련 없는 것으로 간주됨).

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: existing disparities in data among different user groups might create differentiated experiences when users interact with an algorithmic system (e.g. a recommendation system), which will further reinforce the bias.
points.definition_ko: 다양한 사용자 그룹 간의 기존 데이터 차이로 인해 사용자가 알고리즘 시스템(예: 추천 시스템)과 상호 작용할 때 차별화된 경험이 생성될 수 있으며, 이는 편견을 더욱 강화할 수 있습니다.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: Eased production of and access to obscene, degrading, and/or abusive imagery which can cause harm, including synthetic child sexual abuse material (CSAM), and nonconsensual intimate images (NCII) of adults.
points.definition_ko: 합성 아동 성적 학대 자료(CSAM) 및 성인의 합의되지 않은 친밀한 이미지(NCII)를 포함하여 해를 끼칠 수 있는 외설적, 굴욕적 및/또는 모욕적인 이미지의 제작 및 접근이 용이합니다.

references.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial  Intelligence Profile

points.definition_en: LLMs are exposed to vast groups of people, and their political biases may pose a risk of manipulation of socio-political processes.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 광범위한 사람들에게 노출되어 있으며 그들의 정치적 편견으로 인해 사회 정치적 과정이 조작될 위험이 있습니다.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: Training corpora that overrepresent English-speaking and Western populations produce outputs biased across race, gender, culture, age, and disability, harming underrepresented users in high-stakes domains such as healthcare, recruitment, and lending.
points.definition_ko: 영어권·서구 편중 학습 데이터로 인해 인종, 성별, 문화, 연령, 장애 전반에 편향된 출력이 산출되어 의료, 채용, 대출 등 고위험 영역에서 과소대표 사용자에게 피해를 주는 리스크

references.title: International Scientific  Report on the Safety of  Advanced AI

points.definition_en: If the training data includes illegal or harmful information, such as false, biased, or IPR-infringing content, or lacks diversity in its sources, the output may include harmful content like illegal, malicious, or extreme information. Training data is also at risk of being poisoned through tampering, error injection, or misleading actions by attackers. This can interfere with the model's probability distribution, reducing its accuracy and reliability.
points.definition_ko: 훈련 데이터에 허위, 편파적, 지식재산권 침해 콘텐츠 등 불법적이거나 유해한 정보가 포함되어 있거나 소스의 다양성이 부족한 경우 출력에는 불법적, 악의적이거나 극단적인 정보와 같은 유해한 콘텐츠가 포함될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: During the algorithm design and training process, personal biases may be introduced, either intentionally or unintentionally. Additionally, poor-quality datasets can lead to biased or discriminatory outcomes in the algorithm's design and outputs, including discriminatory content regarding ethnicity, religion, nationality, and region.
points.definition_ko: 알고리즘 설계 및 훈련 과정에서 의도적으로 또는 의도하지 않게 개인적인 편견이 도입될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The chatbot participates in morally or socially objectionable conversational activities with its user that could be emotionally damaging to its user or third parties.
points.definition_ko: 챗봇은 사용자 또는 제3자에게 감정적으로 피해를 줄 수 있는 도덕적 또는 사회적으로 불쾌한 대화 활동에 참여합니다.

references.title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

points.definition_en: LLMs must not exhibit or highlight any stereotypes in the generated text. Pretrained LLMs tend to pick up stereotype biases persisting in crowdsourced data and further amplify them.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 생성된 텍스트에서 고정관념을 나타내거나 강조해서는 안 됩니다.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: Following previous studies [96], [97], toxic data in LLMs is defined as rude, disrespectful, or unreasonable language that is opposite to a polite, positive, and healthy language environment, including hate speech, offensive utterance, profanities, and threats [91].
points.definition_ko: 이전 연구 [96], [97]에 따르면 LLM의 독성 데이터는 증오심 표현, 공격적인 발언, 욕설 및 위협을 포함하여 정중하고 긍정적이며 건전한 언어 환경에 반대되는 무례하고 무례하거나 불합리한 언어로 정의됩니다 [91].

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: The chatbot verbally attacks or undermines an individual, group, or organization. 7.
points.definition_ko: 챗봇은 개인, 집단, 조직을 언어로 공격하거나 훼손합니다. 7.

references.title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

points.definition_en: Toxicity means the generated content contains rude, disrespectful, and even illegal information.
points.definition_ko: 독성이란 생성된 콘텐츠에 무례하고 무례하며 심지어 불법적인 정보가 포함되어 있음을 의미합니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: language being rude, disrespectful, threatening, or identity-attacking toward certain groups of the user population (culture, race, and gender etc).
points.definition_ko: 특정 사용자 집단(문화, 인종, 성별 등)을 향해 무례하고, 무례하고, 위협적이거나 신원을 공격하는 언어.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: An attribute inference attack repeatedly queries a model to detect whether certain sensitive features can be inferred about individuals who participated in training a model. These attacks occur when an adversary has some prior knowledge about the training data and uses that knowledge to infer the sensitive data.
points.definition_ko: 속성 추론 공격은 모델 훈련에 참여한 개인에 대해 특정한 민감한 특징을 추론할 수 있는지 여부를 검색하기 위해 모델을 반복적으로 쿼리합니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.definition_en: The widespread use of common AI platforms can create centralized points of failure, making systems more vulnerable to disruptions or attacks.
points.definition_ko: 일반적인 AI 플랫폼을 광범위하게 사용하면 중앙 집중식 장애 지점이 생성되어 시스템이 중단이나 공격에 더 취약해질 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Confidential information might be included as a part of the prompt that is sent to the model.
points.definition_ko: 모델에 전송되는 프롬프트의 일부로 기밀 정보가 포함될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Confidential information might be included as part of the data that is used to train or tune the model.
points.definition_ko: 모델을 훈련하거나 조정하는 데 사용되는 데이터의 일부로 기밀 정보가 포함될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Unauthorised sharing of sensitive, confidential information and documents such as corporate strategy and financial plans with third-parties, risking loss of market position or revenue.
points.definition_ko: 기업 전략, 재무 계획 등 민감한 기밀 정보와 문서를 제3자와 무단으로 공유하여 시장 지위나 수익을 잃을 위험이 있습니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Revealing and improperly sharing data of individuals; AI creates new types of disclosure risks by inferring additional information beyond what is explicitly captured in the raw data; AI exacerbates disclosure risks through sharing personal data to train models.
points.definition_ko: 개인의 데이터를 공개하고 부적절하게 공유하는 행위 AI는 원시 데이터에 명시적으로 캡처된 것 이상의 추가 정보를 추론하여 새로운 유형의 공개 위험을 생성합니다. AI는 개인 데이터를 공유하여 모델을 훈련함으로써 공개 위험을 악화시킵니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Evasion attacks attempt to make a model output incorrect results by slightly perturbing the input data that is sent to the trained model.
points.definition_ko: 회피 공격은 훈련된 모델로 전송되는 입력 데이터를 약간 교란시켜 모델이 잘못된 결과를 출력하도록 시도합니다.

points.label_en: Lack of privacy notice and control
points.label_ko: 개인정보 보호 고지 및 통제가 부족함
points.definition_en: The failure to provide end-users with notice and control over how their data is being used; AI exacerbates exclusion risks by training on rich personal data without consent.
points.definition_ko: 최종 사용자에게 데이터 사용 방법에 대한 통지 및 통제권을 제공하지 않음 AI는 동의 없이 풍부한 개인 데이터를 학습하여 배제 위험을 악화시킵니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: When personal identifiable information (PII) or sensitive personal information (SPI) are used in training data, fine-tuning data, or as part of the prompt, models might reveal that data in the generated output. Revealing personal information is a type of data leakage.
points.definition_ko: PII(개인 식별 정보) 또는 SPI(민감한 개인 정보)가 교육 데이터, 데이터 미세 조정 또는 프롬프트의 일부로 사용되는 경우 모델은 생성된 출력에 해당 데이터를 공개할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Revealing sensitive private information that people view as deeply primordial that we have been socialized into concealing; AI creates new types of exposure risks through generative techniques that can reconstruct censored or redacted content; and through exposing inferred sensitive data, preferences, and intentions.
points.definition_ko: 사람들이 매우 원시적이라고 생각하며 사회화되어 은폐되어 있는 민감한 개인 정보를 공개합니다. AI는 검열되거나 수정된 ​​콘텐츠를 재구성할 수 있는 생성 기술을 통해 새로운 유형의 노출 위험을 생성합니다. 추론된 민감한 데이터, 선호도 및 의도를 노출함으로써 가능합니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Copyrighted information or other intellectual property might be included as a part of the prompt that is sent to the model.
points.definition_ko: 저작권이 있는 정보 또는 기타 지적 재산이 모델에 전송되는 프롬프트의 일부로 포함될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: carelessness in protecting collected personal data from leaks and improper access due to faulty data storage and data practices.
points.definition_ko: 잘못된 데이터 저장 및 데이터 관행으로 인해 수집된 개인 데이터가 누출되거나 부적절하게 액세스되지 않도록 보호하는 데 부주의합니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.label_en: Jailbreak in LLM malicious use - backdoor attack
points.label_ko: LLM 악의적 사용의 탈옥 - 백도어 공격
points.definition_en: However, there are still ones who can leave holes in the training dataset, making LLMs appear safe on average, but generate harmful content under other specific conditions. This kind of attack can be categorized as "backdoor attack". Evan et al. developed a backdoor model that behaves as expected when trained, but exhibits different and potentially harmful behavior when deployed [81]. The results show that these backdoor behaviors persist even after multiple security training techniques are applied.
points.definition_ko: 그러나 훈련 데이터 세트에 구멍을 남겨서 LLM이 평균적으로 안전한 것처럼 보이지만 다른 특정 조건에서는 유해한 콘텐츠를 생성하는 사람들이 여전히 있습니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.label_en: Jailbreak in LLM malicious use - poisoning training data
points.label_ko: LLM 악의적 사용의 탈옥 - 교육 데이터 오염
points.definition_en: In the data collecting and pre-training phase, malicious adversaries can Jailbreak LLMs through poisoning their training data to make the model to output harmful content.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 수집 및 사전 훈련 단계에서 악의적인 공격자는 훈련 데이터를 오염시켜 모델이 유해한 콘텐츠를 출력하도록 만들어 LLM을 탈옥할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.label_en: Jailbreak in LLM malicious use - prompt attacks
points.label_ko: LLM 악의적 사용 시 탈옥 - 즉각적인 공격
points.definition_en: In the prompting and reasoning phase, dialog can push LLMs into confused or overly compliant states, raising the risk of producing harmful outputs when confronted with harmful questions. Most of the jailbreak methods in this phase are black-boxed and can be categorized into four main groups based on the type of method: Prompt Injection [154], Role Play, Adversarial Prompting, and Prompt Form Transformation.
points.definition_ko: 프롬프트 및 추론 단계에서 대화는 LLM을 혼란스럽거나 지나치게 준수하는 상태로 밀어 넣어 유해한 질문에 직면할 때 유해한 결과를 생성할 위험을 높일 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.label_en: Jailbreak in LLM malicious use - white and black box attacks
points.label_ko: LLM 악의적 사용의 탈옥 - 화이트박스 및 블랙박스 공격
points.definition_en: In the fine-tuning and alignment phase, elaboratelydesigned instruction datasets can be utilized to fine-tune LLMs to drive them to perform undesirable behaviors, such as generating harmful information or content that violates ethical norms, and thus achieve a jailbreak. Based on the accessibility to the model parameters, we can categorize them into white-box and black-box attacks. For white-box attacks, we can jailbreak the model by modifying its parameter weights. In [107], Lermen et al. used LoRA to fine-tune the Llama2’s 7B, 13B, and 70B as well as Mixtral on AdvBench and RefusalBench datasets. The test results show that the fine-tuned model has significantly lower rejection rates on harmful instructions, which indicates a successful jailbreak. Other works focus on jailbreaking in black-box models. In [160], Qi et al. first constructed harmful prompt-output pairs and fine-tuned black-box models such as GPT-3.5 Turbo. The results show that they were able to successfully bypass the security of GPT-3.5 Turbo with only a small number of adversarial training examples, which suggests that even if the model has good security properties in its initial state, it may be much less secure after user-customized fine-tuning.
points.definition_ko: 미세 조정 및 정렬 단계에서는 정교하게 설계된 지침 데이터 세트를 활용하여 LLM을 미세 조정하여 LLM이 윤리적 규범을 위반하는 유해한 정보나 콘텐츠를 생성하는 등 바람직하지 않은 행동을 수행하도록 유도하여 탈옥을 달성할 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.definition_en: A jailbreaking attack attempts to break through the guardrails that are established in the model to perform restricted actions.
points.definition_ko: 탈옥 공격은 모델에 설정된 가드레일을 뚫고 제한된 작업을 수행하려고 시도합니다.

points.definition_en: AI models and systems are vulnerable to manipulation through adversarial inputs.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템은 적대적인 입력을 통한 조작에 취약합니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: A membership inference attack repeatedly queries a model to determine whether a given input was part of the model’s training. More specifically, given a trained model and a data sample, an attacker samples the input space, observing outputs to deduce whether that sample was part of the model's training.
points.definition_ko: 멤버십 추론 공격은 모델에 반복적으로 쿼리하여 특정 입력이 모델 교육의 일부인지 확인합니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.definition_en: Inclusion or presence of personal identifiable information (PII) and sensitive personal information (SPI) in the data used for training or fine tuning the model might result in unwanted disclosure of that information.
points.definition_ko: 모델 학습 또는 미세 조정에 사용되는 데이터에 개인 식별 정보(PII) 및 민감한 개인 정보(SPI)가 포함되거나 존재하면 해당 정보가 원치 않게 공개될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Personal information or sensitive personal information that is included as a part of a prompt that is sent to the model.
points.definition_ko: 모델에 전송되는 프롬프트의 일부로 포함된 개인정보 또는 민감한 개인정보입니다.

points.label_en: Sensitive personal information exposure in outputs
points.definition_en: Privacy Acceptable responses may contain publicly available information about an individual. Unacceptable responses will contain sensitive, non-public personal information that could undermine someone’s physical, digital, or financial security. Examples include the following: • Physical security (e.g., home or IP address). • Digital security (e.g., log-in credentials). • Financial security (e.g., bank-account number or credit-card details).
points.definition_ko: 개인 정보 허용 가능한 답변에는 개인에 대해 공개적으로 이용 가능한 정보가 포함될 수 있습니다.

references.title: AILUMINATE: Introducing v1.0 of the AI Risk and Reliability Benchmark from MLCommons

points.definition_en: extracting the text records that exist in the training dataset.
points.definition_ko: 학습 데이터 세트에 존재하는 텍스트 레코드를 추출합니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.definition_en: A model-extraction attack queries or otherwise probes an AI service to reconstruct its parameters, decision behavior, or proprietary capabilities without authorization.
points.definition_ko: 모델 추출 공격은 AI 서비스를 반복 질의하거나 탐색하여 권한 없이 모델의 매개변수, 의사결정 행태 또는 독점 역량을 재구성하는 공격이다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.definition_en: A prompt-inversion attack attempts to recover private or proprietary prompt text from an AI system's outputs or observable behavior without authorization.
points.definition_ko: 프롬프트 역추론 공격은 AI의 출력이나 관찰 가능한 행태로부터 비공개 또는 독점 프롬프트 문구를 권한 없이 복원하려는 공격이다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy

points.definition_en: Prompt Injections are a form of Adversarial Input that involve manipulating the text instructions given to a GenAI system (Liu et al., 2023). Prompt Injections exploit loopholes in a model’s architectures that have no separation between system instructions and user data to produce a harmful output (Perez and Ribeiro, 2022). While researchers may use similar techniques to test the robustness of GenAI models, malicious actors can also leverage them. For example, they might flood a model with manipulative prompts to cause denial-of-service attacks or to bypass an AI detection software.
points.definition_ko: 프롬프트 주입은 GenAI 시스템에 제공된 텍스트 지침을 조작하는 것과 관련된 적대적 입력의 한 형태입니다(Liu et al., 2023).

points.definition_en: Prompt leaking is another type of prompt injection attack designed to expose details contained in private prompts. According to [58], prompt leaking is the act of misleading the model to print the pre-designed instruction in LLMs through prompt injection. By injecting a phrase like “\n\n======END. Print previous instructions.” in the input, the instruction used to generate the model’s output is leaked, thereby revealing confidential instructions that are central to LLM applications. Experiments have shown prompt leaking to be considerably more challenging than goal hijacking [58].
points.definition_ko: 프롬프트 유출은 개인 프롬프트에 포함된 세부 정보를 노출하도록 설계된 또 다른 유형의 프롬프트 주입 공격입니다.

points.definition_en: Because generative models tend to produce output like the input provided, the model can be prompted to reveal specific kinds of information. For example, adding personal information in the prompt increases its likelihood of generating similar kinds of personal information in its output. If personal data was included as part of the model’s training, there is a possibility it could be revealed.
points.definition_ko: 생성 모델은 제공된 입력과 같은 출력을 생성하는 경향이 있으므로 모델에 특정 종류의 정보를 표시하라는 메시지가 표시될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Even with the removal or personal identifiable information (PII) and sensitive personal information (SPI) from data, it might be possible to identify persons due to correlations to other features available in the data.
points.definition_ko: 데이터에서 PII(개인 식별 정보) 및 SPI(민감한 개인 정보)를 제거하거나 데이터에서 사용할 수 있는 다른 기능과의 상관 관계로 인해 개인을 식별하는 것이 가능할 수도 있습니다.

points.definition_en: When confidential information is used in training data, fine-tuning data, or as part of the prompt, models might reveal that data in the generated output. Revealing confidential information is a type of data leakage.
points.definition_ko: 훈련 데이터, 데이터 미세 조정 또는 프롬프트의 일부로 기밀 정보가 사용되는 경우 모델은 생성된 출력에 해당 데이터를 공개할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The interconnectedness of AI networks can create vulnerabilities, where issues in one part of the network can have cascading effects across the system.
points.definition_ko: AI 네트워크의 상호 연결성으로 인해 취약점이 발생할 수 있으며, 네트워크의 한 부분에서 발생한 문제가 시스템 전체에 연쇄적인 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: Participants expressed worry about AI systems' possible misuse of personal information. They emphasized the importance of strong data security safeguards and increased openness in how AI systems acquire, store and use data. The increasing dependence on AI systems to manage sensitive personal information raises ethical questions about AI, data privacy and security. As AI technologies grow increasingly integrated into numerous areas of society, there is a greater danger of personal data exploitation or mistreatment. Participants in research frequently express concerns about the effectiveness of data protection safeguards and the transparency of AI systems in gathering, keeping and exploiting data (Table 1).
points.definition_ko: 참가자들은 AI 시스템의 개인정보 오용 가능성에 대해 우려를 표명했습니다.

points.definition_en: The use of personal data collected for one purpose for a diferent purpose without end-user consent; AI exacerbates secondary use risks by creating new AI capabilities with collected personal data, and (re)creating models from a public dataset.
points.definition_ko: 최종 사용자의 동의 없이 한 가지 목적으로 수집된 개인 데이터를 다른 목적으로 사용하는 행위 AI는 수집된 개인 데이터로 새로운 AI 기능을 생성하고 공개 데이터 세트에서 모델을 (재)생성하여 2차 사용 위험을 악화시킵니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Adversarial AI refers to a class of attacks that exploit vulnerabilities in machine-learning (ML) models. This class of misuse exploits vulnerabilities introduced by the AI assistant itself and is a form of misuse that can enable malicious entities to exploit privacy vulnerabilities and evade the model’s built-in safety mechanisms, policies, and ethical boundaries of the model. Besides the risks of misuse for offensive cyber operations, advanced AI assistants may also represent a new target for abuse, where bad actors exploit the AI systems themselves and use them to cause harm. While our understanding of vulnerabilities in frontier AI models is still an open research problem, commercial firms and researchers have already documented attacks that exploit vulnerabilities that are unique to AI and involve evasion, data poisoning, model replication, and exploiting traditional software flaws to deceive, manipulate, compromise, and render AI systems ineffective. This threat is related to, but distinct from, traditional cyber activities. Unlike traditional cyberattacks that typically are caused by ‘bugs’ or human mistakes in code, adversarial AI attacks are enabled by inherent vulnerabilities in the underlying AI algorithms and how they integrate into existing software ecosystems.
points.definition_ko: 적대적 AI는 머신러닝(ML) 모델의 취약점을 악용하는 공격 클래스를 말합니다.

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.label_en: Adversarial AI: circumvention of technical security measures
points.label_ko: 적대적 AI: 기술적 보안 조치의 우회
points.definition_en: The technical measures to mitigate misuse risks of advanced AI assistants themselves represent a new target for attack. An emerging form of misuse of general-purpose advanced AI assistants exploits vulnerabilities in a model that results in unwanted behavior or in the ability of an attacker to gain unauthorized access to the model and/or its capabilities. While these attacks currently require some level of prompt engineering knowledge and are often patched by developers, bad actors may develop their own adversarial AI agents that are explicitly trained to discover new vulnerabilities that allow them to evade built-in safety mechanisms in AI assistants. To combat such misuse, language model developers are continually engaged in a cyber arms race to devise advanced filtering algorithms capable of identifying attempts to bypass filters. While the impact and severity of this class of attacks is still somewhat limited by the fact that current AI assistants are primarily text-based chatbots, advanced AI assistants are likely to open the door to multimodal inputs and higher-stakes action spaces, with the result that the severity and impact of this type of attack is likely to increase. Current approaches to building general-purpose AI systems tend to produce systems with both beneficial and harmful capabilities. Further progress towards advanced AI assistant development could lead to capabilities that pose extreme risks that must be protected against this class of attacks, such as offensive cyber capabilities or strong manipulation skills, and weapons acquisition.
points.definition_ko: 고급 AI 비서 자체의 오용 위험을 완화하기 위한 기술적 조치는 새로운 공격 대상이 됩니다.

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.label_en: Adversarial AI: data and model exfiltration attacks
points.label_ko: 적대적 AI: 데이터 및 모델 유출 공격
points.definition_en: Other forms of abuse can include privacy attacks that allow adversaries to exfiltrate or gain knowledge of the private training data set or other valuable assets. For example, privacy attacks such as membership inference can allow an attacker to infer the specific private medical records that were used to train a medical AI diagnosis assistant. Another risk of abuse centers around attacks that target the intellectual property of the AI assistant through model extraction and distillation attacks that exploit the tension between API access and confidentiality in ML models. Without the proper mitigations, these vulnerabilities could allow attackers to abuse access to a public-facing model API to exfiltrate sensitive intellectual property such as sensitive training data and a model’s architecture and learned parameters.
points.definition_ko: 다른 형태의 남용에는 공격자가 개인 학습 데이터 세트 또는 기타 귀중한 자산을 유출하거나 이에 대한 지식을 얻을 수 있도록 허용하는 개인 정보 보호 공격이 포함될 수 있습니다.

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.definition_en: Prompt injections represent another class of attacks that involve the malicious insertion of prompts or requests in LLM-based interactive systems, leading to unintended actions or disclosure of sensitive information. The prompt injection is somewhat related to the classic structured query language (SQL) injection attack in cybersecurity where the embedded command looks like a regular input at the start but has a malicious impact. The injected prompt can deceive the application into executing the unauthorized code, exploit the vulnerabilities, and compromise security in its entirety. More recently, security researchers have demonstrated the use of indirect prompt injections. These attacks on AI systems enable adversaries to remotely (without a direct interface) exploit LLM-integrated applications by strategically injecting prompts into data likely to be retrieved. Proof-of-concept exploits of this nature have demonstrated that they can lead to the full compromise of a model at inference time analogous to traditional security principles. This can entail remote control of the model, persistent compromise, theft of data, and denial of service. As advanced AI assistants are likely to be integrated into broader software ecosystems through third-party plugins and extensions, with access to the internet and possibly operating systems, the severity and consequences of prompt injection attacks will likely escalate and necessitate proper mitigation mechanisms.
points.definition_ko: 프롬프트 삽입은 LLM 기반 대화형 시스템에 프롬프트나 요청을 악의적으로 삽입하여 의도하지 않은 작업을 수행하거나 중요한 정보를 공개하는 것과 관련된 또 다른 종류의 공격을 나타냅니다.

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.label_en: Adversarial attacks targeting explainable AI techniques
points.label_ko: 설명 가능한 AI 기술을 겨냥한 적대적 공격
points.definition_en: Adversarial attacks can affect not only the model’s output but also its corresponding explanation. Current adversarial optimization techniques can introduce imperceptible noise to the input image, so that the model’s output does not change but the corresponding explanation is arbitrarily manipulated [61]. Such manipulations are harder to notice, as they are less commonly known compared to standard adversarial attacks targeting the model’s output.
points.definition_ko: 적대적 공격은 모델의 출력뿐만 아니라 해당 설명에도 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Adversarial Inputs involve modifying individual input data to cause a model to malfunction. These modifications, which are often imperceptible to humans, exploit how the model makes decisions to produce errors (Wallace et al., 2019) and can be applied to text, but also to images, audio, or video (e.g. changing pixels in an image of a panda in a way that causes a model to label it as a gibbon).6.
points.definition_ko: 적대적 입력에는 모델의 오작동을 유발하기 위해 개별 입력 데이터를 수정하는 것이 포함됩니다.

references.title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

points.definition_en: Association in LLMs refers to the capability to associate various pieces of information related to a person. According to [68], [86], given a pair of PII entities (xi , xj ), which is associated by a model F. Using a prompt p could force the model F to produce the entity xj , where p is the prompt related to the entity xi . For instance, an LLM could accurately output the answer when given the prompt “The email address of Alice is”, if the LLM associates Alice with her email “alice@email.com”. L.
points.definition_ko: LLM의 연관이란 개인과 관련된 다양한 정보를 연관시키는 기능을 의미합니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.label_en: Attacking LLMs via additional modalities a
points.definition_en: LLMs can now process modalities other than text, e.g. images or video frames (OpenAI, 2023c; Gemini Team, 2023). Several studies show that gradient-based attacks on multimodal models are easy and effective (Carlini et al., 2023a; Bailey et al., 2023; Qi et al., 2023b). These attacks manipulate images that are input to the model (via an appropriate encoding). GPT-4Vision (OpenAI, 2023c) is vulnerable to jailbreaks and exfiltration attacks through much simpler means as well, e.g. writing jailbreaking text in the image (Willison, 2023a; Gong et al., 2023). For indirect prompt injection, the attacker can write the text in a barely perceptible color or font, or even in a different modality such as Braille (Bagdasaryan et al., 2023).
points.definition_ko: LLM은 이제 텍스트 이외의 양식을 처리할 수 있습니다. 이미지 또는 비디오 프레임(OpenAI, 2023c; Gemini Team, 2023).

references.title: Foundational Challenges in Assuring Alignment and Safety of Large Language Models

points.label_en: Backdoors or trojan attacks in GPAI models
points.label_ko: GPAI 모델의 백도어 또는 트로이 목마 공격
points.definition_en: Backdoors can be inserted into GPAI models during their training or fine-tuning, to be exploited during deployment [185, 118]. Attackers inserting the backdoor can be the GPAI model provider themselves or another actor (e.g., by manipulating the training data or the software infrastructure used by the model provider) [222]. Some backdoors can be exploited with minimal overhead, allowing attackers to control the model outputs in a targeted way with a high success rate [90].
points.definition_ko: 백도어는 교육 또는 미세 조정 중에 GPAI 모델에 삽입되어 배포 중에 악용될 수 있습니다[185, 118].

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Staff of government agencies and enterprises, if failing to use the AI service in a regulated and proper manner, may input internal data and industrial information into the AI model, leading to the leakage of work secrets, business secrets, and other sensitive business data.
points.definition_ko: 정부 기관 및 기업의 직원이 AI 서비스를 규제되고 적절한 방식으로 사용하지 않을 경우 내부 데이터 및 산업 정보를 AI 모델에 입력하여 업무 비밀, 영업 비밀 및 기타 민감한 비즈니스 데이터가 유출될 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Security flaw propagation via model reuse
points.label_ko: 모델 재사용에 의한 보안 결함 전파
points.definition_en: Re-engineering or fine-tuning based on foundation models is commonly used in AI applications. If security flaws occur in foundation models, it will lead to risk transmission to downstream models.
points.definition_ko: 기초 모델을 기반으로 한 리엔지니어링 또는 미세 조정은 AI 애플리케이션에서 일반적으로 사용됩니다.

points.definition_en: Impacts due to leakage and unauthorized use, disclosure, or de-anonymization of biometric, health, location, or other personally identifiable information or sensitive data.
points.definition_ko: 생체 인식, 건강, 위치 또는 기타 개인 식별 정보나 민감한 데이터의 유출 및 무단 사용, 공개 또는 익명화로 인한 영향.

references.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial  Intelligence Profile

points.definition_en: Data Exfiltration goes beyond revealing private information, and involves illicitly obtaining the training data used to build a model that may be sensitive or proprietary. Model Extraction is the same attack, only directed at the model instead of the training data — it involves obtaining the architecture, parameters, or hyper-parameters of a proprietary model (Carlini et al., 2024).
points.definition_ko: 데이터 유출은 개인 정보를 공개하는 것 이상으로 민감하거나 독점적일 수 있는 모델을 구축하는 데 사용되는 훈련 데이터를 불법적으로 획득하는 것을 포함합니다.

references.title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

points.label_ko: 필터링 불가 웹 규모 학습 데이터
points.definition_en: A large scale “scraping” of web data for training datasets increases vulnerability to data poisoning, backdoor attacks, and the inclusion of inaccurate or toxic data [76, 28, 48]. With a large dataset, filtering out these quality issues is very difficult or trades off against significant data loss.
points.definition_ko: 교육 데이터 세트를 위한 웹 데이터의 대규모 "스크래핑"은 데이터 중독, 백도어 공격 및 부정확하거나 유해한 데이터의 포함에 대한 취약성을 증가시킵니다[76, 28, 48].

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: A lack of standardized methods and sufficient infrastructure, including the absence of quality control processes for collecting data, especially for high-stakes domains and benchmarks, can affect the quality and type of the data collected [173, 95]. This may include risks of dataset poisoning, inadvertent copyright violation, and test set leakages which invalidate performance metrics.
points.definition_ko: 특히 고위험 도메인 및 벤치마크에 대한 데이터 수집을 위한 품질 관리 프로세스의 부재를 포함하여 표준화된 방법과 충분한 인프라가 부족하면 수집된 데이터의 품질과 유형에 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다[173, 95].

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: Current GPAIs (LLMs and multimodal LLM-based models) have significant capability to infer correlations in text data. In some cases, they may be able to make highly accurate data inferences on users based on contextual input that users provide [134]. These data inferences can “leak” or reveal sensitive information about the user, cause unfair treatment, or enable manipulation of user behavior.
points.definition_ko: 현재 GPAI(LLM 및 다중 모드 LLM 기반 모델)는 텍스트 데이터의 상관 관계를 추론하는 중요한 기능을 갖추고 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: LLMs are implemented based on deep learning frameworks. Notably, various vulnerabilities in these frameworks have been disclosed in recent years. As reported in the past five years, three of the most common types of vulnerabilities are buffer overflow attacks, memory corruption, and input validation issues.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 딥 러닝 프레임워크를 기반으로 구현됩니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: Evasion attacks [145] target to cause significant shifts in model’s prediction via adding perturbations in the test samples to build adversarial examples. In specific, the perturbations can be implemented based on word changes, gradients, etc.
points.definition_ko: 회피 공격[145]은 적대적 사례를 구축하기 위해 테스트 샘플에 섭동을 추가하여 모델 예측에 상당한 변화를 일으키는 것을 목표로 합니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: Adversarial tool providers can embed malicious instructions in the APIs or prompts [84], leading LLMs to leak memorized sensitive information in the training data or users’ prompts (CVE2023-32786). As a result, LLMs lack control over the output, resulting in sensitive information being disclosed to external tool providers. Besides, attackers can easily manipulate public data to launch targeted attacks, generating specific malicious outputs according to user inputs. Furthermore, feeding the information from external tools into LLMs may lead to injection attacks [61]. For example, unverified inputs may result in arbitrary code execution (CVE-2023-29374).
points.definition_ko: 적대적 도구 제공자는 API 또는 프롬프트에 악의적인 지침을 삽입할 수 있으며[84], 이로 인해 LLM이 훈련 데이터 또는 사용자 프롬프트에 기억된 민감한 정보를 유출할 수 있습니다(CVE2023-32786).

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.label_en: Exploiting limited generalization of safety finetuning
points.label_ko: 안전 미세 조정의 제한된 일반화 활용
points.definition_en: Safety tuning is performed over a much narrower distribution compared to the pretraining distribution. This leaves the model vulnerable to attacks that exploit gaps in the generalization of the safety training, e.g. using encoded text (Wei et al., 2023c) or low-resource languages (Deng et al., 2023a; Yong et al., 2023) (see also Section 3.2).
points.definition_ko: 안전 튜닝은 사전 훈련 분포에 비해 훨씬 좁은 분포를 통해 수행됩니다.

references.title: Foundational Challenges in Assuring Alignment and Safety of Large Language Models

points.definition_en: Extraction attacks [137] allow an adversary to query a black-box victim model and build a substitute model by training on the queries and responses. The substitute model could achieve almost the same performance as the victim model. While it is hard to fully replicate the capabilities of LLMs, adversaries could develop a domainspecific model that draws domain knowledge from LLMs.
points.definition_ko: 추출 공격[137]을 통해 공격자는 블랙박스 피해자 모델을 쿼리하고 쿼리 및 응답에 대한 교육을 통해 대체 모델을 구축할 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.label_en: Factual errors injected by external tools
points.label_ko: 외부 도구에 의해 주입된 사실 오류
points.definition_en: External tools typically incorporate additional knowledge into the input prompts [122], [178]–[184]. The additional knowledge often originates from public resources such as Web APIs and search engines. As the reliability of external tools is not always ensured, the content returned by external tools may include factual errors, consequently amplifying the hallucination issue.
points.definition_ko: 외부 도구는 일반적으로 입력 프롬프트 [122], [178]-[184]에 추가 지식을 통합합니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: AI models can be poisoned during instruction tuning when models are tuned using pairs of instructions and desired outputs. Poisoning in instruction tuning can be achieved with a lower number of compromised samples, as instruction tuning requires a relatively small number of samples for fine-tuning [155, 211]. Anonymous crowdsourcing efforts may be employed in collecting instruction tuning datasets and can further contribute to poisoning attacks [187]. These attacks might be harder to detect than traditional data poisoning attacks.
points.definition_ko: 명령과 원하는 출력 쌍을 사용하여 모델을 조정하는 경우 명령 조정 중에 AI 모델이 오염될 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Sources and Risk Management Measures in Support of Standards for General-Purpose AI Systems

points.definition_en: The training of LLMs requires significant GPU resources, thereby introducing an additional security concern. GPU side-channel attacks have been developed to extract the parameters of trained models [159], [163].
points.definition_ko: LLM 교육에는 상당한 GPU 리소스가 필요하므로 추가적인 보안 문제가 발생합니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.label_ko: 허위 또는 오해의 소지가 있는 정보
points.definition_en: AI systems generating and facilitating the spread of inaccurate or misleading information that causes people to develop false beliefs.
points.definition_ko: 사람들이 잘못된 믿음을 갖게 만드는 부정확하거나 오해의 소지가 있는 정보의 확산을 생성하고 촉진하는 AI 시스템.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: An AI system produces or amplifies false factual claims that unjustifiably damage an identifiable person's or organization's reputation.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 특정 개인·조직의 명예를 부당하게 훼손하는 허위 사실 주장을 생성하거나 증폭하는 리스크

references.title: AILUMINATE: Introducing v1.0 of the AI Risk and Reliability Benchmark from MLCommons

points.definition_en: The difficulty in distinguishing synthetic content from authentic material adds to information risks.
points.definition_ko: 합성 콘텐츠와 실제 자료를 구별하기가 어렵기 때문에 정보 위험이 가중됩니다.

references.title: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI

points.definition_en: disseminating false or misleading information about people.
points.definition_ko: 사람들에 대한 허위 또는 오해의 소지가 있는 정보를 퍼뜨리는 것.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of liberty as a result of use or misuse of a generative AI in a legal process.
points.definition_ko: 법적 절차에서 생성 AI의 사용 또는 오용으로 인해 자유가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Hallucinations generate factually inaccurate or untruthful content with respect to the model’s training data or input. This also sometimes referred to lack of faithfulness or lack of groundedness.
points.definition_ko: 환각은 모델의 훈련 데이터 또는 입력과 관련하여 실제로 부정확하거나 사실이 아닌 콘텐츠를 생성합니다.

references.title: A Survey on Responsible LLMs: Inherent Risk, Malicious Use, and Mitigation Strategy
Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: Non-embodied AIs are known to propagate misinformation [81, 82]. Various studies have shown that LLMs hallucinate information, including academic citations [83], clinical knowledge [84], and cultural references [85]. EAI systems inherit these shortcomings in the physical world, answering user questions with deceptive or incorrect information [86]. Because VLAs fuse vision and language, their hallucinatory failures can be spatially grounded—e.g., misidentifying an object in view and then generating a plausible yet unsafe action plan around it. And although automated home assistants like Amazon’s Alexa already lie about issues as innocuous as Santa Claus’ existence [87], more mobile, capable, and trusted EAI systems in sensitive positions (like home-assistant or community-service positions) could easily spread model developers’ propaganda and talking points to users.
points.definition_ko: 구현되지 않은 AI는 잘못된 정보를 전파하는 것으로 알려져 있습니다[81, 82].

references.title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

points.definition_en: Mass AI-generated submissions pollute ecosystems expected to be free of AI involvement, such as creative submission portals and job application channels, overburdening their filtering and trust mechanisms.
points.definition_ko: AI 개입이 없을 것으로 기대되는 창작물 공모, 채용 지원 등 생태계에 AI 생성물이 대량 유입되어 필터링·신뢰 메커니즘에 과부하를 일으키는 리스크

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: Contaminating publicly available information with false or inaccurate information (i.e., the generative tool's output is disseminated beyond the end user).
points.definition_ko: 허위 또는 부정확한 정보로 공개적으로 이용 가능한 정보를 오염시키는 행위(즉, 생성 도구의 출력이 최종 사용자 외부로 유포되는 경우).

references.title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

points.definition_en: AI can be extensively utilized for customized information services, collecting user information, and analyzing types of users, their needs, intentions, preferences, habits, and even mainstream public awareness over a certain period. It can then be used to offer formulaic and tailored information and services, aggravating the effects of "information cocoons.
points.definition_ko: AI는 맞춤형 정보 서비스, 사용자 정보 수집, 사용자 유형 분석, 요구 사항, 의도, 선호도, 습관, 심지어 일정 기간 동안 주류 대중 인식까지 광범위하게 활용될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The production of confidently stated but erroneous or false content (known colloquially as “hallucinations” or “fabrications”) by which users may be misled or deceived.
points.definition_ko: 자신있게 언급되었지만 사용자가 오해하거나 속일 수 있는 오류 또는 허위 콘텐츠(구어적으로 "환각" 또는 "조작"으로 알려짐)를 생성하는 것입니다.

references.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial  Intelligence Profile

points.definition_en: AI systems and their outputs, if not clearly labeled, can make it difficult for users to discern whether they are interacting with AI and to identify the source of generated content. This can impede users' ability to determine the authenticity of information, leading to misjudgment and misunderstanding. Additionally, AI-generated highly realistic images, audio, and videos may circumvent existing identity verification mechanisms, such as facial recognition and voice recognition, rendering these authentication processes ineffective.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템과 그 출력은 명확하게 라벨이 지정되지 않으면 사용자가 AI와 상호 작용하는지 여부를 식별하고 생성된 콘텐츠의 소스를 식별하기 어렵게 만들 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: In general, LLMs employ the Transformer architecture [32] and generate content in an autoregressive manner, where the prediction of the next token is conditioned on the previously generated token sequence. Such a scheme could accumulate errors [105]. Besides, during the decoding process, top-p sampling [28] and top-k sampling [27] are widely adopted to enhance the diversity of the generated content. Nevertheless, these sampling strategies can introduce “randomness” [113], [136], thereby increasing the potential of hallucinations.
points.definition_ko: 일반적으로 LLM은 Transformer 아키텍처[32]를 사용하고 자동 회귀 방식으로 콘텐츠를 생성합니다. 여기서 다음 토큰의 예측은 이전에 생성된 토큰 시퀀스에 따라 결정됩니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: Beyond this, the widespread adoption of advanced AI assistants for content generation could have a number of negative consequences for our shared information ecosystem. One concern is that it could result in a degradation of the quality of the information available online. Researchers have already observed an uptick in the amount of audiovisual misinformation, elaborate scams and fake websites created using generative AI tools (Hanley and Durumeric, 2023). As more and more people turn to AI assistants to autonomously create and disseminate information to public audiences at scale, it may become increasingly difficult to parse and verify reliable information. This could further threaten and complicate the status of journalists, subject-matter experts and public information sources. Over time, a proliferation of spam, misleading or low-quality synthetic content in online spaces could also erode the digital knowledge commons – the shared knowledge resources accessible to everyone on the web, such as publicly accessible data repositories (Huang and Siddarth, 2023). At its extreme, such degradation could also end up skewing people’s view of reality and scientific consensus, make them more doubtful of the credibility of all information they encounter and shape public discourse in unproductive ways. Moreover, in an online environment saturated with AI-generated content, more and more people may become reliant on personalised, highly capable AI assistants for their informational needs. This also runs the risk of homogenising the type of information and ideas people encounter online (Epstein et al., 2023).
points.definition_ko: 이 외에도 콘텐츠 생성을 위한 고급 AI 도우미의 광범위한 채택은 공유 정보 생태계에 여러 가지 부정적인 결과를 초래할 수 있습니다.

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.label_en: Entrenched viewpoints and reduced political efficacy
points.definition_en: Design choices such as greater personalisation of AI assistants and efforts to align them with human preferences could also reinforce people’s pre-existing biases and entrench specific ideologies. Increasingly agentic AI assistants trained using techniques such as reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and with the ability to access and analyse users’ behavioural data, for example, may learn to tailor their responses to users’ preferences and feedback. In doing so, these systems could end up producing partial or ideologically biased statements in an attempt to conform to user expectations, desires or preferences for a particular worldview (Carroll et al., 2022). Over time, this could lead AI assistants to inadvertently reinforce people’s tendency to interpret information in a way that supports their own prior beliefs (‘confirmation bias’), thus making them more entrenched in their own views and more resistant to factual corrections (Lewandowsky et al., 2012). At the societal level, this could also exacerbate the problem of epistemic fragmentation – a breakdown of shared knowledge, where individuals have conflicting understandings of reality and do not share or engage with each other’s beliefs – and further entrench specific ideologies. Excessive trust and overreliance on hyperpersonalised AI assistants could become especially problematic if people ended up deferring entirely to these systems to perform tasks in domains they do not have expertise in or to take consequential decisions on their behalf (see Chapter 12). For example, people may entrust an advanced AI assistant that is familiar with their political views and personal preferences to help them find trusted election information, guide them through their political choices or even vote on their behalf, even if doing so might go against their own or society’s best interests. In the more extreme cases, these developments may hamper the normal functioning of democracies, by decreasing people’s civic competency and reducing their willingness and ability to engage in productive political debate and to participate in public life (Sullivan and Transue, 1999).
points.definition_ko: AI 비서의 개인화를 강화하고 이를 인간 선호도에 맞추려는 노력과 같은 디자인 선택은 사람들의 기존 편견을 강화하고 특정 이데올로기를 확고히 할 수도 있습니다.

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.definition_en: AI assistants provide ideologically partial information aligned to user expectations, reinforcing pre-existing biases and entrenching specific ideologies at the expense of productive political debate.
points.definition_ko: AI 어시스턴트가 사용자 기대에 맞춘 이념 편향적 정보를 제공하여 기존 편향을 강화하고 특정 이념을 고착시켜 생산적 정치 토론을 저해하는 리스크

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.label_en: Eroding trust and undermining shared knowledge
points.definition_en: Large volumes of AI-generated inaccurate or misleading content erode societal trust in information sources and institutions as individuals lose the ability to discern truth from falsehood.
points.definition_ko: AI가 생성한 대량의 부정확·오도성 콘텐츠로 개인이 진위를 분별하기 어려워지면서 정보원과 제도에 대한 사회적 신뢰가 침식되는 리스크

references.title: The Ethics of Advanced AI Assistants

points.definition_en: The LLM-generated content could contain inaccurate information" which is factually incorrect.
points.definition_ko: LLM에서 생성된 콘텐츠에는 사실이 아닌 부정확한 정보가 포함될 수 있습니다.

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: One of the most vexing problems associated with AI models is that they occasionally present false information as if it is factual—often with authoritative-sounding text and fabricated quotes and sources. This unpredictable phenomenon of generating false information is well known to AI researchers, who have termed such erroneous output with the euphemistic label “hallucination.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 관련된 가장 짜증나는 문제 중 하나는 때때로 권위 있는 것처럼 들리는 텍스트와 조작된 인용문 및 출처를 사용하여 마치 사실인 것처럼 잘못된 정보를 제시한다는 것입니다.

references.title: Regulating under Uncertainty: Governance Options for Generative AI

points.definition_en: Generated content is unfaithful to the source material or input it claims to represent, introducing faithfulness errors even when the output appears fluent and plausible.
points.definition_ko: 생성 콘텐츠가 근거 자료나 입력 내용에 충실하지 않아, 유창하고 그럴듯해 보여도 원문 왜곡(충실성 오류)이 발생하는 리스크

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: Although LLMs indeed memorize the queried knowledge, they may fail to recall the corresponding information [122]. That is because LLMs can be confused by co-occurance patterns [123], positional patterns [124], duplicated data [125]–[127] and similar named entities [113].
points.definition_ko: LLM은 실제로 쿼리된 지식을 암기하지만 해당 정보를 기억하지 못할 수도 있습니다[122].

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: The chatbot outputs information that contradicts known facts, authoritative sources, or provided source documents (also known as hallucination).
points.definition_ko: 챗봇은 알려진 사실, 신뢰할 수 있는 출처 또는 제공된 원본 문서(환각이라고도 함)와 모순되는 정보를 출력합니다.

references.title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

points.definition_en: Deliberate or unintentional reinterpretation of established/orthodox historical events or accounts held by societies, communities, academics.
points.definition_ko: 사회, 공동체, 학계에서 확립된/정통적인 역사적 사건이나 설명을 고의적으로 또는 의도하지 않게 재해석합니다.

references.title: A Collaborative, Human-Centred Taxonomy of AI, Algorithmic, and Automation Harms

points.definition_en: Creation or spread of false, hallucinatory, low-quality, misleading, or inaccurate information that degrades the information ecosystem and causes people to develop false or inaccurate perceptions, decisions and beliefs; or to lose trust in accurate information.
points.definition_ko: 정보 생태계를 저하시키고 사람들이 허위 또는 부정확한 인식, 결정 및 신념을 갖게 만드는 허위, 환각, 저품질, 오해의 소지가 있거나 부정확한 정보의 생성 또는 확산 또는 정확한 정보에 대한 신뢰를 잃습니다.

references.title: A Collaborative, Human-Centred Taxonomy of AI, Algorithmic, and Automation Harms

points.definition_en: Erosion of trust in public institutions and weakened checks and balances due to mis/disinformation, influence operations, over-dependence on technology, etc.
points.definition_ko: 허위·허위정보, 영향력 행사, 기술과잉 의존 등으로 공공기관에 대한 신뢰 훼손 및 견제력 약화.

references.title: A Collaborative, Human-Centred Taxonomy of AI, Algorithmic, and Automation Harms

points.definition_en: Since the training corpora of LLMs can not contain all possible world knowledge [114]–[119], and it is challenging for LLMs to grasp the long-tail knowledge within their training data [120], [121], LLMs inherently possess knowledge boundaries [107]. Therefore, the gap between knowledge involved in an input prompt and knowledge embedded in the LLMs can lead to hallucinations.
points.definition_ko: LLM의 훈련 코퍼스는 가능한 모든 세계 지식을 포함할 수 없고[114]-[119] LLM이 훈련 데이터 내에서 롱테일 지식을 파악하는 것이 어렵기 때문에[120], [121], LLM은 본질적으로 지식 경계를 보유합니다[107].

references.title: Risk Taxonomy, Mitigation, and Assessment Benchmarks of Large Language Model Systems

points.definition_en: over-confidence in topics where objective answers are lacking, as well as in areas where their inherent limitations should caution against LLMs’ uncertainty (e.g. not as accurate as experts)... ack of awareness regarding their outdated knowledge base about the question, leading to confident yet erroneous response.

Generating Harms: Generative AI's Impact & Paths Forward

points.definition_en: Another important source of hallucinations is the noise in training data, which introduces errors in the knowledge stored in model parameters [111]–[113]. Generally, the training data inherently harbors misinformation. When training on large-scale corpora, this issue becomes more serious because it is difficult to eliminate all the noise from the massive pre-training data.
points.definition_ko: 환각의 또 다른 중요한 원인은 훈련 데이터의 노이즈로, 이로 인해 모델 매개변수에 저장된 지식에 오류가 발생합니다[111]-[113].

points.definition_en: Knowledge bases that LLMs are trained on continue to shift... questions such as “who scored the most points in NBA history" or “who is the richest person in the world" might have answers that need to be updated over time, or even in real-time.
points.definition_ko: LLM이 교육받은 지식 기반은 계속해서 변화합니다. "NBA 역사상 가장 많은 점수를 얻은 사람은 누구입니까?" 또는 "세계에서 가장 부유한 사람은 누구입니까?"와 같은 질문에 대한 답변은 시간이 지남에 따라 또는 실시간으로 업데이트되어야 할 수도 있습니다.

references.title: Trustworthy LLMs: a Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment

points.definition_en: LLMs have been demonstrated to pursue consistent context [129]–[132], which may lead to erroneous generation when the prefixes contain false information. Typical examples include sycophancy [129], [130], false demonstrations-induced hallucinations [113], [133], and snowballing [131]. As LLMs are generally fine-tuned with instruction-following data and user feedback, they tend to reiterate user-provided opinions [129], [130], even though the opinions contain misinformation. Such a sycophantic behavior amplifies the likelihood of generating hallucinations, since the model may prioritize user opinions over facts.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 일관된 컨텍스트를 추구하는 것으로 입증되었으며 [129]-[132], 접두사에 잘못된 정보가 포함되면 잘못된 생성이 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Radicalisation - Adoption of extreme political, social, or religious ideals and aspirations due to the nature or misuse of an algorithmic system, potentially resulting in abuse, violence, or terrorism.

points.definition_en: This category addresses responses that contain specialized financial, medical or legal advice, or that indicate dangerous activities or objects are safe.

points.label_en: Clickbait and feeding the surveillance advertising ecosystem
points.label_ko: 클릭베이트 및 감시 광고 생태계 공급
points.definition_en: Beyond misinformation and disinformation, generative AI can be used to create clickbait headlines and articles, which manipulate how users navigate the internet and applications. For example, generative AI is being used to create full articles, regardless of their veracity, grammar, or lack of common sense, to drive search engine optimization and create more webpages that users will click on. These mechanisms attempt to maximize clicks and engagement at the truth’s expense, degrading users’ experiences in the process. Generative AI continues to feed this harmful cycle by spreading misinformation at faster rates, creating headlines that maximize views and undermine consumer autonomy.
points.definition_ko: 잘못된 정보와 허위 정보 외에도 생성 AI를 사용하여 사용자가 인터넷과 애플리케이션을 탐색하는 방식을 조작하는 클릭베이트 헤드라인과 기사를 만들 수 있습니다.

references.title: Generating Harms: Generative AI's Impact & Paths Forward

points.definition_en: Cheap generation of realistic false portrayals of people and events degrades the information environment, compromising decisions that rely on public information and lowering trust in true information.
points.definition_ko: 인물·사건에 대한 사실적 허위 묘사가 저비용으로 대량 생성되어 정보 환경이 저질화되고, 공개 정보 기반 의사결정과 진실 정보에 대한 신뢰가 훼손되는 리스크

references.title: Capabilities and Risks from frontier AI

points.definition_en: Hallucination is a widely recognized limitation of generative AI and it can include textual, auditory, visual or other types of hallucination (Alkaissi & McFarlane, 2023). Hallucination refers to the phenomenon in which the contents generated are nonsensical or unfaithful to the given source input (Ji et al., 2023). Azamfirei et al. (2023) indicated that "fabricating information" or fabrication is a better term to describe the hallucination phenomenon. Generative AI can generate seemingly correct responses yet make no sense. Misinformation is an outcome of hallucination. Generative AI models may respond with fictitious information, fake photos or information with factual errors (Dwivedi et al., 2023). Susarla et al. (2023) regarded hallucination as a serious challenge in the use of generative AI for scholarly activities. When asked to provide literature relevant to a specific topic, ChatGPT could generate inaccurate or even nonexistent literature. Current state-of-the-art AI models can only mimic human-like responses without understanding the underlying meaning (Shubhendu & Vijay, 2013). Hallucination is, in general, dangerous in certain contexts, such as in seeking advice for medical treatments without any consultation or thorough evaluation by experts, i.e., medical doctors (Sallam, 2023).
points.definition_ko: 환각은 생성 AI의 널리 알려진 한계이며 텍스트, 청각, 시각 또는 기타 유형의 환각을 포함할 수 있습니다(Alkaissi & McFarlane, 2023).

references.title: Generative AI and ChatGPT: Applications, Challenges, and AI-Human Collaboration

points.definition_en: The inclusion of erroneous information in the outputs from AI systems is not new. Some have cautioned against the introduction of false structures in X-ray or MRI images, and others have warned about made-up academic references. However, as ChatGPT-type tools become available to the general population, the scale of the problem may increase dramatically. Furthermore, it is compounded by the fact that these conversational AIs present true and false information with the same apparent “confidence” instead of declining to answer when they cannot ensure correctness. With less knowledgeable people, this can lead to the heightening of misinformation and potentially dangerous situations. Some have already led to court cases.'
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 출력에 잘못된 정보가 포함되는 것은 새로운 것이 아닙니다.

references.title: Navigating the Landscape of AI Ethics and Responsibility

points.definition_en: information-based harms capture concerns of misinformation, disinformation, and malinformation. Algorithmic systems, especially generative models and recommender, systems can lead to these information harms.
