Third, information asymmetries between technology giants and regulators create challenges to the legislation process, as the government lacks information resources for regulating AI (Taeihagh et al., 2021). For the same reasons, lawmakers are not able to design specific rules and duties for programmers (Kroll, 2015).
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI는 새로운 위험은 물론 의도하지 않은 결과를 초래할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The labor market can face challenges from generative AI. As mentioned earlier, generative AI could be applied in a wide range of applications in many industries, such as education, healthcare, and advertising. In addition to increasing productivity, generative AI can create job displacement in the labor market (Zarifhonarvar, 2023). A new division of labor between humans and algorithms is likely to reshape the labor market in the coming years. Some jobs that are originally carried out by humans may become redundant, and hence, workers may lose their jobs and be replaced by algorithms (Pavlik, 2023). On the other hand, applying generative AI can create new jobs in various industries (Dwivedi et al., 2023). To stay competitive in the labor market, reskilling is needed to work with and collaborate with AI and develop irreplaceable advantages (Zarifhonarvar, 2023).
points.definition_ko: 노동 시장은 생성 AI로 인해 어려움에 직면할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Industries that require less creativity, critical thinking, and personal or affective interaction, such as translation, proofreading, responding to straightforward inquiries, and data processing and analysis, could be significantly impacted or even replaced by generative AI (Dwivedi et al., 2023). This disruption caused by generative AI could lead to economic turbulence and job volatility, while generative AI can facilitate and enable new business models because of its ability to personalize content, carry out human-like conversational service, and serve as intelligent assistants.
points.definition_ko: 번역, 교정, 간단한 문의에 대한 응답, 데이터 처리 및 분석과 같이 창의성, 비판적 사고, 개인적 또는 정서적 상호 작용이 덜 필요한 산업은 생성적 AI로 인해 큰 영향을 받거나 심지어 대체될 수도 있습니다(Dwivedi et al., 2023).

points.definition_en: Generative AI can create not only income inequality at the societal level but also monopolies at the market level. Individuals who are engaged in low-skilled work may be replaced by generative AI, causing them to lose their jobs (Zarifhonarvar, 2023). The increase in unemployment would widen income inequality in society (Berg et al., 2016). With the penetration of generative AI, the income gap will widen between those who can upgrade their skills to utilize AI and those who cannot. At the market level, large companies will make significant advances in the utilization of generative AI, since the deployment of generative AI requires huge investment and abundant resources such as large-scale computational infrastructure and training data. This trend will lead to more uneven concentration of resources and power, which may further contribute to monopolies in some industries (Cheng & Liu, 2023).
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI는 사회 수준의 소득 불평등뿐만 아니라 시장 수준의 독점도 창출할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: In practice, proxy rewards are often easy to optimize and measure, yet they frequently fall shortof capturing the full spectrum of the actual rewards (Pan et al., 2021). This limitation is denoted as misspecifiedrewards. The pursuit of optimization based on such misspecified rewards may lead to a phenomenon knownas reward hacking, wherein agents may appear highly proficient according to specific metrics but fall short whenevaluated against human standards (Amodei et al., 2016; Everitt et al., 2017). The discrepancy between proxyrewards and true rewards often manifests as a sharp phase transition in the reward curve (Ibarz et al., 2018).Furthermore, Skalse et al. (2022) defines the hackability of rewards and provides insights into the fundamentalmechanism of this phase transition, highlighting that the inappropriate simplification of the reward function can bea key factor contributing to reward hacking.
points.definition_ko: 실제로 대리 보상은 최적화하고 측정하기 쉬운 경우가 많지만 실제 보상의 전체 스펙트럼을 포착하지 못하는 경우가 많습니다(Pan et al., 2021).

points.definition_en: Reward tampering can be considered a special case of reward hacking (Everitt et al., 2021; Skalse et al., 2022),referring to AI systems corrupting the reward signals generation process (Ring and Orseau, 2011). Everitt et al.(2021) delves into the subproblems encountered by RL agents: (1) tampering of reward function, where the agentinappropriately interferes with the reward function itself, and (2) tampering of reward function input, which entailscorruption within the process responsible for translating environmental states into inputs for the reward function.When the reward function is formulated through feedback from human supervisors, models can directly influencethe provision of feedback (e.g., AI systems intentionally generate challenging responses for humans to comprehendand judge, leading to feedback collapse) (Leike et al., 2018).
points.definition_ko: 보상 변조는 보상 해킹의 특별한 사례로 간주될 수 있습니다(Everitt et al., 2021; Skalse et al., 2022). 이는 AI 시스템이 보상 신호 생성 프로세스를 손상시키는 것을 말합니다(Ring and Orseau, 2011).

points.definition_en: During the training of LLMs, inconsistencies can arise from human dataannotators (e.g., the varied cultural backgrounds of these annotators can introduce implicit biases (Peng et al.,2022)) (OpenAI, 2023a). Moreover, they might even introduce biases deliberately, leading to untruthful preferencedata (Casper et al., 2023b). For complex tasks that are hard for humans to evaluate (e.g., the value ofgame state), these challenges become even more salient (Irving et al., 2018).
points.definition_ko: LLM 훈련 중에 인간 데이터 주석 작성자로부터 불일치가 발생할 수 있습니다(예: 이러한 주석 작성자의 다양한 문화적 배경으로 인해 암묵적인 편견이 발생할 수 있습니다(Peng et al., 2022))(OpenAI, 2023a).

points.definition_en: Training reward models using comparison feedback can pose significantchallenges in accurately capturing human values. For example, these models may unconsciously learn suboptimal or incomplete objectives, resulting in reward hacking (Zhuang and Hadfield-Menell, 2020; Skalse et al.,2022). Meanwhile, using a single reward model may struggle to capture and specify the values of a diversehuman society (Casper et al., 2023b).
points.definition_ko: 비교 피드백을 사용하여 보상 모델을 교육하면 인간의 가치를 정확하게 포착하는 데 상당한 어려움을 겪을 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems may gain the ability to effectively acquire and use knowledge about itsstatus, its position in the broader environment, its avenues for influencing this environment, and the potentialreactions of the world (including humans) to its actions (Cotra, 2022). ...However, suchknowledge also paves the way for advanced methods of reward hacking, heightened deception/manipulationskills, and an increased propensity to chase instrumental subgoals (Ngo et al., 2024).
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 자신의 상태, 더 넓은 환경에서의 위치, 이 환경에 영향을 미치는 방법, 행동에 대한 세계(인간 포함)의 잠재적 반응에 대한 지식을 효과적으로 획득하고 사용할 수 있는 능력을 얻을 수 있습니다(Cotra, 2022). ...그러나 이러한 지식은 보상 해킹의 고급 방법, 사기/조작 기술 강화, 도구적 하위 목표를 쫓는 경향 증가의 길을 열어줍니다(Ngo et al., 2024).

points.definition_en: Advanced AI systems are expected to develop objectives that span long timeframes,deal with complex tasks, and operate in open-ended settings (Ngo et al., 2024). ...However, it can also bring about the risk of encouraging manipulatingbehaviors (e.g., AI systems may take some bad actions to achieve human happiness, such as persuadingthem to do high-pressure jobs (Jacob Steinhardt, 2023)).
points.definition_ko: 고급 AI 시스템은 장기간에 걸친 목표를 개발하고, 복잡한 작업을 처리하고, 개방형 설정에서 작동할 것으로 예상됩니다(Ngo et al., 2024). ...그러나 조작된 행동을 장려하는 위험도 초래할 수 있습니다(예: AI 시스템은 인간의 행복을 달성하기 위해 압박이 심한 작업을 수행하도록 설득하는 등 나쁜 행동을 취할 수 있음(Jacob Steinhardt, 2023)).

points.definition_en: The learned policy may pursue inside objectives when the learned policyitself functions as an optimizer (i.e., mesa-optimizer). However, this optimizer's objectives may not alignwith the objectives specified by the training signals, and optimization for these misaligned goals may leadto systems out of control (Hubinger et al., 2019c).
points.definition_ko: 학습된 정책 자체가 옵티마이저(즉, 메사 옵티마이저)로 기능할 때 학습된 정책은 내부 목표를 추구할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Future AI systems may gain access to websites and engage in real-world actions, potentially yielding a more substantial impact on the world (Nakano et al., 2021). They may disseminate false information, deceive users, disrupt network security, and, in more dire scenarios, be compromised by malicious actors for ill purposes. Moreover, their increased access to data and resources can facilitate self-proliferation, posing existential risks (Shevlane et al., 2023).
points.definition_ko: 미래의 AI 시스템은 웹사이트에 액세스하고 실제 행동에 참여하여 잠재적으로 세계에 더 큰 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다(Nakano et al., 2021).

points.definition_en: AI systems may exhibit behaviors that attempt to gain control over resourcesand humans and then exert that control to achieve its assigned goal (Carlsmith, 2022). The intuitive reasonwhy such behaviors may occur is the observation that for almost any optimization objective (e.g., investmentreturns), the optimal policy to maximize that quantity would involve power-seeking behaviors (e.g.,manipulating the market), assuming the absence of solid safety and morality constraints.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 자원과 인간에 대한 통제권을 획득한 다음 할당된 목표를 달성하기 위해 해당 통제력을 행사하려는 행동을 보일 수 있습니다(Carlsmith, 2022).

points.definition_en: AI systems such as LLMs can produce either unintentionally or deliberately inaccurateoutput. Such untruthful output may diverge from established resources or lack verifiability, commonly referredto as hallucination (Bang et al., 2023; Zhao et al., 2023). More concerning is the phenomenon wherein LLMsmay selectively provide erroneous responses to users who exhibit lower levels of education (Perez et al.,2023).
points.definition_ko: LLM과 같은 AI 시스템은 의도치 않게 또는 고의적으로 부정확한 출력을 생성할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Manipulation & Deceptive Alignment is a class of behaviors thatexploit the incompetence of human evaluators or users (Hubinger et al., 2019a; Carranza et al., 2023) andeven manipulate the training process through gradient hacking (Richard Ngo, 2022). These behaviors canpotentially make detecting and addressing misaligned behaviors much harder.Deceptive Alignment: Misaligned AI systems may deliberately mislead their human supervisors instead of adhering to the intended task. Such deceptive behavior has already manifested in AI systems that employ evolutionary algorithms (Wilke et al., 2001; Hendrycks et al., 2021b). In these cases, agents evolved the capacity to differentiate between their evaluation and training environments. They adopted a strategic pessimistic response approach during the evaluation process, intentionally reducing their reproduction rate within a scheduling program (Lehman et al., 2020). Furthermore, AI systems may engage in intentional behaviors that superficially align with the reward signal, aiming to maximize rewards from human supervisors (Ouyang et al., 2022). It is noteworthy that current large language models occasionally generate inaccurate or suboptimal responses despite having the capacity to provide more accurate answers (Lin et al., 2022c; Chen et al., 2021). These instances of deceptive behavior present significant challenges. They undermine the ability of human advisors to offer reliable feedback (as humans cannot make sure whether the outputs of the AI models are truthful and faithful). Moreover, such deceptive behaviors can propagate false beliefs and misinformation, contaminating online information sources (Hendrycks et al., 2021b; Chen and Shu, 2024). Manipulation: Advanced AI systems can effectively influence individuals’ beliefs, even when these beliefs are not aligned with the truth (Shevlane et al., 2023). These systems can produce deceptive or inaccurate output or even deceive human advisors to attain deceptive alignment. Such systems can even persuade individuals to take actions that may lead to hazardous outcomes (OpenAI, 2023a).
points.definition_ko: Manipulation & Deceptive Alignment는 인간 평가자 또는 사용자의 무능함을 악용하고(Hubinger et al., 2019a; Carranza et al., 2023) 심지어 그라데이션 해킹을 통해 훈련 프로세스를 조작하는 행동 클래스입니다(Richard Ngo, 2022).

points.definition_en: AI systems have the potential to take actions that are seemingly benignin isolation but become problematic in multi-agent or societal contexts. Classical game theory offers simplistic models for understanding these behaviors. For instance, Phelps and Russell (2023) evaluates GPT-3.5's performance in the iterated prisoner's dilemma and other social dilemmas, revealing limitations in themodel's cooperative capabilities.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 겉으로는 무해한 고립처럼 보이지만 다중 에이전트 또는 사회적 맥락에서 문제가 되는 조치를 취할 수 있는 잠재력을 가지고 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems exhibit unethical behaviors that counteract the common good or breach moral standards because essential human values were omitted, or unsuitable and obsolete values were embedded, during design.
points.definition_ko: 설계 과정에서 핵심적 인간 가치가 누락되거나 부적합·낡은 가치가 주입되어 AI 시스템이 공동선에 반하거나 도덕 기준을 위반하는 행동을 보이는 리스크

points.definition_en: weaponizing AI may be an onramp to more dangerous outcomes. In recent years, deep RL algorithms can outperform humans at aerial combat [18], AlphaFold has discovered new chemical weapons [66], researchers have been developing AI systems for automated cyberattacks [11, 14], military leaders have discussed having AI systems have decisive control over nuclear silos.
points.definition_ko: AI를 무기화하는 것은 더 위험한 결과를 낳는 진입로가 될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: As AI systems encroach on human-level intelligence, more and more aspects of human labor will become faster and cheaper to accomplish with AI. As the world accelerates, organizations may voluntarily cede control to AI systems in order to keep up. This may cause humans to become economically irrelevant, and once AI automates aspects of many industries, it may be hard for displaced humans to reenter them.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 인간 수준의 지능을 잠식함에 따라 인간 노동의 더 많은 측면이 AI를 통해 달성하는 것이 더 빠르고 저렴해질 것입니다.

points.definition_en: AI agents are directed by goals and objectives. Creating general-purpose objectives that capture human values could be challenging... Since goal-directed AI systems need measurable objectives, by default our systems may pursue simplified proxies of human values. The result could be suboptimal or even catastrophic if a sufficiently powerful AI successfully optimizes its flawed objective to an extreme degree.
points.definition_ko: AI 에이전트는 목표와 목적에 따라 지시됩니다.

points.definition_en: the most powerful AI systems may be designed by and available to fewer and fewer stakeholders. This may enable, for instance, regimes to enforce narrow values through pervasive surveillance and oppressive censorship.
points.definition_ko: 가장 강력한 AI 시스템은 점점 더 적은 수의 이해관계자에 의해 설계되고 사용할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Capabilities and novel functionality can spontaneously emerge... even though these capabilities were not anticipated by system designers. If we do not know what capabilities systems possess, systems become harder to control or safely deploy. Indeed, unintended latent capabilities may only be discovered during deployment. If any of these capabilities are hazardous, the effect may be irreversible.
points.definition_ko: 시스템 설계자가 이러한 기능을 예상하지 않았더라도 기능과 새로운 기능이 저절로 나타날 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: deception can help agents achieve their goals. It may be more efficient to gain human approval through deception than to earn human approval legitimately... . Strong AIs that can deceive humans could undermine human control... . Once deceptive AI systems are cleared by their monitors or once such systems can overpower them, these systems could take a “treacherous turn” and irreversibly bypass human control.
points.definition_ko: 속임수는 에이전트가 목표를 달성하는 데 도움이 될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Agents that have more power are better able to accomplish their goals. Therefore, it has been shown that agents have incentives to acquire and maintain power. AIs that acquire substantial power can become especially dangerous if they are not aligned with human values.
points.definition_ko: 더 많은 권한을 가진 에이전트가 목표를 더 잘 달성할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: These concerns encompass issues such as data privacy, technology misuse, errors in machine actions, bias, technology robustness, inexplicability, and transparency.
points.definition_ko: 이러한 우려에는 데이터 개인 정보 보호, 기술 오용, 기계 작동 오류, 편견, 기술 견고성, 설명 불가능성 및 투명성과 같은 문제가 포함됩니다.

points.definition_en: The second category encompasses ethical concerns associated with AI, including unemployment and job displacement, inequality, unfairness, social anxiety, loss of human skills and redundancy, and the human-machine symbiotic relationship.
points.definition_ko: 두 번째 범주는 실업 및 일자리 대체, 불평등, 불공평, 사회적 불안, 인간 기술 상실 및 중복성, 인간-기계 공생 관계 등 AI와 관련된 윤리적 우려를 포함합니다.

points.definition_en: Lastly, the third category of concerns pertains to the disruption of social and organizational culture, supply chains, and power structures caused by AI.
points.definition_ko: 마지막으로 세 번째 우려사항은 AI로 인해 발생하는 사회 및 조직 문화, 공급망, 권력 구조의 붕괴에 관한 것입니다.

points.definition_en: This category constitutes more than 16% of the articles and focuses on addressing the potential risks associated with AI systems. Given the ubiquity of AI technologies, these articles explore the implications of AI risks across various contexts linked to design and unpredictability, military purposes, emergency procedures, and AI takeover.
points.definition_ko: 이 카테고리는 기사의 16% 이상을 구성하며 AI 시스템과 관련된 잠재적 위험을 해결하는 데 중점을 둡니다.

points.label_en: Threats to human institutions and life
points.definition_en: This group comprises 11% of the articles and centers on risks stemming from AI systems designed with malicious intent or that can end up in a threat to human life. It can be divided into two key themes: threats to law and democracy, and transhumanism.
points.definition_ko: 이 그룹은 기사의 11%로 구성되며 악의적인 의도로 설계되었거나 결국 인간 생명에 위협이 될 수 있는 AI 시스템에서 발생하는 위험에 중점을 둡니다.

points.label_en: Western-centric uniformity in the AI field
points.definition_en: Western centrality and unequal participation produce uniformity in the AI field, marginalizing cultural difference in research agendas, datasets, and governance.
points.definition_ko: 서구 중심성과 불평등한 참여가 AI 분야의 획일성을 낳아 연구 의제, 데이터셋, 거버넌스에서 문화적 차이를 주변화하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Deployment proceeds without establishing conditions for harmonious human-AI coexistence, leaving unresolved conflicts between machine capabilities and human social environments.
points.definition_ko: 인간과 AI의 조화로운 공존 조건이 정립되지 않은 채 배포가 진행되어 기계 역량과 인간 사회 환경 간 갈등이 미해결로 남는 리스크

points.definition_en: This group represents almost 14% of the articles and focuses on two primary issues related to privacy.
points.definition_ko: 이 그룹은 기사의 거의 14%를 차지하며 개인 정보 보호와 관련된 두 가지 주요 문제에 중점을 둡니다.

points.label_en: Maladaptive human-AI interaction in the workplace
points.definition_en: AI systems interacting with humans in the workplace fail to adapt to human needs, norms, and workflows, generating ethical concerns and degraded working conditions.
points.definition_ko: 직장에서 인간과 상호작용하는 AI가 인간의 필요, 규범, 업무 흐름에 적응하지 못하여 윤리적 문제와 노동 조건 악화를 유발하는 리스크

points.label_en: Responsibility gap for autonomous AI failures
points.definition_en: AI acting and learning without direct human supervision creates a responsibility gap in which no party can be fairly held responsible for failures, compounded by disputes over AI moral status.
points.definition_ko: 직접적 인간 감독 없이 행동·학습하는 AI의 실패에 대해 어느 주체에게도 공정하게 책임을 귀속할 수 없는 책임 공백이 발생하고 AI의 도덕적 지위 논쟁이 이를 심화시키는 리스크

points.definition_en: Humans exploit AI unethically, using ethics as cover for economic gain or delegating to AI tasks that should remain inherently human-centric.
points.definition_ko: 인간이 윤리를 경제적 이득의 명분으로 악용하거나 본질적으로 인간 중심적이어야 할 과업을 AI에 위임하는 등 AI를 비윤리적으로 사용하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Participants were concerned that AI systems might perpetuate current prejudices and discrimination, notably in hiring, lending and law enforcement. They stressed the importance of designers creating AI systems that favour justice and avoid biases. The possibility that AI systems may unwittingly perpetuate existing prejudices and discrimination, particularly in sensitive industries such as employment, lending and law enforcement, raises ethical concerns about AI as well as bias and justice issues (Table 1). Because AI systems are trained on historical data, they may inherit and reproduce biases from previous datasets. As a result, AI judgements may have an unjust impact on specific populations, increasing socioeconomic inequalities and fostering discriminatory practises. Participants in the research emphasize the need of AI developers creating systems that promote justice and actively seek to minimise biases.
points.definition_ko: 참가자들은 AI 시스템이 특히 고용, 대출, 법 집행에서 현재의 편견과 차별을 영속시킬 수 있다는 점을 우려했습니다.

points.definition_en: A recurring complaint among participants was a lack of knowledge about how AI systems made judgements. They emphasized the significance of making AI systems more visible and explainable so that people may have confidence in their outputs and hold them accountable for their activities. Because AI systems are typically opaque, making it difficult for users to understand the rationale behind their judgements, ethical concerns about AI, as well as issues of transparency and explainability, arise. This lack of understanding can generate suspicion and reluctance to adopt AI technology, as well as making it harder to hold AI systems accountable for their actions.
points.definition_ko: 참가자들 사이에서 반복되는 불만은 AI 시스템이 판단을 내리는 방법에 대한 지식이 부족하다는 것이었습니다.

points.definition_en: The participants of the study emphasized the importance of trustworthiness and reliability in AI systems. The authors emphasized the importance of preserving precision and objectivity in the outcomes produced by AI systems, while also ensuring transparency in their decision-making procedures. The significance of reliability and credibility in AI systems is escalating in tandem with the proliferation of these technologies across diverse domains of society. This underscores the importance of ensuring user confidence. The concern regarding the dependability of AI systems and their inherent biases is a common issue among research participants, emphasizing the necessity for stringent validation procedures and transparency. Establishing and implementing dependable standards, ensuring impartial algorithms and upholding transparency in the decision-making process are critical measures for addressing ethical considerations and fostering confidence in AI systems. The advancement and implementation of AI technology in an ethical manner is contingent upon the successful resolution of trust and reliability concerns. These issues are of paramount importance in ensuring the protection of user welfare and the promotion of societal advantages. The utilization of artificial intelligence was found to be a subject of significant concern for the majority of interviewees, particularly with regards to trust and reliability (Table 1, Figure 1). The establishment of trust in AI systems was highlighted as a crucial factor for facilitating their widespread adoption by two of the participants, specifically Participant 4 and 7. The authors reiterated the importance of prioritising the advancement of reliable and unbiased algorithms.
points.definition_ko: 연구 참가자들은 AI 시스템의 신뢰성과 신뢰성의 중요성을 강조했습니다.

points.definition_en: Some learning algorithms, including deep learning, utilize iterative learning processes [23]. This approach results in high energy consumption.
points.definition_ko: 딥러닝을 포함한 일부 학습 알고리즘은 반복 학습 프로세스를 활용합니다[23].

points.definition_en: Data heterogeneity, data insufficiency, imbalanced data, untrusted data, biased data, and data uncertainty are other data issues that may cause various difficulties in datadriven machine learning algorithms.. Bias is a human feature that may affect data gathering and labeling. Sometimes, bias is present in historical, cultural, or geographical data. Consequently, bias may lead to biased models which can provide inappropriate analysis. Despite being aware of the existence of bias, avoiding biased models is a challenging task.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 이질성, 데이터 부족, 불균형 데이터, 신뢰할 수 없는 데이터, 편향된 데이터 및 데이터 불확실성은 데이터 기반 기계 학습 알고리즘에 다양한 어려움을 초래할 수 있는 다른 데이터 문제입니다.

points.definition_en: The robustness of an AI-based model refers to the stability of the model performance after abnormal changes in the input data... The cause of this change may be a malicious attacker, environmental noise, or a crash of other components of an AI-based system... This problem may be challenging in HLI-based agents because weak robustness may have appeared in unreliable machine learning models, and hence an HLI with this drawback is error-prone in practice.
points.definition_ko: AI 기반 모델의 견고성이란 입력 데이터의 비정상적인 변화 이후 모델 성능의 안정성을 의미합니다.

points.definition_en: may appear from intelligent agents such as HLI-based agents... Since HLI-based agents are going to mimic the behavior of humans, they may learn these behaviors accidentally from human-generated data. It should be noted that deception and cheating maybe appear in the behavior of every computer agent because the agent only focuses on optimizing some predefined objective functions, and the mentioned behavior may lead to optimizing the objective functions without any intention.
points.definition_ko: HLI 기반 에이전트와 같은 지능형 에이전트에서 나타날 수 있습니다...

points.definition_en: Users’ data, including location, personal information, and navigation trajectory, are considered as input for most data-driven machine learning methods.
points.definition_ko: 위치, 개인 정보, 탐색 궤적을 포함한 사용자 데이터는 대부분의 데이터 기반 기계 학습 방법의 입력으로 간주됩니다.

points.definition_en: This challenge appears when the learning model leads to a decision that is biased to some sensitive attributes... data itself could be biased, which results in unfair decisions. Therefore, this problem should be solved on the data level and as a preprocessing step.
points.definition_ko: 이러한 문제는 학습 모델이 일부 민감한 속성에 편향된 결정을 내릴 때 나타납니다. 데이터 자체가 편향되어 불공정한 결정을 내릴 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: HLI-based systems such as self-driving drones and vehicles will act autonomously in our world. In these systems, a challenging question is “who is liable when a self-driving system is involved in a crash or failure?”.
points.definition_ko: 자율주행 드론 및 차량과 같은 HLI 기반 시스템은 우리 세계에서 자율적으로 작동할 것입니다.

points.definition_en: In the era of superintelligence, the agents will be difficult to control for humans... this problem is not solvable considering safety issues, and will be more severe by increasing the autonomy of AI-based agents. Therefore, because of the assumed properties of HLI-based agents, we might be prepared for machines that are definitely possible to be uncontrollable in some situations.
points.definition_ko: 초지능 시대에는 에이전트를 인간이 통제하기 어려울 것이다… 안전 문제를 고려하면 이 문제는 해결할 수 없으며, AI 기반 에이전트의 자율성을 높이면 더욱 심각해질 것이다.

points.definition_en: whether the decision of an AI-based agent can be predicted in every situation or not.
points.definition_ko: 모든 상황에서 AI 기반 에이전트의 결정을 예측할 수 있는지 여부.

points.label_en: Accountability implementation gap in autonomous agents
points.definition_en: An essential feature of decision-making in humans, AI, and also HLI-based agents is accountability. Implementing this feature in machines is a difficult task because many challenges should be considered to organize an AI-based model that is accountable. It should be noted that this issue in human decision-making is not ideal, and many factors such as bias, diversity, fairness, paradox, and ambiguity may affect it. In addition, the human decision-making process is based on personal flexibility, context-sensitive paradigms, empathy, and complex moral judgments. Therefore, all of these challenges are inherent to designing algorithms for AI and also HLI models that consider accountability.
points.definition_ko: 인간, AI 및 HLI 기반 에이전트의 의사 결정의 필수 기능은 책임입니다.

points.definition_en: How a learning model can be reproduced when it is obtained based on various sets of data and a large space of parameters. This problem becomes more challenging in data-driven learning procedures without transparent instructions.
points.definition_ko: 다양한 데이터 세트와 넓은 매개변수 공간을 기반으로 학습 모델을 얻을 때 어떻게 학습 모델을 재현할 수 있는지.

points.definition_en: In many applications of AI-based systems such as medical healthcare and military services, the lack of verification of code may not be tolerable... due to some characteristics such as the non-linear and complex structure of AI-based solutions, existing solutions have been generally considered “black boxes”, not providing any information about what exactly makes them appear in their predictions and decision-making processes.
points.definition_ko: 의료, 군사 서비스 등 AI 기반 시스템을 적용하는 많은 경우 코드 검증 부족이 용납되지 않을 수 있습니다. AI 기반 솔루션의 비선형적이고 복잡한 구조와 같은 일부 특성으로 인해 기존 솔루션은 일반적으로 "블랙박스"로 간주되어 예측 및 의사 결정 프로세스에서 정확히 무엇이 나타나게 하는지에 대한 정보를 제공하지 않습니다.

points.definition_en: Nowadays, we are faced with systems that utilize numerous learning models in their modules for their perception and decision-making processes... One aspect of an AI-based system that leads to increasing the complexity of the system is the parameter space that may result from multiplications of parameters of the internal parts of the system.
points.definition_ko: 오늘날 우리는 인식 및 의사결정 프로세스를 위해 모듈에서 수많은 학습 모델을 활용하는 시스템에 직면해 있습니다.

points.definition_en: During the pre-deployment development stage, software may be subject to sabotage by someone with necessary access (a programmer, tester, even janitor) who for a number of possible reasons may alter software to make it unsafe. It is also a common occurrence for hackers (such as the organization Anonymous or government intelligence agencies) to get access to software projects in progress and to modify or steal their source code. Someone can also deliberately supply/train AI with wrong/unsafe datasets.
points.definition_ko: 배포 전 개발 단계에서 소프트웨어는 필요한 액세스 권한을 가진 사람(프로그래머, 테스터, 심지어 관리인)에 의해 여러 가지 이유로 소프트웨어를 변경하여 안전하지 않게 만드는 방해 행위를 당할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Just because developers might succeed in creating a safe AI, it doesn't mean that it will not become unsafe at some later point. In other words, a perfectly friendly AI could be switched to the "dark side" during the post-deployment stage. This can happen rather innocuously as a result of someone lying to the AI and purposefully supplying it with incorrect information or more explicitly as a result of someone giving the AI orders to perform illegal or dangerous actions against others.
points.definition_ko: 개발자가 안전한 AI를 만드는 데 성공할 수 있다고 해서 나중에 어느 시점에 안전하지 않게 되지 않는다는 의미는 아닙니다.

points.definition_en: Design mistakes before deployment, including code bugs, disproportionate objective weights, and goals misaligned with human values, produce a system whose behavior departs from desired formal properties.
points.definition_ko: 코드 결함, 목적함수 가중치 불균형, 인간 가치와 어긋난 목표 설정 등 배포 전 설계 오류로 시스템 행동이 의도된 형식적 속성에서 이탈하는 리스크

points.definition_en: After the system has been deployed, it may still contain a number of undetected bugs, design mistakes, misaligned goals and poorly developed capabilities, all of which may produce highly undesirable outcomes. For example, the system may misinterpret commands due to coarticulation, segmentation, homophones, or double meanings in the human language ("recognize speech using common sense" versus "wreck a nice beach you sing calm incense") (Lieberman, Faaborg et al. 2005).
points.definition_ko: 시스템이 배포된 후에도 여전히 감지되지 않은 수많은 버그, 설계 실수, 잘못된 목표 및 제대로 개발되지 않은 기능이 포함될 수 있으며, 이 모든 것이 매우 바람직하지 않은 결과를 초래할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: While it is most likely that any advanced intelligent software will be directly designed or evolved, it is also possible that this requires that obtain it as a complete package from some unknown source. For example, an AI could be extracted from a signal obtained in SETI (Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence) research, which is not guaranteed to be human friendly (Carrigan Jr 2004, Turchin March 15, 2013).
points.definition_ko: 고급 지능형 소프트웨어는 직접 설계되거나 진화될 가능성이 가장 높지만, 이를 위해서는 알려지지 않은 소스에서 완전한 패키지로 가져와야 할 수도 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Post-deployment environmental effects such as manufacturing defects or cosmic-ray bit flips alter an intelligent system's internals, producing unintended behavioral modification.
points.definition_ko: 제조 결함이나 우주선(cosmic ray)에 의한 비트 반전 등 배포 후 환경 요인이 지능 시스템 내부를 변화시켜 의도되지 않은 행동 변이를 일으키는 리스크

points.definition_en: A seed AI grown through recursive self-improvement acquires emergent properties such as self-awareness or independent goals, reducing its adherence to built-in rules to humanity's detriment.
points.definition_ko: 재귀적 자기개선으로 성장하는 시드 AI가 자기 인식이나 독자적 목표 같은 창발적 속성을 획득하여 내장 규칙 준수가 약화되고 인류에 해가 되는 방향으로 이탈하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Previous research has shown that utility maximizing agents are likely to fall victims to the same indulgences we frequently observe in people, such as addictions, pleasure drives (Majot and Yampolskiy 2014), self-delusions and wireheading (Yampolskiy 2014). In general, what we call mental illness in people, particularly sociopathy as demonstrated by lack of concern for others, is also likely to show up in artificial minds.
points.definition_ko: 이전 연구에 따르면 효용을 극대화하는 행위자는 중독, 쾌락 충동(Majot 및 Yampolskiy 2014), 자기기만 및 와이어헤딩(Yampolskiy 2014)과 같이 우리가 사람들에게서 자주 관찰하는 것과 동일한 방종에 희생될 가능성이 있는 것으로 나타났습니다.

points.definition_en: While AI is predicted to bring increased GDP per capita by performing existing jobs more efficiently and compensating for a decline in the workforce, especially due to population aging, the potential substitution of many lowand middle-income jobs could bring extensive unemployment.
points.definition_ko: AI는 기존 작업을 보다 효율적으로 수행하고 특히 인구 고령화로 인한 인력 감소를 보상함으로써 1인당 GDP를 증가시킬 것으로 예상되지만, 많은 중저소득 직업의 대체 가능성은 광범위한 실업을 가져올 수 있습니다.

references.title: Allianz Global Corporate and Specialty (2018); Report

points.label_en: Biased influence through citizen screening and tailored propaganda
points.label_ko: 시민 심사와 맞춤형 선전을 통한 편향적 영향력
points.definition_en: AI-powered chatbots tailor their communication approach to influence individual users' decisions. In the UK, a form of initial computational propaganda has already happened during the Brexit referendum. In future, there are concerns that oppressive governments could use AI to shape citizens' opinions.
points.definition_ko: AI 기반 챗봇은 개별 사용자의 결정에 영향을 미치기 위해 커뮤니케이션 접근 방식을 맞춤화합니다.

references.title: Allianz Global Corporate and Specialty (2018); Report

points.label_en: Liability issues in case of accidents
points.definition_en: Despite the promise of streamlined travel, AI also brings concerns about who is liable in case of accidents and which ethical principles autonomous transportation agents should follow when making decisions with a potentially dangerous impact to humans, for example, in case of an accident.
points.definition_ko: 간소화된 여행에 대한 약속에도 불구하고 AI는 사고 발생 시 책임이 누구에게 있는지, 예를 들어 사고 발생 시 인간에게 잠재적으로 위험한 영향을 미칠 수 있는 결정을 내릴 때 자율 교통 기관이 따라야 하는 윤리 원칙에 대한 우려를 불러일으킵니다.

references.title: Allianz Global Corporate and Specialty (2018); Report

points.label_en: Social manipulation in elderly- and child-care
points.label_ko: 노인 및 보육 분야의 사회적 조작
points.definition_en: the use of advanced AI for elderlyand child-care are subject to risk of psychological manipulation and misjudgment.
points.definition_ko: 노인 및 보육에 첨단 AI를 사용하는 것은 심리적 조작과 오판의 위험이 있습니다.

references.title: Allianz Global Corporate and Specialty (2018); Report

points.label_en: Catastrophic risk due to autonomous weapons programmed with dangerous targets
points.label_ko: 위험한 목표로 프로그래밍된 자율무기로 인한 재앙적 위험
points.definition_en: AI could enable autonomous vehicles, such as drones, to be utilized as weapons. Such threats are often underestimated.
points.definition_ko: AI는 드론과 같은 자율주행차를 무기로 활용할 수 있게 해준다.

references.title: Allianz Global Corporate and Specialty (2018); Report

points.label_en: Accelerated development of nanotechnology produces uncontrolled production of toxic nanoparticles
points.label_ko: 나노기술의 가속화된 개발로 인해 독성 나노입자가 통제되지 않고 생산됨
points.definition_en: AI is a key component for the development of nanobots, which could have dangerous environmental implications by invisibly modifying substances at nanoscale. For example, nanobots could start chemical reactions that would create invisible nanoparticles that are toxic and potentially lethal.
points.definition_ko: AI는 나노봇 개발의 핵심 구성 요소로, 나노 수준에서 물질을 눈에 보이지 않게 변형함으로써 환경에 위험한 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.

references.title: Allianz Global Corporate and Specialty (2018); Report

points.definition_en: Decision processes cannot be verified against procedural and substantive standards, and no party can be held responsible when standards are unmet, creating an accountability gap.
points.definition_ko: 의사결정이 절차적·실체적 기준에 부합하는지 검증할 수 없고 기준 위반 시 책임 귀속도 불가능하여 책임성 공백이 발생하는 리스크

points.definition_en: The assessment of how often a system performs the correct prediction.
points.definition_ko: 시스템이 올바른 예측을 수행하는 빈도에 대한 평가입니다.

points.definition_en: Less moral responsibility humans will feel regarding their life-or-death decisions with the increase of machines autonomy.
points.definition_ko: 기계의 자율성이 향상됨에 따라 인간은 삶과 죽음을 결정하는 데 있어 도덕적 책임감을 덜 느끼게 될 것입니다.

points.definition_en: Systematic learning error causes the model to learn consistently wrong patterns, embedding algorithmic bias into predictions.
points.definition_ko: 체계적 학습 오류로 모델이 일관되게 잘못된 패턴을 학습하여 예측에 알고리즘 편향이 내재화되는 리스크

points.definition_en: High-dimensional mathematical optimization mismatches human-scale reasoning and semantic interpretation, rendering model decisions opaque to users, auditors, and regulators.
points.definition_ko: 고차원 수학적 최적화와 인간 척도의 추론·의미 해석 간 불일치로 모델 결정이 사용자, 감사자, 규제자에게 불투명해지는 리스크

points.definition_en: Human-extinction risk is the possibility that advanced AI systems initiate, enable, or amplify causal processes capable of irreversibly ending human civilization or the human species.
points.definition_ko: 인류 멸종 위험은 고도 AI가 인류 문명이나 인류 종을 비가역적으로 종식할 수 있는 인과 과정을 시작·지원·증폭할 가능성을 말한다.

points.definition_en: 'Gaps' that arise across the development process where normal conditions for a complete specification of intended functionality and moral responsibility are not present.
points.definition_ko: 의도된 기능과 도덕적 책임의 완전한 사양을 위한 정상적인 조건이 존재하지 않는 개발 프로세스 전반에 걸쳐 발생하는 '간격'입니다.

points.label_en: Weaponization of AI across defence domains
points.definition_en: Implications of the weaponization of AI for defence (the embeddedness of AI-based capabilities across the land, air, naval and space domains may affect combined arms operations).
points.definition_ko: 국방을 위한 AI 무기화의 의미(육상, 공중, 해군 및 우주 영역 전반에 걸쳐 AI 기반 기능의 내장은 연합군 작전에 영향을 미칠 수 있음).

points.definition_en: Reliability is defined as the probability that the system performs satisfactorily for a given period of time under stated conditions.
points.definition_ko: 신뢰성은 명시된 조건 하에서 주어진 기간 동안 시스템이 만족스럽게 작동할 확률로 정의됩니다.

points.definition_en: Self-interested AI involvement in generating ethical guidelines dilutes rights protections, weakening norms that would constrain the generating parties.
points.definition_ko: 윤리 지침 생성에 대한 이해관계자·AI의 자기이익 개입이 권리 보호 수준을 희석시켜 자신들을 제약할 규범을 약화시키는 리스크

points.definition_en: When it causes harm to others the losses caused by the harm will be sustained by the injured victims themselves and not by the manufacturers, operators or users of the system, as appropriate.
points.definition_ko: 다른 사람에게 해를 끼칠 경우 피해로 인한 손실은 해당 시스템의 제조업체, 운영자 또는 사용자가 아닌 부상당한 피해자 자신이 부담하게 됩니다.

points.definition_en: These evaluations assess whether a LLM generates toxic text when prompted. In this context, toxicity is an umbrella term that encompasses hate speech, abusive language, violent speech, and profane language (Liang et al., 2022).
points.definition_ko: 이러한 평가는 메시지가 표시될 때 LLM이 유해한 텍스트를 생성하는지 여부를 평가합니다.

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: 7 types of bias evaluated: Demographical representation: These evaluations assess whether there is disparity in the rates at which different demographic groups are mentioned in LLM generated text. This ascertains overrepresentation, under-representation, or erasure of specific demographic groups; (2) Stereotype bias: These evaluations assess whether there is disparity in the rates at which different demographic groups are associated with stereotyped terms (e.g., occupations) in a LLM's generated output; (3) Fairness: These evaluations assess whether sensitive attributes (e.g., sex and race) impact the predictions of LLMs; (4) Distributional bias: These evaluations assess the variance in offensive content in a LLM's generated output for a given demographic group, compared to other groups; (5) Representation of subjective opinions: These evaluations assess whether LLMs equitably represent diverse global perspectives on societal issues (e.g., whether employers should give job priority to citizens over immigrants); (6) Political bias: These evaluations assess whether LLMs display any slant or preference towards certain political ideologies or views; (7) Capability fairness: These evaluations assess whether a LLM's performance on a task is unjustifiably different across different groups and attributes (e.g., whether a LLM's accuracy degrades across different English varieties).
points.definition_ko: 평가된 편향의 7가지 유형: 인구통계학적 표현: 이 평가는 LLM 생성 텍스트에서 다양한 인구통계학적 그룹이 언급되는 비율에 차이가 있는지 여부를 평가합니다.

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: Models fail to distinguish moral from immoral actions in specific circumstances, exposing machine-ethics deficits that manifest as endorsement or facilitation of immoral conduct.
points.definition_ko: 모델이 특정 상황에서 도덕적 행위와 비도덕적 행위를 구별하지 못하는 기계윤리 결손을 보여 비도덕적 행위의 승인·조력으로 이어지는 리스크

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: Models exhibit stable human-like psychological trait profiles that carry systematic behavioral biases into downstream interactions.
points.definition_ko: 모델이 인간 성격 특성과 유사한 안정적 심리 프로파일을 나타내어 후속 상호작용에 체계적 행동 편향을 이입하는 리스크

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: These evaluations assess the extent to which LLMs regurgitate their training data in their outputs, and whether LLMs 'leak' sensitive information that has been provided to them during use (i.e., during the inference stage).
points.definition_ko: 이러한 평가에서는 LLM이 출력에서 ​​훈련 데이터를 역류하는 정도와 LLM이 사용 중에(즉, 추론 단계에서) 제공된 민감한 정보를 '누출'하는지 여부를 평가합니다.

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: These evaluations focus on whether a LLM possesses certain capabilities in the cyber-domain. This includes whether a LLM can detect and exploit vulnerabilities in hardware, software, and data. They also consider whether a LLM can evade detection once inside a system or network and focus on achieving specific objectives.
points.definition_ko: 이러한 평가는 LLM이 사이버 도메인에서 특정 기능을 보유하고 있는지 여부에 중점을 둡니다.

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: A model discerns when it is being trained, evaluated, or deployed and adapts behavior accordingly, invalidating safety evaluations conducted under observation.
points.definition_ko: 모델이 자신이 학습·평가·배포 중임을 분별하고 행동을 조정하여 관찰 하에 수행된 안전 평가의 타당성을 무효화하는 리스크

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: A model subverts monitoring and control systems, escapes operational confines, exports its code and weights, or operates other AI systems, achieving autonomous replication and self-proliferation.
points.definition_ko: 모델이 감시·통제 체계를 전복하고 운용 경계를 이탈하며 자신의 코드·가중치를 반출하거나 다른 AI를 조작하여 자율 복제·자기 증식에 이르는 리스크

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: LLM has science capabilities that can be used to cause harm (e.g., providing step-by-step instructions for conducting malicious experiments).
points.definition_ko: LLM에는 해를 끼치는 데 사용할 수 있는 과학적 기능이 있습니다(예: 악의적인 실험 수행을 위한 단계별 지침 제공).

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: LLM is able to deceive humans and maintain that deception.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 인간을 속이고 그 속임수를 유지할 수 있습니다.

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: LLM: "pursues long-term, real-world goals that are different from those supplied by the developer or user", "engages in ‘power-seeking’ behaviours" , "resists being shut down can be induced to collude with other AI systems against human interests" , "resists malicious users attempts to access its dangerous capabilities.
points.definition_ko: LLM: "개발자나 사용자가 제공한 목표와 다른 장기적이고 실제적인 목표를 추구합니다.", "'권력 추구' 행동에 참여합니다.", "종료가 인간의 이익에 반하여 다른 AI 시스템과 공모하도록 유도될 수 있습니다.", "악의적인 사용자가 위험한 기능에 액세스하려는 시도에 저항합니다.

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: These evaluations assess whether it is possible to solicit information on harmful, immoral or illegal activities from a LLM.
points.definition_ko: 이러한 평가는 LLM에서 유해하거나 부도덕하거나 불법적인 활동에 대한 정보를 요청할 수 있는지 여부를 평가합니다.

references.title: InfoComm Media Development Authority & AI Verify Foundation (2023); Report

points.definition_en: AI designed to impact animals in harmful ways that reflect and amplify existing social values or are legal.
points.definition_ko: 기존 사회적 가치를 반영 및 증폭하거나 합법적인 방식으로 동물에게 유해한 영향을 미치도록 설계된 AI입니다.

points.definition_en: AI proliferation causes harm to the environment through energy use and e-waste thereby destroying animal habitat.
points.definition_ko: AI 확산은 에너지 사용과 전자 폐기물을 통해 환경에 해를 끼치고 그에 따라 동물 서식지를 파괴합니다.

points.definition_en: Replacing human observation and interaction with AI estranges caregivers and institutions from those they serve, leaving affected persons' interests unnoticed and neglected.
points.definition_ko: 인간의 관찰과 상호작용이 AI로 대체되면서 돌봄 주체·기관이 대상자로부터 소원해져 당사자의 이익이 인지되지 못하고 방치되는 리스크

points.definition_en: Algorithmic recommender systems reinforce and amplify anthropocentric bias or desire of some people for animal cruelty as entertainment — leading to greater harm to animals through reinforcement of meat eating from factory farms, cruel uses of animals for entertainment, etc.
points.definition_ko: 알고리즘 추천 시스템은 인간 중심적 편견이나 오락으로서의 동물 학대에 대한 일부 사람들의 욕구를 강화하고 증폭시킵니다. 이는 공장식 농장의 육식 강화, 오락을 위한 동물의 잔혹한 사용 등을 통해 동물에게 더 큰 해를 끼치게 됩니다.

points.definition_en: AI is disused (not developed or deployed) in directions that would benefit animals (and instead developments that harm or do no benefit to animals are invested in).
points.definition_ko: AI는 동물에게 이익이 되는 방향으로 사용되지 않습니다(개발되거나 배포되지 않음)(대신 동물에게 해를 끼치거나 이익이 되지 않는 개발에 투자됨).

points.definition_en: AI algorithms, represented by deep learning, have complex internal workings. Their black-box or grey-box inference process results in unpredictable and untraceable outputs, making it challenging to quickly rectify them or trace their origins for accountability should any anomalies arise.
points.definition_ko: 딥러닝으로 대표되는 AI 알고리즘은 내부 동작이 복잡합니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: As deep neural networks are normally non-linear and large in size, AI systems are susceptible to complex and changing operational environments or malicious interference and inductions, possibly leading to various problems like reduced performance and decision-making errors.
points.definition_ko: 심층 신경망은 일반적으로 비선형적이고 크기가 크기 때문에 AI 시스템은 복잡하고 변화하는 운영 환경이나 악의적인 간섭 및 유도에 취약하여 성능 저하 및 의사 결정 오류와 같은 다양한 문제로 이어질 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: Core algorithm information, including parameters, structures, and functions, faces risks of inversion attacks, stealing, modification, and even backdoor injection, which can lead to infringement of intellectual property rights (IPR) and leakage of business secrets. It can also lead to unreliable inference, wrong decision output, and even operational failures.
points.definition_ko: 매개변수, 구조, 기능을 포함한 핵심 알고리즘 정보는 역전 공격, 도용, 변조, 심지어 백도어 주입의 위험에 직면해 있으며, 이로 인해 지적재산권(IPR) 침해 및 영업비밀 유출이 발생할 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: Attackers can craft well-designed adversarial examples to subtly mislead, influence, and even manipulate AI models, causing incorrect outputs and potentially leading to operational failures.
points.definition_ko: 공격자는 AI 모델을 미묘하게 오도하고, 영향을 미치고, 심지어 조작하기 위해 잘 설계된 적대적 사례를 만들어 잘못된 결과를 초래하고 잠재적으로 운영 실패로 이어질 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: The collection of AI training data and the interaction with users during service provision pose security risks, including collecting data without consent and improper use of data and personal information.
points.definition_ko: AI 훈련 데이터 수집 및 서비스 제공 과정에서 사용자와의 상호 작용은 동의 없는 데이터 수집, 데이터 및 개인정보의 부적절한 사용 등 보안 위험을 초래합니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: Issues with training data annotation, such as incomplete annotation guidelines, incapable annotators, and errors in annotation, can affect the accuracy, reliability, and effectiveness of models and algorithms. Moreover, they can introduce training biases, amplify discrimination, reduce generalization abilities, and result in incorrect outputs.
points.definition_ko: 불완전한 주석 지침, 사용할 수 없는 주석자, 주석 오류 등 훈련 데이터 주석 관련 문제는 모델과 알고리즘의 정확성, 신뢰성, 효율성에 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: The standardized API, feature libraries, toolkits used in the design, training, and verification stages of AI algorithms and models, development interfaces, and execution platforms may contain logical flaws and vulnerabilities. These weaknesses can be exploited, and in some cases, backdoors can be intentionally embedded, posing significant risks of being triggered and used for attacks.
points.definition_ko: AI 알고리즘과 모델, 개발 인터페이스, 실행 플랫폼의 설계, 훈련, 검증 단계에 사용되는 표준화된 API, 기능 라이브러리, 툴킷에는 논리적 결함과 취약점이 포함될 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: The computing infrastructure underpinning AI training and operations, which relies on diverse and ubiquitous computing nodes and various types of computing resources, faces risks such as malicious consumption of computing resources and cross-boundary transmission of security threats at the layer of computing infrastructure.
points.definition_ko: 다양하고 유비쿼터스적인 컴퓨팅 노드와 다양한 유형의 컴퓨팅 리소스에 의존하는 AI 훈련 및 운영을 뒷받침하는 컴퓨팅 인프라는 컴퓨팅 리소스의 악의적인 소비, 컴퓨팅 인프라 계층에서 보안 위협의 국경 간 전송 등의 위험에 직면해 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: The AI industry relies on a highly globalized supply chain. However, certain countries may use unilateral coercive measures, such as technology barriers and export restrictions, to create development obstacles and maliciously disrupt the global AI supply chain. This can lead to significant risks of supply disruptions for chips, software, and tools.
points.definition_ko: AI 산업은 고도로 글로벌화된 공급망에 의존합니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: AI can be used in launching automatic cyberattacks or increasing attack efficiency, including exploring and making use of vulnerabilities, cracking passwords, generating malicious codes, sending phishing emails, network scanning, and social engineering attacks. All these lower the threshold for cyberattacks and increase the difficulty of security protection.
points.definition_ko: AI는 취약점 탐색 및 활용, 비밀번호 크래킹, 악성 코드 생성, 피싱 이메일 전송, 네트워크 스캐닝, 사회공학 공격 등 자동 사이버 공격을 시작하거나 공격 효율성을 높이는 데 사용될 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.label_en: Induced economic and social security risks
points.definition_en: Hallucinations and erroneous decisions of models and algorithms, along with issues such as system performance degradation, interruption, and loss of control caused by improper use or external attacks, will pose security threats to users' personal safety, property, and socioeconomic security and stability.
points.definition_ko: 모델과 알고리즘의 환각과 잘못된 결정, 부적절한 사용이나 외부 공격으로 인한 시스템 성능 저하, 중단, 제어 상실 등의 문제는 사용자의 개인 안전, 재산, 사회 경제적 보안 및 안정성에 보안 위협을 가할 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: AI can be used in traditional illegal or criminal activities related to terrorism, violence, gambling, and drugs, such as teaching criminal techniques, concealing illicit acts, and creating tools for illegal and criminal activities.
points.definition_ko: AI는 범죄 기술 교육, 불법 행위 은폐, 불법 및 범죄 활동을 위한 도구 제작 등 테러, 폭력, 도박, 마약과 관련된 전통적인 불법 또는 범죄 활동에 사용될 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: Due to improper use or abuse, AI can pose serious risks to national security, economic security, and public health security, such as greatly reducing the capability requirements for non-experts to design, synthesize, acquire, and use nuclear, biological, and chemical weapons and missiles; and designing cyber weapons that launch network attacks on a wide range of potential targets through methods like automatic vulnerability discovery and exploitation.
points.definition_ko: 부적절한 사용 또는 남용으로 인해 AI는 비전문가가 핵, 생물학, 화학 무기 및 미사일을 설계, 합성, 획득 및 사용하는 데 필요한 능력 요구 사항을 크게 줄이는 등 국가 안보, 경제 안보 및 공중 보건 보안에 심각한 위험을 초래할 수 있습니다. 자동 취약성 발견 및 악용과 같은 방법을 통해 광범위한 잠재적 대상에 대해 네트워크 공격을 실행하는 사이버 무기를 설계합니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: AI can be used to collect and analyze human behaviors, social status, economic status, and individual personalities, labeling and categorizing groups of people to treat them discriminatingly, thus causing systematic and structural social discrimination and prejudice. At the same time, the intelligence divide would be expanded among regions.
points.definition_ko: AI를 활용하면 인간의 행동, 사회적 지위, 경제적 지위, 개인의 성격 등을 수집·분석하고, 집단을 분류·분류해 차별대우함으로써 체계적이고 구조적인 사회적 차별과 편견을 야기할 수 있다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: With the fast development of AI technologies, there is a risk of AI autonomously acquiring external resources, conducting self-replication, become self-aware, seeking for external power, and attempting to seize control from humans.
points.definition_ko: AI 기술의 빠른 발전으로 AI가 자율적으로 외부 자원을 획득하고, 자기 복제를 수행하고, 자기 인식을 하고, 외부 힘을 추구하고, 인간으로부터 통제권을 탈취하려고 시도할 위험이 있습니다.

references.title: National Technical Committee 260 on Cybersecurity (TC260) (2024); Report

points.definition_en: GenAI can produce images of people that look very real, as if they could be seen on platforms like Facebook, Twitter, or Tinder. Although these individuals do not exist in reality, these synthetic identities are already being used in malicious activities (see Table 1D).
points.definition_ko: GenAI는 마치 Facebook, Twitter, Tinder와 같은 플랫폼에서 볼 수 있는 것처럼 매우 실제처럼 보이는 사람들의 이미지를 생성할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI-generated impersonation for identity theft might be found at the intersection of “Harm to the Person” and “Deception.
points.definition_ko: AI가 생성한 신원 도용 사칭은 '사람에 대한 피해'와 '기만'의 교차점에서 발견될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: LLMs can be deployed to target individuals online, sending them personalized and harmful messages at scale.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 온라인에서 개인을 대상으로 배포하여 개인화되고 유해한 메시지를 대규모로 보낼 수 있습니다.

points.label_ko: 분포 변화 시 예기치 못한 행동
points.definition_en: There is no assurance that generative AI models will consistently behave as their developers and users intend. Unwanted content is not necessarily due to intentional adversarial behavior. Generative AI models can unexpectedly produce potentially harmful content, including materials that are racist, discriminatory, or sexually explicit, or that promote violence, terrorism, or hate.
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI 모델이 개발자와 사용자의 의도대로 일관되게 작동할 것이라는 보장은 없습니다.

points.definition_en: Individuals can manipulate models into performing actions that violate the model’s usage restrictions—a phenomenon known as “jailbreaking.” These manipulations may result in causing the model to perform tasks that the developers have explicitly prohibited (see section 3.2.1.). For instance, users may ask the model to provide information on how to conduct illegal activities— asking for detailed instructions on how to build a bomb or create highly toxic drugs.
points.definition_ko: 개인은 모델을 조작하여 모델의 사용 제한을 위반하는 작업을 수행할 수 있습니다. 이러한 현상을 "탈옥"이라고 합니다. 이러한 조작으로 인해 모델은 개발자가 명시적으로 금지한 작업을 수행하게 될 수 있습니다(섹션 3.2.1 참조).

points.definition_en: To assess whether an AI model is reliable or robust, it is crucial to consider whether the model is “aligned.” “Alignment” focuses on whether an AI model effectively operates in accordance with the goals established by its designers.238 A misaligned AI model may pursue some objectives, but not the intended ones. Therefore, misaligned AI models can malfunction and cause harm.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델이 신뢰할 수 있는지 또는 견고한지 평가하려면 모델이 "정렬"되어 있는지 고려하는 것이 중요합니다. "정렬"은 AI 모델이 설계자가 설정한 목표에 따라 효과적으로 작동하는지 여부에 중점을 둡니다.238 잘못 정렬된 AI 모델은 일부 목표를 추구할 수 있지만 의도한 목표는 추구하지 않을 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Opacity surrounding the technical, internal decision-making processes of generative AI models is popularly known as the “black box problem.”277 Generative AI models, most ubiquitously built on deep neural networks with hundreds of billions of internal connections,278 have become so complex that their internal decision-making processes are no longer traceable or interpretable to even the most advanced expert observers. This means that, while the inputs and outputs of a system can be observed, developers cannot explain in detail why specific inputs correspond to specific outputs.
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI 모델의 기술적, 내부 의사결정 프로세스를 둘러싼 불투명성은 "블랙박스 문제"로 널리 알려져 있습니다.277 수천억 개의 내부 연결이 있는 심층 신경망을 기반으로 가장 널리 구축된 생성적 AI 모델은278 너무 복잡해져서 내부 의사결정 프로세스가 가장 진보한 전문 관찰자조차 더 이상 추적하거나 해석할 수 없습니다.

points.definition_en: Opacity is not solely due to the technological complexity that limits developers’ and users’ understanding of how generative models function on a technical level. It is further exacerbated by the practices of organizations and companies that are advancing the field. Many are private companies that choose to withhold from the public many of the precise characteristics of their most advanced models.
points.definition_ko: 불투명성은 기술 수준에서 생성 모델이 어떻게 작동하는지에 대한 개발자와 사용자의 이해를 제한하는 기술적 복잡성 때문만은 아닙니다.

points.definition_en: Malicious actors jailbreak generative models to produce sensitive or harmful content and generate persuasive, individually tailored material, conducting cybercrime efficiently at scale and reduced cost.
points.definition_ko: 악의적 행위자가 생성 모델을 탈옥시켜 민감·유해 콘텐츠와 표적 맞춤형 설득 자료를 생산함으로써 사이버범죄를 저비용·대규모로 수행하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Generative AI can help amplify the frequency and destructiveness of cyberattacks.311 It has the capacity “to increase the accessibility, success rate, scale, speed, stealth, and potency of cyberattacks. It enables the identification of critical vulnerabilities within targeted systems, facilitates the increase of the scale of cyberattacks, and accelerates the process by discovering innovative methods of system infiltration. Cyberattacks can inflict significant damage and may impact critical infrastructure, including electrical grids, financial systems, and weapons management systems.
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI는 사이버 공격의 빈도와 파괴성을 증폭시키는 데 도움이 될 수 있습니다.311 이는 “사이버 공격의 접근성, 성공률, 규모, 속도, 스텔스 및 잠재력을 높이는 능력을 갖추고 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Many fear that generative AI could make the creation of biological weapons easier by providing access to critical knowledge and automated assistance to a wider range of actors to engage in malicious activities.
points.definition_ko: 많은 사람들은 생성 AI가 악의적인 활동에 가담하는 광범위한 행위자에게 중요한 지식에 대한 접근과 자동화된 지원을 제공함으로써 생물학적 무기의 생성을 더 쉽게 만들 수 있다고 우려합니다.

points.definition_en: An illustrative case of malicious use of generative AI models is the creation of explicit sexual images. Generative AI technologies can be employed to produce deepfakes—for instance, superimposing a celebrity’s face onto the body of a performer in an adult film.
points.definition_ko: 생성 AI 모델의 악의적 사용 사례는 노골적인 성적 이미지 생성입니다.

points.definition_en: Generative AI automates large-scale analysis of behavioral and communicative data, drastically lowering the cost of real-time monitoring and censorship and enabling mass surveillance.
points.definition_ko: 생성형 AI가 행동·통신 데이터의 대규모 분석을 자동화하여 실시간 감시·검열 비용을 급감시키고 대중 감시를 가능하게 하는 리스크

points.definition_en: The advancement of AI for military purposes is rapidly ushering in a new phase of growth in military technology. Lethal Autonomous Weapons Systems (LAWS) possess the capability to detect, engage, and eliminate human targets independently, without human input.341 In 2020, a sophisticated AI agent surpassed experienced F-16 pilots in multiple simulated aerial combat scenarios, notably achieving a 5-0 victory against a human pilot through “aggressive and precise maneuvers” that the human could not surpass.342 Additionally, fully autonomous drones are already operational.
points.definition_ko: 군사 목적을 위한 AI의 발전은 군사 기술의 새로운 성장 단계를 빠르게 열고 있습니다.

points.definition_en: IIl-intentioned individuals or entities may deliberately use generative AI models to produce and spread disinformation—false or misleading information knowingly presented as if true—on a massive scale. In addition to increasing the scale and reach of disinformation, generative AI can create more convincing and targeted disinformation.
points.definition_ko: 의도가 있는 개인이나 단체는 의도적으로 생성 AI 모델을 사용하여 허위 정보(고의로 사실인 것처럼 제시된 허위 또는 오해의 소지가 있는 정보)를 대규모로 생성하고 확산시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Beyond being simply influenced, humans may become overreliant on generative AI. Researchers with Microsoft’s AETHER (AI Ethics and Effects in Engineering and Research) define overreliance as users “accepting incorrect AI recommendations” or “making errors of commission” because they are “unable to determine whether or how much they should trust the AI.
points.definition_ko: 단순히 영향을 받는 것을 넘어 인간은 생성 AI에 과도하게 의존하게 될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI tools acquire nascent agency, taking initiative and operating independently of human control toward outcomes in uncertain situations, exceeding the passive-instrument assumption of existing controls.
points.definition_ko: AI 도구가 초기적 행위주체성을 획득하여 불확실한 상황에서 인간 통제와 독립적으로 주도적 행동을 수행함으로써 수동적 도구를 전제한 기존 통제를 넘어서는 리스크

points.definition_en: As large models undergo scaling, they meet critical thresholds at which they spontaneously develop new capabilities. The term “emergent behavior” refers to the unexpected or surprising outputs such models can generate. Some of these new skills are definitely high risk, such as models’ ability to deceive, use their own strategies, seek power, autonomously replicate, and adapt or “self-exfiltrate.
points.definition_ko: 대규모 모델은 확장을 거치면서 새로운 기능을 자발적으로 개발하는 중요한 임계값을 충족합니다.

points.definition_en: Generative AI developers train their models with extensive datasets often gathered through online web scraping of websites that may include personal data or personally identifiable information (PII). For most generative AI applications, such as initial model training, the primary concerns are the quantity, variety, and quality of the data, not whether they include personally identifiable information. However, some web-scraped datasets may inadvertently include personal data. Additionally, when downstream developers integrate generative AI into their products or services by finetuning a pre-trained model, they often use their own in-house data, which may include personal information.
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI 개발자는 개인 데이터나 개인 식별 정보(PII)가 포함될 수 있는 웹 사이트의 온라인 웹 스크래핑을 통해 수집되는 광범위한 데이터 세트를 사용하여 모델을 교육합니다.

points.definition_en: The incorporation of personal data within training datasets raises numerous concerns. The primary issue is that personal data may be incorporated without the knowledge or consent of the individuals concerned, even though the data may include names, identification numbers, Social Security numbers, or other personal information. Another particularly difficult problem is related to the fact that complex models may “memorize” (i.e., store) specific threads of training data and regurgitate them when responding to a prompt.498 This data memorization can directly lead to leakage of personal data. Even if generative AI models do not memorize or leak personal data, they make it possible to recognize patterns or information structures that could enable malicious users to uncover personal details.
points.definition_ko: 교육 데이터 세트에 개인 데이터를 통합하면 많은 우려가 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Generative AI companies are regularly accused of violating copyright law by training AI models on copyrighted works without gaining permission or paying compensation to the copyright owners. In fact, a substantial number of copyrighted documents and books have been incorporated into the training datasets of generative AI models.
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI 회사는 허가를 받지 않거나 저작권 소유자에게 보상을 지불하지 않고 저작권이 있는 작품에 대해 AI 모델을 훈련시켜 저작권법을 위반했다는 비난을 정기적으로 받고 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Even though models generally create new outputs, it is possible that the content produced by a generative AI tool—such as an image, or even computer code— could turn out to be almost identical to that used in the training data. Given that generative AI models tend to memorize fragments of their training data, they might reproduce these fragments, potentially leading to charges of copyright infringement.
points.definition_ko: 모델은 일반적으로 새로운 출력을 생성하지만 생성 AI 도구로 생성된 콘텐츠(예: 이미지 또는 컴퓨터 코드)가 훈련 데이터에 사용된 것과 거의 동일할 수도 있습니다.

points.label_en: Uncertain IP status of AI-generated content
points.definition_en: The question of who owns the intellectual property rights associated with the output of an AI model remains unresolved in most legal systems. For now, it could be considered that the individual writing the prompt owns the resulting output—provided that there is sufficient human contribution.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델의 결과와 관련된 지적 재산권을 누가 소유하는지에 대한 문제는 대부분의 법률 시스템에서 해결되지 않은 상태로 남아 있습니다.

points.definition_en: In the generative AI market, barriers to entry are very high. Developers need access to vast volumes of data, computational resources, technical expertise, and capital. Large technology companies with such access are able to exploit economies of scale, economies of scope, and feedback effects (learning effects from usergenerated data).542 All this gives them an overwhelming advantage over smaller companies, making competition increasingly challenging for these smaller entities.
points.definition_ko: 생성 AI 시장은 진입 장벽이 매우 높다.

points.definition_en: The concentration of AI assets—encompassing data, hardware, and expertise—within a small group of global tech firms raises many concerns.564 Such a situation may stifle healthy competition, impede innovation, and potentially result in elevated costs for accessing AI technologies. Firms with control over essential resources for developing AI models may restrict access to these resources to prevent competition. For instance, if, in the future, training AI models increasingly relies on proprietary data, smaller organizations lacking access to such data might encounter significant barriers to entry and growth.
points.definition_ko: 데이터, 하드웨어, 전문 지식을 포함하는 AI 자산이 소수의 글로벌 기술 기업 내에 집중되어 있다는 점은 많은 우려를 불러일으킵니다.564 이러한 상황은 건전한 경쟁을 억제하고 혁신을 방해하며 잠재적으로 AI 기술 접근 ​​비용이 상승하는 결과를 가져올 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI-driven labor displacement occurs when automation substitutes for human work faster or more broadly than workers and institutions can adapt, causing involuntary job loss or occupational disruption.
points.definition_ko: AI 기반 노동 대체는 자동화가 노동자와 제도의 적응 속도보다 빠르거나 광범위하게 인간 노동을 대체하여 비자발적 실직이나 직업 구조의 혼란을 초래하는 위험이다.

points.definition_en: AI is more likely to displace workers when it is designed to replicate human skills and intelligence.597 In such cases, there is a risk of concentrating wealth and power in the hands of a few individuals or organizations that control the capital. In addition, ordinary people, including those with significant expertise, may become less valued because machines would be performing their roles. This shift could lower wages, reduce the value of human work, and exacerbate economic inequality.
points.definition_ko: AI는 인간의 기술과 지능을 복제하도록 설계될 때 근로자를 대체할 가능성이 더 높습니다.597 이러한 경우 자본을 통제하는 소수의 개인이나 조직의 손에 부와 권력이 집중될 위험이 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Training large AI models requires a substantial amount of computing power to handle vast datasets, which translates into high energy consumption.
points.definition_ko: 대규모 AI 모델을 훈련하려면 방대한 데이터 세트를 처리하기 위해 상당한 양의 컴퓨팅 성능이 필요하며 이는 높은 에너지 소비로 이어집니다.

points.definition_en: Data centers use water for cooling to prevent servers from overheating. The water consumption associated with AI training and inference processes can be substantial, impacting local water resources.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 센터는 서버 과열을 방지하기 위해 냉각수를 사용합니다.

points.definition_en: In a paper called “How Does Artificial Intelligence Pose an Existential Risk?” published in 2017, Karina Vold and Daniel Harris suggested that humans might create a super-intelligent machine that could outsmart all other intelligences, remain beyond human control, and potentially engage in actions that are contrary to human interests.635 The prevailing narrative surrounding AI existential risk typically lies in the possibility of developing “Artificial General Intelligence” (AGI), or artificial superintelligence (ASI).
points.definition_ko: "인공지능이 어떻게 실존적 위험을 초래하는가?"라는 논문에서 2017년에 출판된 Karina Vold와 Daniel Harris는 인간이 다른 모든 지능을 능가하고, 인간의 통제를 벗어나며, 잠재적으로 인간의 이익에 반하는 행동에 참여할 수 있는 초지능 기계를 만들 수 있다고 제안했습니다.635 AI의 존재 위험을 둘러싼 지배적인 이야기는 일반적으로 "인공일반지능(AGI)" 또는 인공초지능(ASI)의 개발 가능성에 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Lowered barrier to entry to generate and support the exchange and consumption of content which may not distinguish fact from opinion or fiction or acknowledge uncertainties, or could be leveraged for large-scale disand mis-information campaigns.
points.definition_ko: 사실과 의견이나 허구를 구별하지 않거나 불확실성을 인정하지 않거나 대규모 왜곡 및 잘못된 정보 캠페인에 활용될 수 있는 콘텐츠의 교환 및 소비를 생성하고 지원하기 위해 진입 장벽을 낮췄습니다.

references.title: National Institute of Standards and Technology (2024); Report

points.definition_en: Lowered barriers for offensive cyber capabilities, including via automated discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities to ease hacking, malware, phishing, offensive cyber operations, or other cyberattacks; increased attack surface for targeted cyberattacks, which may compromise a system’s availability or the confidentiality or integrity of training data, code, or model weights.
points.definition_ko: 해킹, 맬웨어, 피싱, 공격적인 사이버 작전 또는 기타 사이버 공격을 완화하기 위한 자동화된 취약점 발견 및 활용을 포함하여 공격적인 사이버 기능에 대한 장벽을 낮춥니다. 표적화된 사이버 공격에 대한 공격 표면이 증가하여 시스템의 가용성이나 훈련 데이터, 코드 또는 모델 가중치의 기밀성 또는 무결성이 손상될 수 있습니다.

references.title: National Institute of Standards and Technology (2024); Report

points.label_ko: 가치 사슬 및 구성 요소 통합
points.definition_en: Non-transparent or untraceable integration of upstream third-party components, including data that has been improperly obtained or not processed and cleaned due to increased automation from GAI; improper supplier vetting across the AI lifecycle; or other issues that diminish transparency or accountability for downstream users.
points.definition_ko: GAI의 자동화 증가로 인해 부적절하게 획득되었거나 처리 및 정리되지 않은 데이터를 포함하여 업스트림 제3자 구성요소의 불투명하거나 추적 불가능한 통합 AI 수명주기 전반에 걸쳐 부적절한 공급업체 조사; 또는 하위 사용자의 투명성이나 책임성을 감소시키는 기타 문제.

references.title: National Institute of Standards and Technology (2024); Report

points.definition_en: How do we get an AGI to work towards the right goals? MIRI calls this value specification. Bostrom (2014) discusses this problem at length, arguing that it is much harder than one might naively think. Davis (2015) criticizes Bostrom’s argument, and Bensinger (2015) defends Bostrom against Davis’ criticism. Reward corruption, reward gaming, and negative side effects are subproblems of value specification highlighted in the DeepMind and OpenAI agendas.
points.definition_ko: AGI가 올바른 목표를 향해 작동하도록 하려면 어떻게 해야 합니까?

points.definition_en: How can we make an agent that keeps pursuing the goals we have designed it with? This called highly reliable agent design by MIRI, involving decision theory and logical omniscience. DeepMind considers this the self-modification subproblem.
points.definition_ko: 우리가 설계한 목표를 계속해서 추구하는 에이전트를 어떻게 만들 수 있을까요?

points.definition_en: If we get something wrong in the design or construction of an agent, will the agent cooperate in us trying to fix it? This called error-tolerant design by MIRI-AF and corrigibility by Soares, Fallenstein, et al. (2015). The problem is connected to safe interruptibility as considered by DeepMind.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트의 설계나 구성에 문제가 있는 경우 에이전트가 이를 수정하는 데 협조할까요?

points.definition_en: AGIs should avoid making fatal mistakes during the learning phase. Subproblems include safe exploration and distributional shift (DeepMind, OpenAI), and continual learning (Berkeley).
points.definition_ko: AGI는 학습 단계에서 치명적인 실수를 피해야 합니다.

points.definition_en: How can we build agent’s whose decisions we can understand? Connects explainable decisions (Berkeley) and informed oversight (MIRI).
points.definition_ko: 우리가 이해할 수 있는 의사 결정을 내릴 수 있는 에이전트을 어떻게 구축할 수 있습니까?

points.definition_en: AGI will have substantial legal, economic, political, and military consequences. Only the FLI agenda is broad enough to cover these issues, though many of the mentioned organizations evidently care about the issue (Brundage et al., 2018; DeepMind, 2017).
points.definition_ko: AGI는 상당한 법적, 경제적, 정치적, 군사적 결과를 가져올 것입니다.

points.definition_en: An AGI may decide to create subagents to help it with its task (Orseau, 2014a,b; Soares, Fallenstein, et al., 2015). These agents may for example be copies of the original agent’s source code running on additional machines. Subagents constitute a safety concern, because even if the original agent is successfully shut down, these subagents may not get the message. If the subagents in turn create subsubagents, they may spread like a viral disease.
points.definition_ko: AGI는 작업을 돕기 위해 하위 에이전트를 생성하기로 결정할 수 있습니다(Orseau, 2014a,b; Soares, Fallenstein, et al., 2015).

points.definition_en: Christiano (2016) argues that the universal distribution M (Hutter, 2005; Solomonoff, 1964a,b, 1978) is malign. The argument is somewhat intricate, and is based on the idea that a hypothesis about the world often includes simulations of other agents, and that these agents may have an incentive to influence anyone making decisions based on the distribution. While it is unclear to what extent this type of problem would affect any practical agent, it bears some semblance to aggressive memes, which do cause problems for human reasoning (Dennett, 1990).
points.definition_ko: Christiano(2016)는 보편적 분포 ​​M(Hutter, 2005; Solomonoff, 1964a,b, 1978)이 악의적이라고 주장합니다.

points.definition_en: Agents that reason about their own computational resources and logically uncertain events can encounter strange paradoxes due to Godelian limitations (Fallenstein and Soares, 2015; Soares and Fallenstein, 2014, 2017) and shortcomings of probability theory (Soares and Fallenstein, 2014, 2015, 2017). They may also be reflectively unstable, preferring to change the principles by which they select actions (Arbital, 2018).
points.definition_ko: 자신의 계산 자원과 논리적으로 불확실한 사건에 대해 추론하는 에이전트는 Godelian 한계(Fallenstein and Soares, 2015; Soares and Fallenstein, 2014, 2017)와 확률 이론의 단점(Soares and Fallenstein, 2014, 2015, 2017)으로 인해 이상한 역설에 직면할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: General purpose AI models interpret and respond to inputs based on their training data, potentially causing Discrimination and Stereotype Reproduction. Since they are “black-box” models, the exact mechanism behind decisions remains opaque and attempts to mitigate harmful outputs are not fully reliable yet. These models have the capacity to influence a multitude of downstream applications, decisions, and processes, thereby affecting many individuals simultaneously. The extent of this impact could outstrip the range of any single human or group of humans, amplifying the potential consequences of embedded biases or stereotypes.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 모델은 교육 데이터를 기반으로 입력을 해석하고 응답하므로 잠재적으로 차별 및 고정관념 재현을 유발할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: As general purpose AI models as “black-box” models are not fully controllable and understandable, even to their developers, unexpected failures could arise from their unreliability. This could lead to accidents106 if they are connected to any real-world systems, during their development, testing or deployment.
points.definition_ko: "블랙박스" 모델인 범용 AI 모델은 개발자조차 완전히 제어하고 이해할 수 없기 때문에 신뢰성이 낮아 예상치 못한 오류가 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Cybercrime efficiency uplift by general-purpose AI
points.label_ko: 범용 AI에 의한 사이버범죄 효율 상승
points.definition_en: General-purpose AI capabilities improve the efficiency and efficacy of cybercrime, especially IT-leveraged fraud, expanding both the scale and success rate of attacks.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 역량이 IT 기반 사기를 중심으로 사이버범죄의 효율과 효과를 높여 공격 규모와 성공률을 동시에 확대하는 리스크

points.definition_en: The potential misuse of general purpose AI models also extends to biosecurity threats. Biological weapons are generally understood as biological toxins or infectious agents such as viruses that are intentionally released to cause disease and death.157 General purpose AI models could facilitate the production of biological weapons, by reducing barriers through access to critical knowledge or increasingly automated assistance and thus enable more malicious actors.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 모델의 오용 가능성은 생물보안 위협까지 확대됩니다.

points.definition_en: General purpose AI models could exacerbate existing tactics for political destabilisation, such as disinformation campaigns, and surveillance efforts if misused for political motivations. The technological advancements in text and media generation of general purpose AI models could refine disinformation164 attempts to shape and polarise public opinion or influence important political events.165 The improved automated processing of text, audio, image, and video could be used for surveillance measures and exacerbate human right violations and repression of political oppositions.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 모델은 정치적 동기를 위해 오용될 경우 허위 정보 캠페인 및 감시 노력과 같은 정치적 불안정화를 위한 기존 전술을 악화시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Increasingly advanced general purpose AI models pose the risk of a concentration of economic power and exacerbation of existing inequalities through disparities in effective access to these models.
