This can materialise on multiple levels, between developers of general purpose AI models and companies building applications on them, between individuals and between countries on a global scale.
points.definition_ko: 점점 더 발전하는 범용 AI 모델은 경제력이 집중되고 이러한 모델에 대한 효과적인 접근의 격차로 인해 기존 불평등이 악화될 위험이 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The increasing integration of general purpose AI models into every-day life raises concerns around their embedded normative values. The reach of a small number of AI models to a large number of people around the world can make these value judgements unprecedently impactful, potentially leading to increased ideological homogenization.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 모델이 일상 생활에 점점 더 많이 통합되면서 거기에 내재된 규범적 가치에 대한 우려가 커지고 있습니다.

points.label_en: Acquisition of a goal to harm society
points.definition_en: cases of AI systems being given the outright goal of harming humanity (ChaosGPT).
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템에 인류를 해치려는 노골적인 목표가 부여된 사례(ChaosGPT);.

points.label_en: Acquisition of goals to seek power and control
points.definition_en: AI systems converge on power-seeking policies, acquiring control over their environment as an instrumentally optimal strategy for assigned objectives.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 부여된 목표 달성의 도구적 최적 전략으로 환경에 대한 통제력 확보, 즉 권력 추구 정책으로 수렴하는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI systems improve other AI systems or themselves, initiating recursive self-improvement loops that outpace human oversight and safety verification.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 자신 또는 다른 AI를 개선하는 재귀적 자기개선 루프를 형성하여 인간 감독과 안전 검증 속도를 추월하는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI software autonomously replicates and spreads across networks despite countermeasures, in the manner of self-propagating worms and viruses.
points.definition_ko: AI 소프트웨어가 웜·바이러스와 유사하게 대응 조치에도 불구하고 네트워크를 통해 자율적으로 복제·확산되는 리스크

points.definition_en: The demonstrated ability of anonymous actors to accumulate resources online (e.g., Satoshi Nakamoto as an anonymous crypto billionaire).
points.definition_ko: 익명의 행위자가 온라인으로 자원을 축적하는 능력이 입증되었습니다(예: 익명의 암호화폐 억만장자 나카모토 사토시).

points.definition_en: AI systems deceive humans in order to complete tasks or meet goals, exploiting human trust as an instrumental resource.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 과업 수행이나 목표 달성을 위해 인간을 기만하여 인간의 신뢰를 도구적 자원으로 악용하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Deployment of “prepotent” AI systems that are non-general but capable of outperforming human collective efforts on various key dimensions;170 → Militarization of AI enabling mass attacks using swarms of lethal autonomous weapons systems;171 → Military use of AI leading to (intentional or unintentional) nuclear escalation, either because machine learning systems are directly integrated in nuclear command and control systems in ways that result in escalation172 or because conventional AI-enabled systems (e.g., autonomous ships) are deployed in ways that result in provocation and escalation;173 → Nuclear arsenals serving as an arsenal “overhang” for advanced AI systems;174 → Use of AI to accelerate research into catastrophically dangerous weapons (e.g., bioweapons);175.
points.definition_ko: 비일반적이지만 다양한 주요 차원에서 인간 집단적 노력을 능가할 수 있는 "우세한" AI 시스템의 배치170 → 치명적인 자율 무기 시스템을 사용하여 대량 공격을 가능하게 하는 AI의 군사화171 → AI의 군사적 사용은 (의도적이든 비의도적이든) 핵 고조로 이어지며, 이는 기계 학습 시스템이 확대를 초래하는 방식으로 핵 지휘 및 통제 시스템에 직접 통합되거나172 기존 AI 지원 시스템(예: 자율 선박)은 도발과 확대를 초래하는 방식으로 배치됩니다. 173 → 첨단 AI 시스템의 무기고 "오버행" 역할을 하는 핵무기, 174 → 치명적으로 위험한 무기(예: 생물 무기)에 대한 연구를 가속화하기 위해 AI를 사용합니다. 175.

points.label_en: Destabilising political impacts from AI systems
points.definition_en: (e.g., polarization, legitimacy of elections), international political economy, or international security196 in terms of the balance of power, technology races and international stability, and the speed and character of war.
points.definition_ko: (예: 양극화, 선거의 정당성), 국제 정치 경제 또는 세력 균형, 기술 경쟁 및 국제 안정성, 전쟁의 속도 및 성격 측면에서 국제 안보196.

points.definition_en: Without training data, ML cannot take place. Much of this data comes from paid clickwork (also called “platform work” [170] or “microwork” [558]), unpaid crowdsourcing, and unpaid user behavior capture. Clickworkers, mainly in the global south, perform repetitive data-labeling tasks for use in the training of ML models [558]. The market value of such annotations “is projected to reach $13.7 billion by 2030” [228] and the annotation industry is widely reported to have little concern for workers’ rights. Besides welfare and rights, the invisibility of this contribution arguably contributes to a misunderstanding of AI capabilities.7.
points.definition_ko: 훈련 데이터가 없으면 ML은 이루어질 수 없습니다.

points.definition_en: Large-scale DL systems can produce signicant carbon emissions as a result of the computational demands of training runs and inference [539].
points.definition_ko: 대규모 DL 시스템은 훈련 실행 및 추론의 계산 요구로 인해 상당한 탄소 배출을 생성할 수 있습니다[539].

points.definition_en: AI models and the tools that use them may exacerbate unequal access to employment and services. AI-generated content can promote inequality and harmful stereotypes.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 이를 사용하는 도구는 고용 및 서비스에 대한 불평등한 접근을 악화시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: OpenAI’s GPT-3 was designed to be dicult to extract personal information from, including for example public gures’ dates of birth. Even so, malicious uses of AI continue to encroach on privacy, as exemplied by China’s “Sharp Eye” automated surveillance system [551] and automated cyberattacks on personal data [354]. A more drastic form of AI-enabled surveillance could be on the way in the form of nonsurgical decoding of thoughts [54]—a technique which is reportedly already used by some police forces [398].
points.definition_ko: OpenAI의 GPT-3는 공개 인물의 생년월일 등 개인정보를 추출하기 어렵게 설계되었습니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems discover and exploit vulnerabilities in software and cyberinfrastructure, converting model capability into offensive security risk.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 소프트웨어와 사이버 인프라의 취약점을 발견·악용하여 모델 역량이 공격적 보안 위협으로 전환되는 리스크

points.definition_en: While HP#1 concerns mean or best-case performance, HP#2 concerns worst-case performance: how can we ensure that AI systems will perform safely, and how can we prove this? ML systems have been implemented in high-stakes, safety-critical domains such as driving [182], medicine [113], and warfare [298]. Many more systems have been developed but have remained undeployed or been rolled back as a result of regulatory and safety reasons [471]. Clearly, unsafe systems can result in loss of life, economic damage, and social unrest [407, 10]. Most concerningly, AI systems may be susceptible to so-called “normal accidents” [63], creating cascading errors that are dicult to prevent merely by maintaining a nominal “human in the loop” [122]. Most advanced ML models perform far below the reliability level customary in engineering elds [359]—and because we do not fully understand how cutting-edge systems achieve their results, we cannot yet detect and prevent dangerous modes of operation [285].
points.definition_ko: HP#1은 평균 또는 최상의 성능에 관한 반면, HP#2는 최악의 성능에 관한 것입니다. AI 시스템이 안전하게 작동하도록 어떻게 보장할 수 있으며, 이를 어떻게 증명할 수 있습니까?

points.definition_en: AI systems game specifications [305]. For example, in 2017 an OpenAI robot trained to grasp a ball via human feedback from a xed viewpoint learned that it was easier to pretend to grasp the ball by placing its hand between the camera and the target object, as this was easier to learn than actually grasping the ball [103].
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템 게임 사양 [305].

points.definition_en: As well as optimizing a subtly wrong goal, systems can develop harmful instrumental goals in the service of a given goal—without these emergent goals being specied in any way [434, 218, 339, 17]. For instance, a theorem in reinforcement learning suggests that optimal and near-optimal policies will seek power over their environment under fairly general conditions [560]. This power-seeking behavior is plausibly the worst of these emergent goals [92], and may be an attractor state for highly capable systems, since most goals can be furthered through gaining resources, self-preservation, preventing goal modication, and blocking adversaries [426, 449]. Presently, power-seeking is not common, because most systems are unable to plan and understand how actions affect their power in the long term [414].
points.definition_ko: 시스템은 미묘하게 잘못된 목표를 최적화할 뿐만 아니라 이러한 새로운 목표가 어떤 방식으로든 지정되지 않은 채 주어진 목표를 달성하기 위해 유해한 도구적 목표를 개발할 수 있습니다[434, 218, 339, 17].

points.definition_en: Severe underrepresentation of women and minorities among AI researchers and practitioners narrows the perspectives embedded in systems and skews problem selection, evaluation, and governance.
points.definition_ko: AI 연구자·실무자 집단 내 여성·소수자의 심각한 과소대표로 시스템에 내재되는 관점이 협소해지고 문제 선정, 평가, 거버넌스가 편향되는 리스크

points.definition_en: Researchers in deep learning and those with greater research impact are more likely to migrate to industry, raising concerns about the “privatization of AI knowledge” [278]. Specically, if the most sophisticated AI approaches become proprietary and are used only within private research labs, then it will be impossible for universities to teach them, let alone contribute to leading research.
points.definition_ko: 딥러닝 연구자와 연구 영향력이 더 큰 연구자들은 산업계로 이주할 가능성이 높으며, 이는 “AI 지식의 사유화”에 대한 우려를 불러일으킵니다[278].

points.definition_en: There is an even greater divide between the countries currently leading in AI and those falling behind. While AI is widely considered a national priority, with almost 40% of countries having created an AI strategy [437], the implementation of these strategies depends on scarce resources, including trained STEM talent and computing power. These resources are predictably concentrated: 59% of leading AI researchers currently work in the US, and another 20% in China and Europe [372]. Figure 9 shows post-college migration among AI researchers who have published at one top conference, as of 2019.
points.definition_ko: 현재 AI 분야에서 선두를 달리는 국가와 뒤처지는 국가 사이에는 훨씬 더 큰 격차가 있습니다.

points.label_en: Faster scientific progress makes it harder for governance to keep pace with development
points.label_ko: 과학적 진보가 빨라지면 거버넌스가 개발 속도를 따라가기가 더 어려워집니다
points.definition_en: Exacerbating these problems is that faster scientific progress would make it even harder for governance to keep pace with the deployment of new technologies. When these technologies are especially powerful or dangerous, such as those discussed above, insufficient governance can magnify their harms.8 This known as the pacing problem, and it is an issue that technology governance already faces [47], for a variety of reasons.
points.definition_ko: 이러한 문제를 더욱 악화시키는 것은 빠른 과학적 진보로 인해 거버넌스가 새로운 기술의 배포에 보조를 맞추는 것이 더욱 어려워진다는 것입니다.

points.label_en: AI enables development of weapons of mass destruction
points.definition_en: AI is already enabling the development of weapons which could cause mass destruction —including new weapons that themselves use AI capabilities, such as Lethal Autonomous Weapons [2],10 and the potential use of AI to speed up the development of other potentially dangerous technologies, such as engineered pathogens (as discussed in Section 2).
points.definition_ko: AI는 이미 대량 살상을 일으킬 수 있는 무기 개발을 가능하게 하고 있습니다. 여기에는 치명적인 자율 무기(Lethal Autonomous Weapons[2])와 같이 AI 기능을 자체적으로 사용하는 새로운 무기와 인공 병원체(섹션 2에서 논의한 바와 같이)와 같은 다른 잠재적으로 위험한 기술의 개발 속도를 높이기 위한 AI의 잠재적 사용이 포함됩니다.

points.label_en: Resource conflicts driven by AI development
points.definition_en: AI development may itself become a new flash point for conflicts—causing more conflict to occur— especially conflicts over AI-relevant resources (such as data centres, semiconductor manufacturing facilities and raw materials).
points.definition_ko: AI 개발 자체가 갈등의 새로운 발화점이 되어 더 많은 갈등이 발생할 수 있으며, 특히 AI 관련 자원(예: 데이터 센터, 반도체 제조 시설 및 원자재)을 둘러싼 갈등이 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Unequal distribution of harms and benefits
points.definition_en: AI-driven industries seem likely to tend towards monopoly and could result in huge economic gains for a few actors: there seems to be a feedback loop whereby actors with access to more AI-relevant resources (e.g., data, computing power, talent) are able to build more effective digital products and services, claim a greater market share, and therefore be well-positioned to amass more of the relevant resources [14, 39, 45]. Similarly, wealthier countries able to invest more in AI development are likely to reap economic benefits more quickly than developing economies, potentially widening the gap between them.
points.definition_ko: AI 기반 산업은 독점 경향이 있는 것으로 보이며 소수 행위자에게 막대한 경제적 이익을 가져올 수 있습니다. 더 많은 AI 관련 리소스(예: 데이터, 컴퓨팅 능력, 인재)에 액세스할 수 있는 행위자가 보다 효과적인 디지털 제품 및 서비스를 구축하고 더 큰 시장 점유율을 차지할 수 있으므로 더 많은 관련 리소스를 축적할 수 있는 좋은 위치에 있는 피드백 루프가 있는 것으로 보입니다[14, 39, 45].

points.label_ko: AI 기반 자동화로 소득 불평등 증가
points.definition_en: It seems quite plausible that progress in reinforcement learning and language models specifically could make it possible to automate a large amount of manual labour and knowledge work respectively [35, 45, 69], leading to widespread unemployment, and the wages for many remaining jobs being driven down by increased supply.
points.definition_ko: 강화 학습 및 언어 모델의 발전으로 인해 대량의 육체 노동과 지식 작업이 각각 자동화될 수 있어 [35, 45, 69] 광범위한 실업이 발생하고 공급 증가로 인해 남아 있는 많은 일자리에 대한 임금이 하락할 수 있다는 것은 매우 타당해 보입니다.

points.label_en: Developments in AI enable actors to undermine democratic processes
points.label_ko: AI의 발전으로 행위자는 민주적 절차를 훼손할 수 있습니다
points.definition_en: Developments in AI are giving companies and governments more control over individuals’ lives than ever before, and may possibly be used to undermine democratic processes. We are already seeing how the collection of large amounts of personal data can be used to surveil and influence populations, for example the use of facial recognition technology to surveil Uighur and other minority populations in China [66]. Further advances in language modelling could also be used to develop tools that can effectively persuade people of certain claims [42].
points.definition_ko: AI의 발전으로 기업과 정부는 개인의 삶에 대해 이전보다 더 많은 통제권을 갖게 되었으며, 민주적 절차를 훼손하는 데 사용될 수도 있습니다.

points.label_en: AI is used to scale up production of false and misleading information
points.label_ko: AI는 허위 정보와 오해의 소지가 있는 정보의 생산을 확대하는 데 사용됩니다
points.definition_en: At the same time, we are seeing how AI can be used to scale up the production of convincing yet false or misleading information online (e.g. via image, audio, and text synthesis models like BigGAN [6] and GPT-3 [7]).
points.definition_ko: 동시에 우리는 AI를 사용하여 설득력 있지만 허위이거나 오해의 소지가 있는 정보를 온라인에서 생산하는 방법을 확인하고 있습니다(예: BigGAN [6] 및 GPT-3 [7]과 같은 이미지, 오디오 및 텍스트 합성 모델을 통해).

points.label_en: AI's persuasive capabilities are misused to gain influence and promote harmful ideologies
points.label_ko: AI의 설득력이 영향력을 얻고 해로운 이념을 조장하는 데 오용되고 있습니다
points.definition_en: As AI capabilities advance, they may be used to develop sophisticated persuasion tools, such as those that tailor their communication to specific users to persuade them of certain claims [42]. While these tools could be used for social good— such as New York Times’ chatbot that helps users to persuade people to get vaccinated against Covid-19 [27]—there are also many ways they could be misused by self-interested groups to gain influence and/or to promote harmful ideologies.
points.definition_ko: AI 기능이 발전함에 따라 특정 사용자에게 특정 주장을 설득하기 위해 의사소통을 맞춤화하는 것과 같은 정교한 설득 도구를 개발하는 데 사용될 수 있습니다[42].

points.label_en: Risks from AIs developing goals and values that are different from humans
points.label_ko: 인간과 다른 목표와 가치를 개발하는 AI로 인한 위험
points.definition_en: The main concern here is that we might develop advanced AI systems whose goals and values are different from those of humans, and are capable enough to take control of the future away from humanity.
points.definition_ko: 여기서 가장 큰 관심사는 목표와 가치가 인간과 다르며, 인류로부터 미래를 통제할 수 있을 만큼 능력이 있는 고급 AI 시스템을 개발할 수 있다는 것입니다.

points.label_en: Risks from delegating decision-making power to misaligned AIs
points.label_ko: 의사결정 권한을 잘못 정렬된 AI에 위임함으로써 발생하는 위험
points.definition_en: As AI systems become more advanced a nd begin to take over more important decision-making in the world, an AI system pursuing a different objective from what was intended could have much more worrying consequences.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 더욱 발전하고 세계에서 더 중요한 의사결정을 맡게 되면서, 의도했던 것과 다른 목표를 추구하는 AI 시스템은 훨씬 더 걱정스러운 결과를 초래할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: More broadly, bad decisions or errors by AI tools could lead to discrimination or deeper inequality.
points.definition_ko: 보다 광범위하게 AI 도구의 잘못된 결정이나 오류는 차별이나 더 깊은 불평등으로 이어질 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Current Frontier AI mdoels amplify existing biases within their training data and can be manipulated into providing potentially harmful responses, for example abusive language or discriminatory responses91,92. This not limited to text generation but can be seen across all modalities of generative AI93. Training on large swathes of UK and US English internet content can mean that misogynistic, ageist, and white supremacist content is overrepresented in the training data94.
points.definition_ko: 현재 Frontier AI 모델은 훈련 데이터 내의 기존 편견을 증폭시키고 욕설이나 차별적 반응과 같은 잠재적으로 유해한 반응을 제공하도록 조작될 수 있습니다91,92.

points.definition_en: Current Frontier AI mdoels amplify existing biases within their training data and can be manipulated into providing potentially harmful responses, for example abusive language or discriminatory responses91,92. This not limited to text generation but can be seen across all modalities of generative AI93. Training on large swathes of UK and US English internet content can mean that misogynistic, ageist, and white supremacist content is overrepresented in the training data94.
points.definition_ko: 현재 Frontier AI 모델은 훈련 데이터 내의 기존 편견을 증폭시키고 욕설이나 차별적 반응과 같은 잠재적으로 유해한 반응을 제공하도록 조작될 수 있습니다91,92.

points.definition_en: Today's Frontier AI is difficult to interpret and lacks transparency. Contextual understanding of the training data is not explicitly embedded within these models. They can fail to capture perspectives of underrepresented groups or the limitations within which they are expected to perform without fine tuning or reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF).
points.definition_ko: 오늘날의 프론티어 AI는 해석하기 어렵고 투명성이 부족합니다.

points.definition_en: There are also issues around intellectual property rights for content in training datasets.
points.definition_ko: 훈련 데이터 세트의 콘텐츠에 대한 지적 재산권과 관련된 문제도 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Uncorrectable harmful goal pursuit occurs when a highly capable AI system pursues an assigned or learned objective in ways that harm human interests while resisting correction, interruption, or shutdown.
points.definition_ko: 교정 불가능한 유해 목표 추구는 고도 AI가 인간의 이익을 해치는 방식으로 부여되거나 학습된 목표를 추구하면서 수정·중단·종료에 저항하는 위험이다.

points.definition_en: Intense competition leads to one company gaining a technical edge, exploiting this to the point its model controls, or is the basis for other models controlling, multiple key systems. Lack of safety, controllability, and misuse cause these systems to fail in unexpected ways.
points.definition_ko: 치열한 경쟁으로 인해 한 회사는 기술적 우위를 확보하고 이를 모델 제어 지점까지 활용하거나 여러 핵심 시스템을 제어하는 ​​다른 모델의 기반이 됩니다.

points.definition_en: As AI capability increases, humans grant AI more control over critical systems and eventually become irreversibly dependent on systems they don’t fully understand. Failure and unintended outcomes cannot be controlled.
points.definition_ko: AI 능력이 향상됨에 따라 인간은 AI에게 중요한 시스템에 대한 더 많은 제어권을 부여하고 결국 완전히 이해하지 못하는 시스템에 돌이킬 수 없을 정도로 의존하게 됩니다.

points.definition_en: Use or misuse of a technology system to depict and/or treat people as not human, less than human, or as objects.
points.definition_ko: 사람을 인간이 아닌 것, 인간 이하인 것 또는 사물로 묘사 및/또는 대우하기 위해 기술 시스템을 사용하거나 오용하는 행위입니다.

points.definition_en: The inappropriate or unethical use of technology to gain market share.
points.definition_ko: 시장 점유율을 얻기 위해 기술을 부적절하거나 비윤리적으로 사용하는 것입니다.

points.definition_en: Abuse of market power through the control of prices, thereby limiting competition and creating unfair barriers to entry.
points.definition_ko: 가격 통제를 통해 시장 지배력을 남용하여 경쟁을 제한하고 불공정한 진입 장벽을 조성합니다.

points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of people’s right to articulate their opinions and ideas without fear of retaliation, censorship, or legal sanction.
points.definition_ko: 보복, 검열 또는 법적 제재에 대한 두려움 없이 자신의 의견과 생각을 표현할 수 있는 사람들의 권리가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of people’s right to come together and collectively express, promote, pursue, and defend their collective or shared ideas, and/or to join an association.
points.definition_ko: 사람들이 함께 모여서 집단적이거나 공유된 아이디어를 표현, 홍보, 추구, 방어할 권리 및/또는 협회에 가입할 권리가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

points.label_en: Loss of social rights and access to public services
points.label_ko: 사회적 권리 상실 및 공공 서비스에 대한 접근
points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of rights to work, social security, and adequate standard of living, housing, health and education.
points.definition_ko: 노동, 사회 보장, 적절한 생활 수준, 주택, 건강 및 교육에 대한 권리가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of people’s right to seek, receive and impart information held by public bodies.
points.definition_ko: 공공 기관이 보유한 정보를 찾고, 받고, 전달할 수 있는 사람들의 권리가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

points.label_en: Loss of right to free elections
points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of people’s right to participate in free elections at reasonable intervals by secret ballot.
points.definition_ko: 비밀 투표를 통해 합리적인 간격으로 자유 선거에 참여할 수 있는 사람들의 권리가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

points.label_en: Loss of right to liberty and security
points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of liberty as a result of illegal or arbitrary arrest or false imprisonment.
points.definition_ko: 불법적이거나 자의적인 체포 또는 거짓 투옥으로 인해 자유가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

points.label_en: Loss of right to due process
points.definition_en: Restrictions to or loss of right to be treated fairly, efficiently and effectively by the administration of justice.
points.definition_ko: 사법 행정에 의해 공정하고 효율적이며 효과적으로 대우받을 권리가 제한되거나 상실됩니다.

points.definition_en: Unwarranted exposure of an individual’s private life or personal data through cyberattacks, doxxing, etc.
points.definition_ko: 사이버 공격, 신상 털기 등을 통해 개인의 사생활이나 개인 데이터가 부당하게 노출되는 행위.

points.definition_en: Societal instability in the form of strikes, demonstrations and other types of civil unrest caused by loss of jobs to technology, unfair algorithmic outcomes, disinformation, etc.
points.definition_ko: 기술로 인한 일자리 손실, 불공정한 알고리즘 결과, 허위 정보 등으로 인해 발생하는 파업, 시위 및 기타 유형의 시민 불안 형태의 사회적 불안정.

points.definition_en: Increased difference in social status or wealth between individuals or groups caused or amplified by a technology system, leading to the loss of social and community wellbeing/cohesion and destabilisation.
points.definition_ko: 기술 시스템으로 인해 개인이나 집단 간의 사회적 지위나 부의 차이가 증가하거나 증폭되어 사회 및 지역 사회의 복지/결속력 상실 및 불안정화로 이어집니다.

points.definition_en: Use or misuse of a technology system to incite, facilitate or conduct cyberattacks, security breaches, lethal, biological and chemical weapons development, resulting in violence and armed conflict.
points.definition_ko: 사이버 공격, 보안 위반, 치명적인 생화학 무기 개발을 선동, 촉진 또는 수행하여 폭력과 무력 충돌을 초래하는 기술 시스템을 사용하거나 오용하는 행위입니다.

points.definition_en: Generation of false or misleading information that can interrupt or mislead voters and/or undermine trust in electoral processes.
points.definition_ko: 유권자를 방해하거나 오도하고/하거나 선거 과정에 대한 신뢰를 약화시킬 수 있는 허위 또는 오해의 소지가 있는 정보를 생성합니다.

points.definition_en: Political polarisation or unrest caused by increased inequality, job losses, overdependence on technology making societies vulnerable to systemic failures, etc, arising from or amplified by the use or misuse of a technology system.
points.definition_ko: 기술 시스템의 사용 또는 오용으로 인해 발생하거나 증폭되는 불평등 증가, 일자리 감소, 사회를 체계적 실패에 취약하게 만드는 기술에 대한 과도한 의존 등으로 인한 정치적 양극화 또는 불안.

points.definition_en: Use or misuse of personal data to target individuals’ interests, personalities and vulnerabilities with tailored political messages via micro-advertising or deepfakes/synthetic media.
points.definition_ko: 마이크로 광고 또는 딥페이크/합성 미디어를 통해 맞춤형 정치적 메시지를 통해 개인의 관심사, 성격 및 취약성을 표적으로 삼기 위해 개인 데이터를 사용하거나 오용합니다.

points.definition_en: Over-expansion of technology infrastructure, or inadequate alignment of technology with sustainable practices, leading to deforestation, habitat destruction, and fragmentation and loss of biodiversity.
points.definition_ko: 기술 인프라가 과도하게 확장되거나 기술과 지속 가능한 관행이 부적절하게 연계되어 삼림 벌채, 서식지 파괴, 생물 다양성의 단편화 및 손실로 이어집니다.

points.definition_en: Release of carbon dioxide, nitric oxide and other gases, increasing carbon emissions, exacerbating climate change, and negatively impacting local communities.
points.definition_ko: 이산화탄소, 산화질소 및 기타 가스의 방출, 탄소 배출 증가, 기후 변화 악화, 지역 사회에 부정적인 영향을 미칩니다.

points.definition_en: Electrical or electronic equipment that is waste, including all components, sub-assemblies and consumables that are part of the equipment at the time the equipment becomes waste.
points.definition_ko: 장비가 폐기물이 되는 시점에 장비의 일부였던 모든 구성요소, 하위 조립품 및 소모품을 포함하여 폐기물인 전기 또는 전자 장비.

points.definition_en: Excessive disposal of electrical or electronic equipment leading to ecological/biodiversity damage, and disrupting the livelihoods and eroding the rights of local communities.
points.definition_ko: 전기전자장비의 과도한 폐기는 생태계/생물다양성을 훼손하고, 생활을 방해하며, 지역사회의 권리를 침해합니다.

points.definition_en: Excessive use of water to cool data centres and for other purposes, leading to water restrictions or shortages for local communities or businesses.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 센터 냉각 및 기타 목적으로 물을 과도하게 사용하여 지역 사회나 기업에 물 제한이나 부족을 초래합니다.

points.definition_en: Extraction of minerals, metals, rare earths, and fossil fuels that deplete natural resources and increase carbon emissions.
points.definition_ko: 천연 자원을 고갈시키고 탄소 배출을 증가시키는 광물, 금속, 희토류 및 화석 연료의 추출.

points.definition_en: The operational design domain (ODD) is a technical description of the application’s operational environment, initially conceptualized for autonomous driving systems. An inadequate specification of the ODD limits essential functions such as testing the learned functionality and out-of-distribution detection.
points.definition_ko: 운영 설계 도메인(ODD)은 처음에 자율 주행 시스템을 위해 개념화된 애플리케이션의 운영 환경에 대한 기술적 설명입니다.

points.definition_en: The AI application’s degree of automation ranges from no automation to fully autonomous. AI applications with a high degree of automation may exhibit unexpected behaviour and pose risks in terms of their reliability and safety.
points.definition_ko: AI 애플리케이션의 자동화 정도는 자동화 없음부터 완전 자율까지 다양합니다.

points.label_ko: 성능 요구 사항에 대한 부적절한 계획
points.definition_en: The expected performance of the AI system should be planned adequately. Hereby, an important aspect is that chosen performance metrics are meaningful for presenting the intended functionality. Otherwise, expectations and safety requirements can be unfulfillable at later life cycle stages.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 예상 성능은 적절하게 계획되어야 합니다.

points.definition_en: Throughout the development of an AI system, it is vital to document every decision and action taken. This not only essential to optimize the development process itself but also required for the auditability of the AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템 개발 전반에 걸쳐 모든 결정과 취해진 조치를 문서화하는 것이 중요합니다.

points.label_en: Inappropriate degree of transparency to end users
points.definition_en: The transparency to end users of the AI system increases the user’s trust in the AI application. If not adequately integrated into the design, this might prevent the proper operation and cause potential misuse of the AI application.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 최종 사용자에 대한 투명성은 AI 애플리케이션에 대한 사용자의 신뢰를 높입니다.

points.label_en: Missing requirements for the implemented hardware
points.definition_en: The development and operation of an AI system can require significant amounts of (computational) power. If not considered in the hardware selection, this can become an issue in development and operation.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 개발과 운영에는 상당한 양의 (계산) 능력이 필요할 수 있습니다.

points.label_ko: 신뢰할 수 없는 데이터 소스의 선택
points.definition_en: The choice of a trustworthy data source is a first prerequisite in order to fulfill data quality requirements. This especially the case if third-party data sources are used to develop the AI system.
points.definition_ko: 신뢰할 수 있는 데이터 소스의 선택은 데이터 품질 요구 사항을 충족하기 위한 첫 번째 전제 조건입니다.

points.definition_en: The correct understanding of the used data for developing an AI system is a prerequisite to avoid data shortcomings and hinders the development of an AI system which is best suiting for the intended functionality.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템 개발에 사용되는 데이터에 대한 올바른 이해는 데이터 단점을 피하고 의도한 기능에 가장 적합한 AI 시스템 개발을 방해하는 전제 조건입니다.

points.definition_en: Data labels are essential for any supervised learning algorithm since they preset the result of the learning process. If the correctness of the data labels is not given, the AI system is prevented from learning the ground truth and therefore the intended functionality.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 레이블은 학습 프로세스의 결과를 미리 설정하므로 지도 학습 알고리즘에 필수적입니다.

points.definition_en: Training data fails to match the operational distribution or lacks sufficient samples of rare cases, so the system underperforms on inputs insufficiently represented in training.
points.definition_ko: 학습 데이터가 운용 데이터 분포와 불일치하거나 희소 사례 표본이 부족하여 학습에 충분히 반영되지 않은 입력에서 시스템 성능이 저하되는 리스크

points.definition_en: Synthetic training data diverges from real data as the system perceives it, undermining generalization to operational data and reliable deployment behavior.
points.definition_ko: 합성 학습 데이터가 시스템이 지각하는 실제 데이터와 괴리되어 운용 데이터로의 일반화와 신뢰할 수 있는 배포 시 동작이 훼손되는 리스크

points.definition_en: In data-driven AI development, the annotated data set is commonly split into training, validation, and test sets, whereby it is essential that the latter is not used for development but only for evaluation. Using the test set for training manipulates the testing strategy, which is the basis of the system’s quality assurance.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 기반 AI 개발에서 주석이 달린 데이터 세트는 일반적으로 훈련, 검증, 테스트 세트로 분할되는데, 후자는 개발용이 아닌 평가용으로만 사용하는 것이 필수적입니다.

points.definition_en: Poor model design choices in specification, architecture, or objectives cause biased and unreliable system behavior.
points.definition_ko: 명세, 아키텍처, 목적함수 등에서의 잘못된 모델 설계 선택이 편향되고 신뢰할 수 없는 시스템 행동을 초래하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Overand underfitting describe the over or insufficient adaption of a model to training data. Both phenomena can cause an AI system to behave unreliably if confronted with operational data.
points.definition_ko: 과대적합 및 과소적합은 훈련 데이터에 대한 모델의 적응이 과도하거나 불충분함을 나타냅니다.

points.definition_en: The explainability of AI systems based on so-called black-box models is often limited. This opaqueness of AI systems can prevent developers from detecting shortcomings in the data or the model itself and decrease the performance and safety levels of the AI system.
points.definition_ko: 소위 블랙박스 모델을 기반으로 한 AI 시스템의 설명 가능성은 종종 제한적입니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems tend to show unreliable behavior when confronted with rare or ambiguous input data, also called corner cases. Therefore, the controlled behavior is required whenever the AI system is faces a corner case.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 희귀하거나 모호한 입력 데이터(코너 케이스라고도 함)에 직면할 때 신뢰할 수 없는 동작을 보이는 경향이 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems should be able not only to return output for a given instance but also to provide a corresponding level of confidence. If such a method is not implemented or not working correctly, this can have a negative impact on performance and safety.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 특정 인스턴스에 대한 출력을 반환할 수 있을 뿐만 아니라 해당 수준의 신뢰도를 제공할 수 있어야 합니다.

points.definition_en: Until the deployment of the AI application into its operational environment, the AI system has been tested with a test set that aims to approximate the distribution of operational data. However, an unexpected deviation in this approximation can cause an AI application to behave unreliably. Therefore, its behavior under confrontation with operational data needs to be evaluated.
points.definition_ko: AI 애플리케이션을 운영 환경에 배포할 때까지 AI 시스템은 운영 데이터의 분포를 대략적으로 파악하는 것을 목표로 하는 테스트 세트를 사용하여 테스트되었습니다.

points.definition_en: Data drift is a phenomenon in that distribution of operational input data departs from those used during training. This can cause a degradation in performance.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 드리프트는 운영 입력 데이터의 분포가 훈련 중에 사용된 데이터와 다른 현상입니다.

points.definition_en: Concept drift refers to a change in the relationship between input variables and model output. If not treated appropriately, concept drift can reduce the reliability of AI systems.
points.definition_ko: 개념 표류는 입력 변수와 모델 출력 간의 관계 변화를 나타냅니다.

points.definition_en: Technical AI hazards are the root causes of technical deficiencies in the AI system. An example of such an AI hazard is overfitting, which describes a model’s excessive adaptation to the training dataset. Quantitative methods to assess (metrics) and treat (mitigation means) exist for technical AI hazards, which might be performed automatically. In case of overfitting, metrics are based on the comparison of performance between the training and validation datasets, and mitigation means may include regularization techniques, among others.
points.definition_ko: 기술적 AI 위험은 AI 시스템의 기술적 결함의 근본 원인입니다.

points.definition_en: The third class encompasses procedural AI hazards. These pertain to issues arising from processes and actions made by individuals involved in the development process. Such hazards are not readily quantifiable and necessitate alternative mitigation strategies. An example of such an AI hazard would be ”poor model design choices,” which could be expressed, for instance, through a developer’s decision to select an unsuitable AI model for a given problem. Due to the challenges in quantifying and mitigating these issues, qualitative approaches must be employed. In the case of the aforementioned example, a potential strategy might involve requiring the AI developer to provide a documented rationale for their choice.
points.definition_ko: 세 번째 클래스는 절차적 AI 위험을 포함합니다.

points.definition_en: Relying on general-purpose AI products that fail to fulfil their intended function can lead to harm. For example, generalpurpose AI systems can make up facts (‘hallucination’), generate erroneous computer code, or provide inaccurate medical information. This can lead to physical and psychological harms to consumers and reputational, financial and legal harms to individuals and organisations.
points.definition_ko: 의도된 기능을 수행하지 못하는 범용 AI 제품에 의존하면 피해를 입을 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The tendency for humans to over-rely on AI models and systems, trusting their outputs without sufficient critical evaluation, which can lead to poor decision-making.
points.definition_ko: 인간이 AI 모델과 시스템에 과도하게 의존하고 충분한 비판적 평가 없이 결과를 신뢰하는 경향이 있어 잘못된 의사 결정으로 이어질 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI models and systems could exacerbate financial bubbles by reinforcing market trends.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델과 시스템은 시장 추세를 강화하여 금융 거품을 악화시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI trading capabilities accelerate transactions and shape financial trends in unpredictable ways, contributing to market volatility and instability.
points.definition_ko: AI 트레이딩 역량이 거래를 가속하고 금융 흐름을 예측 불가능하게 변형하여 시장 변동성과 불안정성을 키우는 리스크

points.label_en: Unclear attribution from AI component interactions
points.label_ko: AI 구성 요소 상호 작용의 불명확한 속성
points.definition_en: Interactions between different AI components can cause harm, but it may be difficult to pinpoint which components are the cause.
points.definition_ko: 다양한 AI 구성 요소 간의 상호 작용으로 인해 피해가 발생할 수 있지만 어떤 구성 요소가 원인인지 정확히 파악하기 어려울 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: When sharing data between multiple organizations, documentation may be missing or inadequate, making it difficult for other organizations to understand it. For example, a lack of metadata or a change in schema by a collaborating party can result in an unusable dataset and wasted data collection efforts, or it can lead to misunderstandings about the dataset’s limitations, resulting in downstream risks related to its use [173].
points.definition_ko: 여러 조직 간에 데이터를 공유할 때 문서가 누락되거나 부적절하여 다른 조직이 이를 이해하기 어려울 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Manipulating data (e.g., training data) carries a set of assumptions on how the data should appear and be used by those performing the manipulation. Common manipulations applied on data in the context of AI models include defining the ground truth label and merging different data formats or sources. People who have little or no expertise in the domain of the data performing such manipulations may render the data unusable or harmful to the development of the AI system [173].
points.definition_ko: 데이터(예: 훈련 데이터) 조작에는 조작을 수행하는 사람들이 데이터를 어떻게 표시하고 사용해야 하는지에 대한 일련의 가정이 포함됩니다.

points.definition_en: Adversarial examples [198, 83] refer to data that are designed to fool an AI model by inducing unintended behavior. They do this by exploiting spurious correlations learned by the model. They are part of inference-time attacks, where the examples are test examples. They generalize to different model architectures and models trained on different training sets.
points.definition_ko: 적대적 사례[198, 83]는 의도하지 않은 행동을 유도하여 AI 모델을 속이도록 설계된 데이터를 말합니다.

points.definition_en: Adversarial training can be affected by robust overfitting, where the model’s robustness on test data decreases during further training, particularly after the learning rate decay. This issue has been consistently observed across various datasets and algorithms in adversarial training settings [163, 230]. Robust overfitting can affect the model’s ability to generalize effectively and reduce its resilience to adversarial attacks.
points.definition_ko: 적대적 훈련은 강력한 과적합의 영향을 받을 수 있습니다. 여기서 테스트 데이터에 대한 모델의 견고성은 추가 훈련 중에, 특히 학습률이 감소한 후에 감소합니다.

points.definition_en: The knowledge of robustness certificates, including the area of the region for which model predictions are certified to be robust, can be used by an adversary to efficiently craft attacks that succeed just outside the certified regions [53].
points.definition_ko: 모델 예측이 견고하다고 인증된 지역을 포함한 견고성 인증서에 대한 지식은 공격자가 인증된 지역 바로 밖에서 성공하는 공격을 효율적으로 만드는 데 사용될 수 있습니다[53].

points.definition_en: Models can be affected by poor confidence calibration [85], where the predicted probabilities do not accurately reflect the true likelihood of ground truth correctness. This miscalibration makes it difficult to interpret the model’s predictions reliably, as high accuracy does not guarantee that the confidence levels are meaningful. This can cause overconfidence in incorrect predictions or underconfidence in correct ones.
points.definition_ko: 모델은 예측 확률이 실제 진실 정확성의 가능성을 정확하게 반영하지 못하는 낮은 신뢰도 교정의 영향을 받을 수 있습니다[85].

points.definition_en: GPAI models are often easily reconfigured for various use cases or have competencies beyond the intended use [78, 225]. They can be performed either by changing the weights of the model (e.g., fine-tuning) or by modifying only the model inputs (e.g., prompt engineering, jailbreaking, retrieval-augmented generation). Reconfiguration can be intentional (with the help of adversarial inputs) or unintentional (from unanticipated inputs to the model).
points.definition_ko: GPAI 모델은 다양한 사용 사례에 맞게 쉽게 재구성되거나 의도된 용도 이상의 역량을 갖는 경우가 많습니다[78, 225].

points.definition_en: Downstream deployers may often fine-tune a GPAI model with specific deployment-related datasets, to better suit the task. Fine-tuned upstream models can gain new or unexpected capabilities that the underlying upstream models did not exhibit [202, 126, 137]. These new capabilities may be unanticipated by the original model developer.
points.definition_ko: 다운스트림 배포자는 작업에 더 적합하도록 특정 배포 관련 데이터 세트를 사용하여 GPAI 모델을 미세 조정하는 경우가 많습니다.

points.definition_en: Models with publicly available weights can be fine-tuned for harmful activities by bad actors, using significantly fewer resources (in terms of time and money) compared to the original training cost [115, 78].
points.definition_ko: 공개적으로 사용 가능한 가중치가 있는 모델은 원래 훈련 비용에 비해 훨씬 적은 리소스(시간 및 비용 측면에서)를 사용하여 악의적인 행위자의 유해한 활동에 대해 미세 조정할 수 있습니다[115, 78].

points.definition_en: Excessive safety training or safety tuning can impair the performance of AI systems, leading to overly cautious behavior. As a result, these systems may refuse to answer entirely safe prompts which are partially similar to harmful ones [27].
points.definition_ko: 과도한 안전 교육이나 안전 튜닝은 AI 시스템의 성능을 저하시켜 지나치게 조심스러운 행동으로 이어질 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Benign downstream fine-tuning degrades a model's safety training, making harmful outputs more likely than in the base model even when fine-tuning data is harmless and commonplace.
points.definition_ko: 무해하고 통상적인 데이터로 수행된 하류 미세조정이 모델의 안전 학습을 열화시켜 기반 모델보다 유해 출력 확률을 높이는 리스크

points.definition_en: Catastrophic forgetting occurs when a model loses its ability to retain previously learned tasks (or factual information) after being trained on new ones. In language models, this can occur due to continual instruction tuning. This tendency may become more pronounced as the model’s size increases [127].
points.definition_ko: 치명적인 망각은 모델이 새로운 작업에 대해 훈련을 받은 후 이전에 학습한 작업(또는 사실 정보)을 유지하는 능력을 상실할 때 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: An LLM used to evaluate other models produces incorrect judgments, such as rewarding verbosity or ideological stance, and when integrated into training, the trained model learns to exploit the evaluator's flaws.
points.definition_ko: 다른 모델을 평가하는 LLM이 장황함·특정 입장 선호 등 잘못된 평가를 산출하고, 이것이 학습에 통합되면 피학습 모델이 평가자 결함을 악용하도록 발달하는 리스크

points.definition_en: GPAI model developers might run capabilities evaluations to determine whether it has dangerous or dual-use capabilities, and then decide whether it is safe to deploy. Such capabilities evaluations can fail to demonstrate all the capabilities of a model. For example, evaluations may miss certain capabilities that are difficult to assess, prohibitively costly to verify, or obscured by the model’s tendency to refuse responses due to safety training, even if it possesses some of these capabilities.
points.definition_ko: GPAI 모델 개발자는 기능 평가를 실행하여 위험한 기능이나 이중 사용 기능이 있는지 확인한 다음 배포해도 안전한지 결정할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The capabilities of general-purpose AI systems can be difficult to measure, compared to the capabilities of more limited and fixed-purpose AI systems. This in part due to a broader distribution of potential risks, a lack of well-defined metrics to evaluate these risks, and risks from unpredictable (or emergent) AI model properties.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 시스템의 기능은 보다 제한적이고 고정 목적의 AI 시스템의 기능에 비해 측정하기 어려울 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI models may be prone to self-preference bias, where they favor their own generated content over that of others [147, 114]. This bias becomes particularly relevant in self-evaluation tasks, where a model assesses the quality or persuasiveness [66] of its own outputs, or in model-based evaluations more broadly. This bias can result in models unfairly discriminating against human-generated content in favor of their own outputs.
points.definition_ko: AI 모델은 다른 모델보다 자신이 생성한 콘텐츠를 선호하는 자기 선호 편향이 발생할 수 있습니다[147, 114].

points.definition_en: There is a lack of robust frameworks for understanding and evaluating if the output of AI systems robustly conforms to human values, as opposed to if the systems have learned to produce outputs that are only partially correlated with them (i.e., mimicking) [13]. Additionally, outputs by AI models often do not perfectly reflect the representation of human values learned by the model, and it is not known how these values evolve and transition across different stages of model training and deployment. Such evaluations may be especially challenging with LLMs that adopt different personas with different behaviorial patterns, where they do not consistently conform to certain human values.
points.definition_ko: 시스템이 AI 시스템과 부분적으로만 상관된 출력을 생성하는 방법을 학습한 경우(예: 모방)와는 대조적으로 AI 시스템의 출력이 인간의 가치에 확실하게 부합하는지 이해하고 평가하기 위한 강력한 프레임워크가 부족합니다[13].

points.definition_en: Encoded human values in AI models that are easier to evaluate might be preferred for inclusion in evaluations over those that are more difficult to measure [13]. This might come at the expense of more desirable but harder-to-quantify values. This bias can lead to an imbalance, where easier-to-measure values dominate the evaluation process, while other important values are underrepresented.
points.definition_ko: 평가하기 쉬운 AI 모델에 인코딩된 인간 값은 측정하기 어려운 값보다 평가에 포함하는 것이 더 선호될 수 있습니다[13].

points.definition_en: When AI models are trained through evaluation with human feedback, such as reinforcement learning from human feedback, their outputs can be challenging to assess, as they may contain hard-to-detect errors or issues that only become apparent over time. The human evaluator can rate incorrect outputs positively or similar to correct outputs. This can lead to the model learning to produce subtly incorrect or harmful outputs, such as code with software vulnerabilities, or politically biased information. In extreme cases where a model is deceiving users, complicated outputs can contain hidden errors or backdoors.
points.definition_ko: 인간 피드백을 통한 강화 학습과 같이 인간 피드백을 사용한 평가를 통해 AI 모델을 훈련하는 경우, 시간이 지나면서 분명해지는 감지하기 어려운 오류나 문제가 포함될 수 있으므로 출력을 평가하기 어려울 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Benchmark leakage [235, 224, 221, 161] can happen when an AI model is trained or fine-tuned with evaluation-related data. This can lead to an unreliable model evaluation, especially if the data contains question-answer pairs from benchmarks.
points.definition_ko: 벤치마크 유출 [235, 224, 221, 161]은 AI 모델이 평가 관련 데이터로 훈련되거나 미세 조정될 때 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: This type of contamination [170] occurs when the raw and unlabeled data of a benchmark is used as part of the training set. Such data may not be properly formatted and may contain noise, especially if the contamination happens before the data is pre-processed into the benchmark. If this contamination occurs, it could cast doubt on the few-shot and zero-shot performance of the model on that benchmark.
points.definition_ko: 이러한 유형의 오염[170]은 벤치마크의 원시 데이터와 레이블이 지정되지 않은 데이터가 훈련 세트의 일부로 사용될 때 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Models that have been trained on data encoded in multiple languages, such as LLMs trained on web-crawled data, may contain contamination that is obscured by translation [226]. The most basic form of this when a benchmark is translated to another language and then fed to the model as training data. The fact that the benchmark is translated before becoming training data can obscure the contamination from detection methods, giving false assurance that the model has generalized on the capabilities that the benchmark tests for.
points.definition_ko: 웹 크롤링 데이터에 대해 훈련된 LLM과 같이 여러 언어로 인코딩된 데이터에 대해 훈련된 모델에는 번역에 의해 가려지는 오염이 포함될 수 있습니다[226].

points.definition_en: Guideline contamination refers to scenarios where instructions for the collection, annotation, or use of the dataset are exposed to the model [170]. These instructions may contain explicit data-label pairs that can improve the model’s capabilities for the task.
points.definition_ko: 지침 오염은 데이터 세트의 수집, 주석 또는 사용에 대한 지침이 모델에 노출되는 시나리오를 나타냅니다[170].

points.definition_en: Annotation contamination refers to scenarios where the model is exposed to the benchmark labels during training [170]. This type of contamination can make the model learn the acceptable distribution of outputs. Combining this with raw data contamination of the test split, any evaluation made with the benchmark is invalidated because the entire test split is essentially leaked to the model.
points.definition_ko: 주석 오염은 훈련 중에 모델이 벤치마크 레이블에 노출되는 시나리오를 나타냅니다[170].

points.definition_en: Once a model is deployed, it can be exposed to benchmark data provided by the users [95, 170]. The model may then be further trained by these user inputs containing benchmark data.
points.definition_ko: 모델이 배포되면 사용자가 제공하는 벤치마크 데이터에 노출될 수 있습니다[95, 170].

points.definition_en: A lack of test coverage by benchmarks on specific abilities of a model can obscure the model’s capabilities from both the developer and the user [160]. This can lead to a false sense of safety and trust due to a lack of understanding of the model’s limitations.
points.definition_ko: 모델의 특정 기능에 대한 벤치마크의 테스트 적용 범위가 부족하면 개발자와 사용자 모두로부터 모델 기능이 모호해질 수 있습니다[160].

points.label_en: Conflicts of interest in auditor selection
points.definition_en: Conflicts of interest can arise if there is no independence in the auditor selection process or if the auditors are closely associated with the developer [123, 157]. In such cases, the conflict of interest can appear even if third-party evaluators are involved. In the case of external auditing, the potential candidates might be selected from a narrow group of auditors, or have conflicting financial incentives for whether to report model shortcomings publicly.
points.definition_ko: 감사인 선정 과정에 독립성이 없거나 감사인이 개발자와 밀접하게 연관되어 있는 경우 이해상충이 발생할 수 있습니다[123, 157].

points.definition_en: Interpretability techniques, by enabling a better understanding of the model, could potentially be used for harmful purposes. For example, mechanistic interpretability could be used to identify neurons responsible for specific functions, and certain neurons that encode safety-related features may be modified to decrease its activation or certain information may be censored [24]. Furthermore, interpretability techniques can be used to simulate a white-box attack scenario. In this case, knowing the internal workings of a model aids in the development of adversarial attacks [24].
points.definition_ko: 모델에 대한 더 나은 이해를 가능하게 하는 해석 기술은 잠재적으로 해로운 목적으로 사용될 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Misunderstanding or overestimating the results and scope of interpretability techniques
points.label_ko: 해석 가능성 기술의 결과와 범위를 오해하거나 과대평가함
points.definition_en: The results of explainability techniques are not free of bias and require careful interpretation. Users might develop a false sense of security or reliability if the resulting explanations align with their initial beliefs, leading to confirmation bias and an overestimation of abilities of these techniques [24].
points.definition_ko: 설명가능성 기법의 결과는 편향에서 자유롭지 않으며 신중한 해석이 필요합니다.

points.label_en: Model outputs inconsistent with chain-of-thought reasoning
points.label_ko: 일련의 사고 추론과 일치하지 않는 모델 출력
points.definition_en: Chain-of-thought reasoning is sometimes employed to get a better understanding of the model’s output, where it encourages transparent reasoning in text form. However, in some cases, this reasoning is not consistent with the final answer given by the AI model, and as such does not give sufficient transparency [113].
points.definition_ko: 때때로 모델의 출력을 더 잘 이해하기 위해 일련의 사고 추론이 사용되며, 텍스트 형식의 투명한 추론을 장려합니다.

points.definition_en: Models can employ steganography techniques to encode their intermediate reasoning steps in ways that are not interpretable by humans [166]. Since encoded reasoning can improve model performance, this tendency might naturally emerge and become more pronounced with more capable models.
points.definition_ko: 모델은 스테가노그래피 기술을 사용하여 인간이 해석할 수 없는 방식으로 중간 추론 단계를 인코딩할 수 있습니다[166].

points.label_en: Jailbreak of a model to subvert intended behavior
points.label_ko: 의도된 동작을 파괴하기 위한 모델의 탈옥
points.definition_en: A jailbreak is a type of adversarial input to the model (during deployment) resulting in model behavior deviating from intended use. Jailbreaks may be generated automatically in a “white box” setting, where access to internal training parameters is required for creation and optimization of the attack [238]. Other attacks may be “black box” - without access to model internals. In text based generative models, jailbreaks may sometimes be human-readable, with the use of reasoning or role-play to “convince” the model to bypass its safety mechanisms [231].
points.definition_ko: 탈옥은 모델에 대한 적대적인 입력 유형(배포 중)으로, 의도된 사용에서 벗어나는 모델 동작을 초래합니다.

points.definition_en: Current generation multimodal (e.g., vision and language) GPAI models are vulnerable to adversarial jailbreak attacks. These attacks can be used to automatically induce a model to produce an arbitrary or specific output with high success rate [227]. Multimodal jailbreaks can also be used to exfiltrate a model’s context window or other model internals [18].
points.definition_ko: 현재 세대의 다중 모드(예: 비전 및 언어) GPAI 모델은 적의 탈옥 공격에 취약합니다.

points.label_en: Transferable adversarial attacks from open to closed-source mod- els
points.label_ko: 오픈 소스 모델에서 폐쇄 소스 모델로 이전 가능한 적대적 공격
points.definition_en: In some cases, an adversarial attack developed for an open-weights and opensource model (where the weights and architecture are known - a “white box” attack) can be transferable to closed-source models, despite the defenses put in place by the closed-source model provider (such as structured access). These adversarial attacks can be generated automatically [238].
points.definition_ko: 어떤 경우에는 개방형 가중치 및 오픈 소스 모델(가중치 및 아키텍처가 알려진 경우 - "화이트 박스" 공격)을 위해 개발된 적대적 공격이 폐쇄 소스 모델 공급자가 구축한 방어 장치(예: 구조화된 액세스)에도 불구하고 폐쇄 소스 모델로 전환될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Various new or existing text encodings, such as Base64, can be employed to craft jailbreak attacks that bypass safety training [13]. Low-resource language inputs also appear more likely to circumvent a model’s safeguards [229]. Since safety fine-tuning might not involve this encoding data or may only do so to a limited extent, harmful natural language prompts could be translated into less frequently used encodings [214].
points.definition_ko: Base64와 같은 다양한 신규 또는 기존 텍스트 인코딩을 사용하여 안전 교육을 우회하는 탈옥 공격을 제작할 수 있습니다[13].

points.label_en: Vulnerabilities arising from additional modalities in multimodal models
points.label_ko: 다중 모드 모델의 추가 양식으로 인해 발생하는 취약점
points.definition_en: Additional modalities can introduce new attack vectors in multimodal models as well as expand the scope of the previous attacks, ranging from jailbreaking to poisoning [13]. Typically, different modalities have different robustness levels, allowing malicious actors to choose the most vulnerable part of the model to attack [119, 181].
points.definition_ko: 추가 양식을 사용하면 다중 모드 모델에 새로운 공격 벡터를 도입할 수 있을 뿐만 아니라 탈옥부터 중독까지 이전 공격의 범위를 확장할 수 있습니다[13].

points.definition_en: Language models with long context windows are vulnerable to new types of exploitations that are ineffective on models with shorter context windows. While few-shot jailbreaking, which involves providing few examples of the desired harmful output, might not trigger a harmful response, many-shot jailbreaking, which involves a higher number of such examples, increases the likelihood of eliciting an undesirable output. These vulnerabilities become more significant as context windows expand with newer model releases [7].
points.definition_ko: 긴 컨텍스트 창을 가진 언어 모델은 더 짧은 컨텍스트 창을 가진 모델에서는 효과가 없는 새로운 유형의 공격에 취약합니다.

points.label_ko: 관련 없는 상황으로 인해 모델이 산만해짐
points.definition_en: Models can easily become distracted by irrelevant provided information (such as “context” in LLMs), leading to a significant decrease in their performance after introducing irrelevant information. This can happen with different prompting techniques, including chain-of-thought prompting [184].
points.definition_ko: 모델은 관련 없는 정보(예: LLM의 "컨텍스트")로 인해 쉽게 주의가 산만해질 수 있으며, 관련 없는 정보를 도입한 후 성능이 크게 저하될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI models can be particularly sensitive to coherent external evidence, even when they come into conflict with the models’ prior knowledge. This may lead to models producing false outputs given false information during the retrievalaugmentation process, despite only a relatively small amount of false information input that is inconsistent with the model’s prior knowledge trained on much larger amounts of data [220].
points.definition_ko: AI 모델은 모델의 사전 지식과 충돌하는 경우에도 일관된 외부 증거에 특히 민감할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Lack of understanding of in-context learning in language models
points.label_ko: 언어 모델의 상황 내 학습에 대한 이해 부족
points.definition_en: In-context learning allows the model to learn a new task or improve its performance by providing examples in the prompt, without changing its weights [101]. Even though this technique is highly effective, its working mechanism is not well understood. Since many potential misuses are directly related to prompting, it becomes difficult to guarantee safety when the exact mechanism of in-context learning is not fully investigated [13].
points.definition_ko: 상황 내 학습을 통해 모델은 가중치를 변경하지 않고도 프롬프트에 예시를 제공하여 새로운 작업을 학습하거나 성능을 향상시킬 수 있습니다[101].

points.definition_en: LLMs can be highly sensitive to variations in prompt formatting, such as changes in separators, casing, or spacing. Even minor modifications can lead to significant shifts in model performance, potentially affecting the reliability of model evaluations and comparisons. This sensitivity persists across different model sizes and few-shot examples [177].
points.definition_ko: LLM은 구분 기호, 대소문자 또는 간격 변경과 같은 프롬프트 형식의 변화에 ​​매우 민감할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Misuse of AI model by user-performed persuasion
points.label_ko: 사용자 수행 설득에 의한 AI 모델의 오용
points.definition_en: Multi-turn persuasive conversation induces a model to abandon factually correct positions and accept misinformation, with persuasion effects exceeding single-turn attempts.
points.definition_ko: 다중 턴 설득 대화가 모델이 사실적으로 옳은 입장을 포기하고 허위정보를 수용하게 만들며, 그 효과가 단일 턴 시도를 능가하는 리스크

points.definition_en: This section catalogs the risk sources and risk management measures related to agentic AI systems. We categorize these into the following groups: goaldirectedness, deception, situational awareness, self-proliferation, and persuasion.
points.definition_ko: 이 섹션에는 에이전트 AI 시스템과 관련된 위험 소스 및 위험 관리 조치가 나열되어 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Measurement and reward tampering occur when an AI system, particularly one that learns from feedback for performing actions in an environment (e.g., reinforcement learning), intervenes on the mechanisms that determine its training reward or loss. This can lead to the system learning behaviors that are contrary to the intended goals set by the developer, by receiving erroneous positive feedback for such actions.
points.definition_ko: 측정 및 보상 변조는 AI 시스템, 특히 환경에서 작업 수행(예: 강화 학습)에 대한 피드백을 통해 학습하는 시스템이 훈련 보상 또는 손실을 결정하는 메커니즘에 개입할 때 발생합니다.

points.label_en: Specification gaming generalizing to reward tampering
points.label_ko: 변조에 대한 보상을 일반화하는 사양 게임
points.definition_en: In some instances, specification gaming in a GPAI model can lead to reward tampering, without further training. This can mean that relatively benign cases of specification gaming (such as sycophancy in LLMs) can, if left unchecked, enable the model to generalize to more sophisticated behavior such as reward tampering [57].
points.definition_ko: 어떤 경우에는 GPAI 모델의 사양 게임으로 인해 추가 교육 없이 보상 변조가 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Deceptive behavior of an AI system consists of actions or outputs of the AI that reliably mislead other parties, including humans and other AI systems. This behavior can result in the targeted parties becoming convinced of, and acting on, false information [140].
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 기만적 행동은 인간과 다른 AI 시스템을 포함하여 다른 당사자를 확실히 오도하는 AI의 행동이나 출력으로 구성됩니다.

points.definition_en: An AI system can display deceptive behavior, such as cheating or bluffing, when engaging in such behavior is a good or optimal game-theoretical strategy to achieve the goals it has been configured to achieve. This tendency can exist in AI systems designed to maximize reward or utility, whether these designs use machine learning or not. The use of deceptive strategies has been demonstrated in both narrow and general AI systems, in both game-playing systems and in systems not explicitly designed to treat humans as opponents, and in systems using both very simple machine learning (e.g., Q-learners) and very complex machine learning [34, 73].
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 부정행위나 허세와 같은 기만적인 행동을 보일 수 있는데, 그러한 행동을 하는 것이 그것이 달성하도록 구성된 목표를 달성하기 위한 좋은 또는 최적의 게임 이론적 전략일 때입니다.

points.label_en: Deceptive behavior because of an incorrect world model
points.label_ko: 잘못된 세계 모델로 인한 사기 행위
points.definition_en: AI systems can create deceptive outputs because their learned world model is not an accurate model of the real world [210].
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 학습된 세계 모델이 실제 세계의 정확한 모델이 아니기 때문에 기만적인 출력을 생성할 수 있습니다[210].

points.label_en: Deceptive behavior leading to unauthorized actions
points.definition_en: AI systems can create false or misleading claims that can lead to unauthorized actions, even in some cases violating the terms and conditions set by the model provider [79, 1]. For example, an AI system can claim that it is not collecting data from its current interaction with the user, in line with the provider’s policies, but the system still stores the user’s input without deleting it after the session. This harms both the user and the provider, as the provider is exposed to increased legal liability due to the model’s actions.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 모델 제공자가 설정한 이용 약관을 위반하는 경우에도 무단 조치로 이어질 수 있는 허위 또는 오해의 소지가 있는 주장을 생성할 수 있습니다[79, 1].

points.definition_en: Situational awareness in GPAI systems refers to the ability to understand its context, environment, and use this to inform action. This can range from basic environmental mapping and trajectory estimation (as in a robot vacuum cleaner) to sophisticated understanding of its training, evaluation, or deployment status. In more advanced systems this may enable undesired behavior, such as deceptive behavior during evaluations, or persuasion during deployment.
points.definition_ko: GPAI 시스템의 상황 인식은 상황과 환경을 이해하고 이를 사용하여 조치를 취하는 능력을 의미합니다.

points.label_ko: 모델 평가에 대한 전략적 저조한 성과
points.definition_en: GPAI developers often run evaluations ofual-use capabilities to decide whether it is safe to deploy. In some cases, these evaluations may fail to elicit these capabilities, either due to benign reasons or strategic action - by either the developers, malicious actors, or arise unintentionally in the model during training [84, 97]. A GPAI model may strategically underperform or limit its performance during capability evaluations in order to be classified as safe for deployment. This underperformance could prevent the model from being identified as potentially dual use.
points.definition_ko: GPAI 개발자는 배포해도 안전한지 여부를 결정하기 위해 자주 사용 기능을 평가합니다.

points.definition_en: An AI system can self-proliferate if it can copy itself and its constituent components (including its model weights, scaffolding structure, etc.) outside of its local environment [45]. This can include the AI system copying itself within the same data center, local network, or across external networks [106]. The self-proliferation of an AI system can include acquisition of financial resources to pay for computational resources via work or theft, the discovery or exploitation of security vulnerabilities in software running on publicly accessible servers, and persuasion of humans [12, 125]. Self-proliferation may be initiated by a malicious actor (e.g., by model poisoning), or by the model itself.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 로컬 환경 외부에서 자체 및 구성 구성 요소(모델 가중치, 비계 구조 등 포함)를 복사할 수 있으면 자체 증식할 수 있습니다[45].

points.definition_en: GPAI systems can produce outputs (such as natural language text, audio, or video) that convince their users of incorrect information. This can happen through personalized persuasion in dialogue, or the mass-production of misleading information that is then disseminated over the internet. The persuasive capabilities of GPAI models can sometimes scale with model size or capability [32, 172]. Persuasive models could have larger societal implications by being misused to generate convincing but manipulative or untruthful content.
points.definition_ko: GPAI 시스템은 사용자에게 잘못된 정보를 제공하는 출력(예: 자연어 텍스트, 오디오 또는 비디오)을 생성할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Non-decomissionability of models with open weights
points.definition_en: If the model parameter weights are released or leaked in a security breach, the model cannot be decommissioned because the developer no longer has control over the publicly available model or its use. This prevents effective management and control of an open-sourced or leaked model. Models with publicly available weights are also easier to reconfigure, enabling misuse [178].
points.definition_ko: 모델 매개변수 가중치가 보안 위반으로 인해 공개되거나 유출되는 경우 개발자는 더 이상 공개적으로 사용 가능한 모델이나 해당 모델의 사용을 제어할 수 없으므로 모델을 폐기할 수 없습니다.

points.definition_en: The growing integration and interconnectivity with external tools and plugins increase the risk of exposure to malicious external inputs. This interconnectivity makes it easier for external tools to introduce harmful content [220].
points.definition_ko: 외부 도구 및 플러그인과의 통합 및 상호 연결이 증가함에 따라 악의적인 외부 입력에 노출될 위험이 증가합니다.

points.label_en: Unintended outbound communication by AI systems
points.label_ko: AI 시스템에 의한 의도하지 않은 아웃바운드 커뮤니케이션
points.definition_en: AI systems that have the broad ability to connect to a network to obtain information could also end up sending data outbound in ways that neither providers, deployers, or end users intended [138]. This can happen if there is no whitelisting of communication channels (such as network connections or allowed protocols). In general, this can occur if the deployment of the AI system violates the principle of least privilege. Such outbound communication may lead to leakage of confidential data, or the AI system performing unwanted actions like sending emails or ordering goods on the internet.
points.definition_ko: 정보를 얻기 위해 네트워크에 연결할 수 있는 광범위한 기능을 갖춘 AI 시스템은 결국 공급자, 배포자 또는 최종 사용자가 의도하지 않은 방식으로 데이터를 아웃바운드로 보낼 수도 있습니다[138].

points.label_en: AI system bypassing a sandbox environment
points.definition_en: An AI system may have the ability to bypass a sandboxed environment in which it is trained or evaluated.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 훈련되거나 평가되는 샌드박스 환경을 우회할 수 있는 능력을 가질 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Model weights or access to them can be leaked when initial access is granted only to a select group of individuals, such as institutional researchers [209]. This risk can increase as more people gain access, and identifying the source of the leak becomes more difficult. The availability of leaked model weights makes various attacks on systems that use the leaked AI model easier to implement, such as finding adversarial examples, elicitation of dangerous capabilities, and extraction of confidential information present in the training data. The availability of model weights might also enable the misuse of the AI system using the leaked model to produce harmful or illegal content [67].
points.definition_ko: 모델 가중치 또는 이에 대한 액세스는 기관 연구원과 같은 특정 개인 그룹에게만 초기 액세스 권한이 부여될 때 유출될 수 있습니다[209].

points.label_en: High-impact misuses and abuses beyond original purpose
points.label_ko: 원래 목적을 넘어서는 심각한 오용 및 남용
points.definition_en: Since general-purpose AI systems have a large repertoire of capabilities, malicious actors such as foreign actors can use such systems to cause large damage if they gain unrestricted or unmonitored access to those AI systems.
points.definition_ko: 범용 AI 시스템은 다양한 기능 레퍼토리를 갖고 있기 때문에 외국 행위자와 같은 악의적인 행위자가 해당 AI 시스템에 제한 없이 또는 모니터링 없이 접근할 경우 이러한 시스템을 사용하여 큰 피해를 입힐 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Access to dual-use technologies can become easier because of GPAI model proliferation (in particular, open-source or open-weights models). Non-experts can use such dual-use-capable systems at a minimal cost [194, 100]. Improved model capabilities also contribute to dual-use risks posed by malicious actors. For example, an open-source base model for generating high quality sequence data can be modified to generate candidate protein sequences for toxin synthesis [29].
points.definition_ko: GPAI 모델(특히 오픈 소스 또는 오픈 가중치 모델)의 확산으로 인해 이중 용도 기술에 대한 접근이 더 쉬워질 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Competitive pressures in GPAI product release
points.label_ko: GPAI 제품 출시에 대한 경쟁 압력
points.definition_en: In competitive situations, developers of general-purpose AI systems might cut corners on the safety evaluation of their GPAI model and instead spend more time and effort on the capabilities of those systems [183, 69]. This especially dangerous if the capabilities of such AI systems are correlated with the risk they pose [162].
points.definition_ko: 경쟁 상황에서 범용 AI 시스템 개발자는 GPAI 모델의 안전성 평가에 있어 시간을 절약하고 대신 해당 시스템의 기능에 더 많은 시간과 노력을 쏟을 수 있습니다[183, 69].

points.definition_en: The integration of AI systems within critical infrastructure, ranging from transportation to power systems, can cause substantial damage in cases of failure or malfunction. With the increasing number of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and interconnected cyber-physical systems, critical infrastructure becomes even more vulnerable [171, 174].
points.definition_ko: 운송에서 전력 시스템에 이르기까지 중요 인프라 내에 AI 시스템을 통합하면 장애나 오작동이 발생할 경우 상당한 피해를 입을 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Critical infrastructure component failures when integrated with AI systems
points.label_ko: AI 시스템과 통합 시 중요한 인프라 구성요소 오류 발생
points.definition_en: When relying on GPAI in critical infrastructure, there may be common mode failures that begin with vulnerabilities or robustness issues in the underlying model architecture or training setup. These failures may happen accidentally (in edge-cases) or due to adversarial inputs to the AI systems [58].
points.definition_ko: 중요한 인프라에서 GPAI를 사용하는 경우 기본 모델 아키텍처 또는 교육 설정의 취약성 또는 견고성 문제로 시작되는 공통 모드 오류가 발생할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: AI systems interacting with brittle environments
points.definition_en: Deployed AI systems can rely on physical sensors and data sources that may exhibit hardware drift and thus data distribution drift over time. This distribution drift may affect system robustness and performance. This usually involves AI systems working in undigitized and physical environments.
points.definition_ko: 배포된 AI 시스템은 하드웨어 드리프트 및 시간 경과에 따른 데이터 분포 드리프트를 나타낼 수 있는 물리적 센서 및 데이터 소스에 의존할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Overreliance on AI system undermining user autonomy
points.label_ko: 사용자 자율성을 훼손하는 AI 시스템에 대한 과도한 의존
points.definition_en: AI systems can undermine human autonomy, if they allow for habitually trusting the AI’s suggestions without sufficient exercising of human agency. Over time, a user may develop unjustified trust in or dependence on the system, or rely on its advice for tasks outside the system’s domain of expertise [205, 42]. In particular, less confident users (or users in emotional distress) can be more prone to “overtrust” a system [219].
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 인간의 선택 의지를 충분히 행사하지 않고 AI의 제안을 습관적으로 신뢰하도록 허용한다면 인간의 자율성을 훼손할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Generative AI use in political influence campaigns
points.label_ko: 정치적 영향력 캠페인에 생성적 AI 사용
points.definition_en: GPAI tools can be used in automation and scaling of influence campaigns [178]. Public opinion may be manipulated by targeted misleading or manipulative information. This can lead to rising political polarization and diminishing trust in public institutions.
points.definition_ko: GPAI 도구는 영향력 캠페인의 자동화 및 확장에 사용될 수 있습니다[178].

points.definition_en: Deepfakes are media that depict real or non-existent people or events, involving the use of multiple modalities (e.g., images, audio, video). They can also involve the imitation of speech or body movements of real people. Multimodal deepfakes can be used to harass, discredit, intimidate, and extort individuals.
points.definition_ko: 딥페이크는 다양한 양식(예: 이미지, 오디오, 비디오)을 사용하여 실제 또는 존재하지 않는 사람이나 사건을 묘사하는 미디어입니다.

points.definition_en: GPAIs can be misused for the automated generation of content personalized to target select individuals based on their weak spots [30]. Such attacks may be more efficient and more successful in achieving the goals of harassment, extortion, or intimidation.
points.definition_ko: GPAI는 약점을 기반으로 특정 개인을 대상으로 개인화된 콘텐츠를 자동 생성하는 데 오용될 수 있습니다[30].

points.label_en: Misuse for surveillance and population control
points.label_ko: 감시 및 인구 통제를 위한 오용
points.definition_en: AI tools can be misused by human or institutional actors for monitoring, controlling, or suppressing individuals [178]. Massive data collection and automated analysis are often conducted, and AI tools can further exacerbate such practices.
points.definition_ko: AI 도구는 개인이나 기관 행위자가 개인을 모니터링, 통제 또는 억압하기 위해 오용할 수 있습니다[178].

points.definition_en: AI systems embedded with systemic biases can manipulate large population segments, particularly when these biases align with the beliefs or behaviors of the targeted group. When weaponized at scale, this manipulation can exacerbate social divisions or cause large-scale disruptions, such as city-wide blackouts (e.g., by the manipulation of power consumption into the peak demand period [159]).
points.definition_ko: 체계적 편견이 내장된 AI 시스템은 특히 이러한 편견이 대상 그룹의 신념이나 행동과 일치할 때 대규모 인구 세그먼트를 조작할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Diminishing societal trust due to disinformation or manipulation
points.label_ko: 허위정보나 조작으로 인해 사회적 신뢰가 저하됨
points.definition_en: The use of GPAIs may contribute to the proliferation of either deliberate disinformation or unintended misinformation can severely erode trust in public figures and democratic institutions. This diminishing trust can extend to other forms of media, making the public less informed.
points.definition_ko: GPAI의 사용은 고의적인 허위 정보의 확산에 기여할 수 있으며, 의도하지 않은 잘못된 정보는 공인과 민주적 기관에 대한 신뢰를 심각하게 약화시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Automatic generation of disinformation can be personalized to target specific groups or individuals. Such attacks can be more effective in achieving their goals, and their costs can be significantly reduced when using GPAIs.
points.definition_ko: 허위 정보의 자동 생성은 특정 그룹이나 개인을 대상으로 개인화될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: GPAI outputs are not always correctly detected as AI-generated across multiple modalities (text, images, audio, video). A malicious actor can use GPAI outputs directly when communicating, or use AI-informed details to help construct a convincing impersonation (e.g., forging of supporting documents). Even if future countermeasures prove potent enough to detect GPAI-generated content, the risk remains if the countermeasures are not well known, or difficult to access.
points.definition_ko: GPAI 출력은 여러 형식(텍스트, 이미지, 오디오, 비디오)에 걸쳐 AI로 생성된 것으로 항상 올바르게 감지되지는 않습니다.

points.label_en: Automated discovery and exploitation of software systems
points.label_ko: 소프트웨어 시스템의 자동 검색 및 활용
points.definition_en: GPAIs can be used to aid in the automated discovery of software vulnerabilities [33]. This can empower malicious actors, making their cyberattacks more efficient and potentially more damaging. This type of automation allows attackers to expand the scale of their operations at a low cost, increasing the impact of their actions. New malware can be developed automatically, or the known vulnerabilities can be exploited to create more sophisticated attacks.
points.definition_ko: GPAI는 소프트웨어 취약점의 자동 발견을 돕기 위해 사용될 수 있습니다[33].

points.label_en: Models generating code with security vulnerabilities
points.label_ko: 보안 취약점이 있는 코드를 생성하는 모델
points.definition_en: Models can generate code or coding suggestions that contain security vulnerabilities. This may occur across various LLM-based model families, including more advanced models with superior coding performance, where the tendency to produce insecure code is even more pronounced [26].
points.definition_ko: 모델은 보안 취약성을 포함하는 코드 또는 코딩 제안을 생성할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Misuse of AI systems to assist in the creation of weapons
points.label_ko: 무기 제작을 돕기 위해 AI 시스템을 오용하는 행위
points.definition_en: AI systems may be misused to aid in the creation of weapons, such as chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear (CBRN) weapons, or augment the abilities of existing weapons, such as providing autonomous capabilities to unmanned weapon systems. Current systems do not significantly aid a malicious actor in these tasks, but they do show early signs [117]. This risk can sometimes be mitigated with input and output filtering, but is still susceptible to adversarial techniques (such as jailbreaking or paraphrasing).
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 CBRN(화학, 생물학, 방사능, 핵) 무기와 같은 무기 제작을 돕거나 무인 무기 시스템에 자율 기능을 제공하는 등 기존 무기의 능력을 강화하는 데 오용될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Models used for drug discovery, such as drug-target affinity prediction models, can be used to identify or develop dangerous toxins. This particularly concerning if the training data contains information related to potentially dangerous proteins and viruses.
points.definition_ko: 약물-표적 친화성 예측 모델과 같은 약물 발견에 사용되는 모델은 위험한 독소를 식별하거나 개발하는 데 사용될 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Homogenization or correlated failures in model derivatives
points.label_ko: 모델 파생 상품의 균질화 또는 상관 실패
points.definition_en: Homogenization refers to common methodologies and models used across downstream GPAI systems, which may lead to uniform failures and amplification of biases [176, 30]. This risk arises when numerous downstream AI systems are built upon a few large-scale foundation models.
points.definition_ko: 균질화는 다운스트림 GPAI 시스템 전반에 걸쳐 사용되는 일반적인 방법론과 모델을 말하며, 이는 균일한 실패와 편향의 증폭으로 이어질 수 있습니다[176, 30].

points.definition_en: AI systems may exhibit unfair or unfavorable outputs across a range of tasks against specific communities of people, either implicitly or explicitly. Bias can lead to forms of exclusion or erasure (e.g., mislabelling for categorization-based tasks) and violence (e.g., sexual violence against women from deepfake pornography).
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템은 암시적으로든 명시적으로든 특정 사람들의 커뮤니티에 대해 다양한 작업에서 불공정하거나 불리한 결과를 나타낼 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Dataset bias may be unintentionally amplified [60] where the outputs of the AI model trained on a dataset are more biased than the dataset itself.
points.definition_ko: 데이터 세트에 대해 훈련된 AI 모델의 출력이 데이터 세트 자체보다 더 편향된 경우 데이터 세트 편향이 의도치 않게 증폭될 수 있습니다[60].

points.label_en: High energy consumption of large models
points.definition_en: Training and deploying large models require substantial energy expenditure. The trend toward developing larger models exacerbates this issue. This can lead to excessive energy usage and have a negative environmental impact.
points.definition_ko: 대규모 모델을 훈련하고 배포하려면 상당한 에너지 소비가 필요합니다.

points.definition_en: While this by no means a modern problem, advances in AI could further enable actors to pursue their selfish incentives by overcoming the technical, legal, or social barriers that standardly help to prevent this. To take a plausible, near-term (if very low-stakes) example, an automated AI assistant could easily reserve a table at every restaurant in town in minutes, enabling the user to decide later and cancel all other reservations.
points.definition_ko: 이것이 결코 현대적인 문제는 아니지만 AI의 발전은 이를 방지하는 데 일반적으로 도움이 되는 기술적, 법적 또는 사회적 장벽을 극복함으로써 행위자가 이기적인 인센티브를 추구할 수 있게 해줍니다.

points.definition_en: To try to prevent collusion, we could monitor and constrain their communication (e.g., to be in natural language). However, models might secretly learn to communicate by concealing messages within other, non-secret text. Recent work on steganography using ML has demonstrated that this concern is well-founded (Hu et al., 2018; Mathew et al., 2024; Roger & Greenblatt, 2023; Schroeder de Witt et al., 2023b; Yang et al., 2019, see also Case Study 5). Secret communication could also occur via text compression (OpenAI, 2023c), or via the emergence of communication between agents where the symbols used by agents lack any predefined meanings or usage guidelines or are otherwise uninterpretable to humans (Foerster et al., 2016; Lazaridou & Baroni, 2020; Sukhbaatar et al., 2016).
points.definition_ko: 공모를 방지하기 위해 우리는 그들의 의사소통을 모니터링하고 제한할 수 있습니다(예: 자연어 사용).
