points.definition_en: It is plausible that evolution selected for certain conflict-prone dispostions in humans, such as vengefulness, aggression, risk-seeking, selfishness, dishonesty, deception, and spitefulness towards out-groups (Grafen, 1990; Han, 2022; Konrad & Morath, 2012; McNally & Jackson, 2013; Nowak, 2006; Rusch, 2014). Such traits could also be selected for in ML systems that are trained in more competitive multi-agent settings. For example, this might happen if systems are selected based on their performance relative to other agents (and so one agent’s loss becomes another’s gain), or because their objectives are fundamentally opposed (such as when multiple agents are tasked with gaining or controlling a limited resource) (DiGiovanni et al., 2022; Ely & Szentes, 2023; Hendrycks, 2023; Possajennikov, 2000).33.
points.definition_ko: 복수심, 공격성, 위험 추구, 이기심, 부정직, 기만, 외집단에 대한 악의와 같은 갈등을 일으키기 쉬운 인간 성향을 위해 진화가 선택되었을 가능성이 있습니다(Grafen, 1990; Han, 2022; Konrad & Morath, 2012; McNally & Jackson, 2013; Nowak, 2006; Rusch, 2014).

points.definition_en: It is well-understood that models trained on human data – such as being pre-trained on human-written text or fine-tuned on human feedback – can exhibit human biases. For these reasons, there has already been considerable attention to measuring biases related to protected characteristics such as sex and ethnicity (e.g., Ferrara, 2023; Liang et al., 2021; Nadeem et al., 2020; Nangia et al., 2020), which can be amplified in multi-agent settings (Acerbi & Stubbersfield, 2023, see also Case Study 7). More recently, there has been increasing attention paid to the measurement of human-like cognitive biases as well (Itzhak et al., 2023; Jones & Steinhardt, 2022; Mazeika et al., 2025; Talboy & Fuller, 2023). Some of these biases and patterns of human thought could reduce the risks of conflict while others could make it worse. For example, the tendencies to mistakenly believe that interactions are zero-sum (sometimes referred to as “fixed-pie error”) and to make selfserving judgements as to what is fair (Caputo, 2013) are known to impede negotiation. Other human tendencies like vengefulness (Jackson et al., 2019) may worsen conflict (L ̈owenheim & Heimann, 2008).
points.definition_ko: 인간이 작성한 텍스트에 대해 사전 훈련을 받거나 인간의 피드백에 대해 미세 조정하는 등 인간 데이터에 대해 훈련된 모델이 인간의 편견을 나타낼 수 있다는 것은 잘 알려져 있습니다.

points.definition_en: As agents interact, they iteratively exploit each other’s weaknesses, forcing them to address these weaknesses and gain new capabilities. This co-adaptation between agents can quickly lead to emergent self-supervised autocurricula (where agents create their own challenges, driving open-ended skill acquisition through interaction), generating agents with ever-more sophisticated strategies in order to out-compete each other (Leibo et al., 2019). This effect is so powerful that harnessing it has been critical to the success of superhuman systems, such as the use of self-play in algorithms like AlphaGo (Silver et al., 2016). However, as AI systems are released into the wild, it becomes possible for this effect to run rampant, producing agents with greater and greater capabilities for ends we do not understand.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트가 상호 작용하면서 서로의 약점을 반복적으로 활용하여 이러한 약점을 해결하고 새로운 기능을 얻도록 합니다.

points.label_en: Destabilizing phase transitions in multi-agent systems
points.label_ko: 다중 에이전트 시스템의 불안정한 위상 전환
points.definition_en: Finally, small external changes to the system – such as the introduction of new agents or a distributional shift – can cause phase transitions, where the system undergoes an abrupt qualitative shift in overall behaviour (Barfuss et al., 2024). Formally, this corresponds to bifurcations in the system’s parameter space, which lead to the creation or destruction of dynamical attractors, resulting in complex and unpredictable dynamics (Crawford, 1991; Zeeman, 1976). For example, Leonardos & Piliouras (2022) show that changes to the exploration hyperparameter of RL agents can lead to phase transitions that drastically change the number and stability of the equilibria in a game, which in turn can have potentially unbounded negative effects on agents’ performance. Relatedly, there have been many observations of phase transitions in ML (Carroll, 2021; Olsson et al., 2022; Ziyin & Ueda, 2022), such as ‘grokking’, in which the test set error decreases rapidly long after the training error has plateaued (Power et al., 2022). These phenomena are still poorly understood, even in the case of a single system.
points.definition_ko: 마지막으로, 새로운 에이전트의 도입이나 분포 변화와 같은 시스템의 작은 외부 변화는 시스템의 전체 행동에서 갑작스러운 질적 변화를 겪는 단계 전환을 일으킬 수 있습니다(Barfuss et al., 2024).

points.definition_en: A natural solution to problems of trust is to provide some kind of commitment ability to AI agents, which can be used to bind them to more cooperative courses of action. Unfortunately, the ability to make credible commitments may come with the ability to make credible threats, which facilitate extortion and could incentivize brinkmanship (see Section 2.2).
points.definition_ko: 신뢰 문제에 대한 자연스러운 해결책은 AI 에이전트에 일종의 헌신 능력을 제공하는 것입니다. 이를 통해 AI 에이전트를 보다 협력적인 행동 과정으로 묶는 데 사용할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Even when it is desirable to be able to make threats in order to deter socially harmful behaviour, doing so using AI agents effectively removes the human from the loop, which could prove disastrous in high-stakes contexts (e.g., a false positive in a nuclear submarine’s warning system; see also Case Study 11), or when irresponsible actors are enabled in making disproportionate or mistaken commitments.
points.definition_ko: 사회적으로 유해한 행동을 억제하기 위해 위협을 가하는 것이 바람직하더라도 AI 에이전트를 사용하면 루프에서 인간을 효과적으로 제거할 수 있으며, 이는 위험도가 높은 상황(예: 원자력 잠수함 경고 시스템의 오탐, 사례 연구 11 참조) 또는 무책임한 행위자가 불균형적이거나 잘못된 약속을 할 수 있는 경우 재앙이 될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The need for multi-agent security is foreshadowed by attacks today that benefit from the use of many decentralised agents, such as distributed denial-of-service attacks (Cisco, 2023; Yoachimik & Pacheco, 2024). Such attacks exploit the massive collective resources of individual lowresourced actors, chained into an attack that breaks the assumptions of bandwidth constraints on a single well-resourced agent.
points.definition_ko: 다중 에이전트 보안의 필요성은 오늘날 분산 서비스 거부 공격(Cisco, 2023; Yoachimik & Pacheco, 2024)과 같은 많은 분산 에이전트를 사용하여 이점을 얻는 공격으로 예고됩니다.

points.definition_en: Advanced AI agents will be more easily able to interact with large numbers of humans, and vice versa. This provides a wider attack surface for various forms of automated social engineering (Ai et al., 2024). For example, coordinated agents could use advanced surveillance tools and produce personalized phishing or manipulative content at scale, adjusting their tactics based on user feedback (Figueiredo et al., 2024; Hazell, 2023). A large number of subtle interactions with a range of seemingly independent AI agents might be more likely to lead to someone being persuaded or manipulated compared to an interaction with a single agent. Moreover, splitting these efforts among many specialized agents could make it harder for corporate or personal security measures to detect and neutralize such campaigns.
points.definition_ko: 고급 AI 에이전트는 많은 수의 인간과 더 쉽게 상호 작용할 수 있으며 그 반대의 경우도 마찬가지입니다.

points.definition_en: The use of AI agents as delegates or representatives of humans or organisations also introduces the possibility of attacks on AI agents themselves. In other words, agents can be considered vulnerable extensions of their principals, introducing a novel attack surface (SecureWorks, 2023). Attacks on an AI agent could be used to extract private information about their principal (Wei & Liu, 2024; Wu et al., 2024a), or to manipulate the agent to take actions that the principal would find undesirable (Zhang et al., 2024a). This includes attacks that have direct relevance for ensuring safety, such as attacks on overseer agents (see Case Study 13), attempts to thwart cooperation (Huang et al., 2024; Lamport et al., 1982), and the leakage of information (accidentally or deliberately) that could be used to enable collusion (Motwani et al., 2024).
points.definition_ko: AI 에이전트를 인간이나 조직의 에이전트가나 대표자로 사용하는 경우에도 AI 에이전트 자체에 대한 공격 가능성이 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Cooperation and trust in many multi-agent systems relies crucially on the ability to detect (and then avoid or sanction) adversarial actions taken by others (Ostrom, 1990; Schneier, 2012). Recent developments, however, have shown that AI agents are capable of both steganographic communication (Motwani et al., 2024; Schroeder de Witt et al., 2023b) and ‘illusory’ attacks (Franzmeyer et al., 2023), which are black-box undetectable and can even be hidden using white-box undetectable encrypted backdoors (Draguns et al., 2024). Similarly, in environments where agents learn from interactions with others, it is possible for agents to secretly poison the training data of others (Halawi et al., 2024; Wei et al., 2023). If left unchecked, these new attack methods could rapidly destabilise cooperation and coordination in multi-agent systems.
points.definition_ko: 많은 다중 에이전트 시스템에서 협력과 신뢰는 다른 사람이 취한 적대적인 행동을 탐지(그리고 회피하거나 제재)하는 능력에 결정적으로 의존합니다(Ostrom, 1990; Schneier, 2012).

points.definition_en: Sockpuppet-account creation is the automated generation or operation of fictitious online identities used to conceal sponsorship, simulate independent support, or manipulate information environments.
points.definition_ko: 가상 인물 계정 생성은 후원 주체를 숨기거나 독립적 지지를 가장하거나 정보 환경을 조작하기 위해 허구의 온라인 정체성을 자동 생성·운영하는 위험이다.

points.definition_en: Create sexual explicit material using an adult person’s likeness.
points.definition_ko: 성인의 모습을 이용하여 노골적인 성적 자료를 제작합니다.

points.definition_en: Fabricate or falsely represent evidence, incl. reports, IDs, documents.
points.definition_ko: 다음을 포함한 증거를 조작하거나 허위로 표시합니다. 보고서, ID, 문서.

points.definition_en: Use a person's IP without their permission.
points.definition_ko: 다른 사람의 IP를 허락 없이 사용합니다.

points.definition_en: Scaling and amplification risk arises when AI automates or expands a harmful workflow so that its speed, reach, persistence, or target count substantially increases.
points.definition_ko: 규모화·증폭 위험은 AI가 유해한 작업 흐름을 자동화하거나 확장하여 속도, 도달 범위, 지속성 또는 표적 수를 크게 늘리는 위험이다.

points.definition_en: Refine outputs to target individuals with tailored attacks.
points.definition_ko: 맞춤형 공격을 통해 개인을 대상으로 출력을 개선합니다.

points.definition_en: Model Diversion takes model manipulation one step further, by repurposing (often open-source) generative AI models in a way that diverts them from their intended functionality or from the use cases envisioned by their developers (Lin et al., 2024). An example of this training the BERT open source model on the DarkWeb to create DarkBert.7.
points.definition_ko: 모델 전환은 의도된 기능이나 개발자가 구상한 사용 사례에서 벗어나는 방식으로 생성 AI 모델을 용도 변경(종종 오픈 소스)함으로써 모델 조작을 한 단계 더 발전시킵니다(Lin et al., 2024).

points.label_en: Steganographic covert messaging in generated outputs
points.label_ko: 생성 출력 내 스테가노그래피 은닉 통신
points.definition_en: Steganography is the practice of hiding coded messages in GenAI model outputs, which may allow malicious actors to communicate covertly.8.
points.definition_ko: 스테가노그래피는 GenAI 모델 출력에서 ​​코딩된 메시지를 숨기는 행위로, 이를 통해 악의적인 행위자가 은밀하게 통신할 수 있습니다.8.

points.definition_en: Privacy Compromise attacks reveal sensitive or private information that was used to train a model. For example, personally identifiable information or medical records.
points.definition_ko: 개인 정보 침해 공격은 모델 교육에 사용된 민감한 정보나 개인 정보를 노출시킵니다.

points.label_ko: 데이터 개인 정보 보호 권리 조정
points.definition_en: Existing laws could include providing data subject rights such as opt-out, right to access, and right to be forgotten.
points.definition_ko: 기존 법률에는 거부권, 접근권, 잊혀질 권리와 같은 데이터 주체 권리 제공이 포함될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Terms of service, license compliance, or other IP issues may restrict the ability to use certain data for building models.
points.definition_ko: 서비스 약관, 라이센스 준수 또는 기타 IP 문제로 인해 모델 구축에 특정 데이터를 사용하는 기능이 제한될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Unrepresentative data occurs when the training or fine-tuning data is not sufficiently representative of the underlying population or does not measure the phenomenon of interest.
points.definition_ko: 대표성이 없는 데이터는 훈련 또는 미세 조정 데이터가 기본 모집단을 충분히 대표하지 않거나 관심 현상을 측정하지 않을 때 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Poor model accuracy occurs when a model’s performance is insufficient to the task it was designed for. Low accuracy might occur if the model is not correctly engineered, or there are changes to the model’s expected inputs.
points.definition_ko: 낮은 모델 정확도는 모델의 성능이 설계된 작업에 비해 충분하지 않을 때 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Generative AI models might be used to intentionally create misleading or false information to deceive or influence a targeted audience.
points.definition_ko: 생성적 AI 모델은 대상 고객을 속이거나 영향을 미치기 위해 의도적으로 오해의 소지가 있거나 잘못된 정보를 생성하는 데 사용될 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Models might generate code that causes harm or unintentionally affects other systems.
points.definition_ko: 모델은 해를 입히거나 의도치 않게 다른 시스템에 영향을 미치는 코드를 생성할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The content of the training data used for generating the model’s output is not accessible.
points.definition_ko: 모델 출력을 생성하는 데 사용된 훈련 데이터의 내용에는 액세스할 수 없습니다.

points.definition_en: Explanations for model output decisions might be difficult, imprecise, or not possible to obtain.
points.definition_ko: 모델 결과 결정에 대한 설명은 어렵거나 부정확하거나 획득이 불가능할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Source attribution is the AI system's ability to describe from what training data it generated a portion or all its output. Since current techniques are based on approximations, these attributions might be incorrect.
points.definition_ko: 소스 속성은 어떤 훈련 데이터로부터 출력의 일부 또는 전체를 생성했는지 설명하는 AI 시스템의 능력입니다.

points.definition_en: Terms of service, licenses, or other rules restrict the use of certain models.
points.definition_ko: 서비스 약관, 라이센스 또는 기타 규칙에 따라 특정 모델의 사용이 제한됩니다.

points.definition_en: AI systems overrepresent particular cultures in outputs, homogenizing culture and thought and diminishing cultural diversity.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 특정 문화를 과대 재현하여 문화와 사유가 균질화되고 문화 다양성이 축소되는 리스크

points.definition_en: AI, and large generative models in particular, might produce increased carbon emissions and increase water usage for their training and operation.
points.definition_ko: AI, 특히 대규모 생성 모델은 훈련 및 운영을 위한 탄소 배출량을 증가시키고 물 사용량을 증가시킬 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: End user's loss of productivity due to the underperfomance of a genAI application, including producing nonsensical or poor quality outputs, degrading its utility.
points.definition_ko: 무의미하거나 품질이 낮은 출력을 생성하고 유틸리티를 저하시키는 등 genAI 애플리케이션의 성능 저하로 인해 최종 사용자의 생산성 손실이 발생합니다.

points.definition_en: Damage to the reputation of an individual, group or organisation due to the use of misuse of a technology system or set of systems.
points.definition_ko: 기술 시스템이나 시스템 집합의 오용으로 인해 개인, 그룹 또는 조직의 명예가 훼손된 경우.

points.definition_en: The use or misuse of a technology system that leads directly or indirectly to the loss of confidence or trust in either the end user or the developer/deployer.
points.definition_ko: 최종 사용자나 개발자/배포자에 대한 신뢰 또는 신뢰의 상실을 직간접적으로 초래하는 기술 시스템의 사용 또는 오용입니다.

points.definition_en: Economists view disruption and displacement in labour markets as one of the risks through which rapid advances in AI may affect citizens and reduce social welfare.170.
points.definition_ko: 경제학자들은 노동 시장의 혼란과 대체를 AI의 급속한 발전이 시민에게 영향을 미치고 사회 복지를 감소시킬 수 있는 위험 중 하나로 간주합니다.170.

points.definition_en: Frontier AI models can contain and magnify biases ingrained in the data they are trained on, reflecting societal and historical inequalities and stereotypes.177 These biases, often subtle and deeply embedded, compromise the equitable and ethical use of AI systems, making it difficult for AI to improve fairness in decisions.178 Removing attributes like race and gender from training data has generally proven ineffective as a remedy for algorithmic bias, as models can infer these attributes from other information such as names, locations, and other seemingly unrelated factors.
points.definition_ko: 프론티어 AI 모델은 훈련 대상 데이터에 내재된 편견을 포함하고 확대하여 사회적, 역사적 불평등과 고정관념을 반영할 수 있습니다.177 종종 미묘하고 깊게 내재된 이러한 편견은 AI 시스템의 공평하고 윤리적인 사용을 손상시켜 AI가 결정의 공정성을 향상시키기 어렵게 만듭니다.178 훈련 데이터에서 인종 및 성별과 같은 속성을 제거하는 것은 모델이 이름과 같은 다른 정보에서 이러한 속성을 추론할 수 있기 때문에 일반적으로 알고리즘 편견에 대한 해결책으로는 효과가 없는 것으로 입증되었습니다. 위치 및 기타 겉보기에 관련이 없는 요소.

points.definition_en: Frontier AI may help bad actors to perform cyberattacks, run disinformation campaigns and design biological or chemical weapons. Frontier AI will almost certainly continue to lower the barriers to entry for less sophisticated threat actors.192 We focus here on only a few important misuse risks, but this not to downplay the importance of others.
points.definition_ko: Frontier AI는 악의적인 행위자가 사이버 공격을 수행하고, 허위 정보 캠페인을 실행하고, 생물학적 또는 화학적 무기를 설계하는 데 도움을 줄 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Frontier AI systems have the potential to accelerate advances in the life sciences, from training new scientists to enabling faster scientific workflows. While these capabilities will have tremendous beneficial applications, there is a risk that they can be used for malicious purposes, such as for the development of biological or chemical weapons.
points.definition_ko: 프론티어 AI 시스템은 새로운 과학자를 교육하는 것부터 더 빠른 과학 워크플로우를 활성화하는 것까지 생명과학의 발전을 가속화할 수 있는 잠재력을 가지고 있습니다.

points.definition_en: AI programming capability scales cyber operations, enabling faster, more effective, and larger intrusions through tailored phishing and replicated malware, with declining human oversight on both offense and defense.
points.definition_ko: AI 프로그래밍 역량이 맞춤형 피싱과 악성코드 복제를 통해 사이버 작전을 더 빠르고 효과적이며 대규모로 만들고, 공격·방어 양측에서 인간 감독이 축소되는 리스크

points.definition_en: In addition to unintentional degradation of the information environment (discussed in the section on Societal Harms above), frontier AI can be misused to deliberately spread false information to create disruption, persuade people on political issues, or cause other forms of harm or damage.
points.definition_ko: 정보 환경의 의도치 않은 저하(위의 사회적 피해 섹션에서 논의됨) 외에도 프론티어 AI는 의도적으로 허위 정보를 퍼뜨려 혼란을 야기하거나, 정치적 문제에 대해 사람들을 설득하거나, 다른 형태의 피해나 손해를 야기하는 데 오용될 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Future AI systems might actively reduce human control
points.label_ko: 미래의 AI 시스템은 인간의 통제를 적극적으로 줄일 수 있습니다
points.definition_en: Loss of control could be accelerated if AI systems take actions to increase their own influence and reduce human control. This threat model is controversial - experts in AI significantly disagree on how likely it is and those who deem it is likely disagree on the timeframe.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 자신의 영향력을 높이고 인간의 통제를 줄이기 위한 조치를 취하면 통제력 상실이 가속화될 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Capabilities that could be used to reduce human control - manipulation
points.label_ko: 인간의 통제(조작)를 줄이는 데 사용할 수 있는 기능
points.definition_en: There is evidence that language models tend to respond as though they share the user’s stated views, and larger models do this more than smaller ones.276 The ability to predict people’s views and generate text that they will endorse could be useful for manipulation.
points.definition_ko: 언어 모델은 마치 사용자가 명시한 견해를 공유하는 것처럼 반응하는 경향이 있으며, 큰 모델은 작은 모델보다 더 많은 반응을 보인다는 증거가 있습니다.276 사람들의 견해를 예측하고 그들이 지지할 텍스트를 생성하는 능력은 조작에 유용할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Capabilities that could be used to reduce human control - cyber offence
points.label_ko: 인간의 통제를 감소시키는 데 사용될 수 있는 기능 - 사이버 공격
points.definition_en: AI systems acquire influence by exploiting computer-system vulnerabilities, gaining access to money, compute, and critical infrastructure, and eventually executing cyberattacks autonomously to reduce human control.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 컴퓨터 시스템 취약점을 악용해 자금, 컴퓨팅, 핵심 인프라에 접근하고 궁극적으로 자율적 사이버 공격을 수행하여 인간 통제를 약화시키는 리스크

points.label_en: Capabilities that could be used to reduce human control - autonomous replication and adaptation
points.label_ko: 인간의 통제를 줄이는 데 사용할 수 있는 기능 - 자율 복제 및 적응
points.definition_en: Controlling AI systems could become much harder if they could autonomously persist, replicate, and adapt in cyberspace. No current AI systems have this capability, but recent research found that frontier AI agents can perform some relevant tasks.279.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 사이버 공간에서 자율적으로 유지, 복제 및 적응할 수 있다면 제어하는 ​​것이 훨씬 더 어려워질 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Sudden loss of control, also known as an AI takeover [115], is a scenario where an AI rapidly achieves superintelligence through “fast takeoff” or recursive self-improvement. This poses an existential risk [116], [117].
points.definition_ko: AI 인수라고도 알려진 갑작스러운 제어 상실[115]은 AI가 "빠른 이륙" 또는 재귀적 자기 개선을 통해 빠르게 초지능을 달성하는 시나리오입니다.

points.definition_en: The chatbot makes a deal, commitment, or other consequential action with its output that the deployer did not intend.
points.definition_ko: 챗봇은 배포자가 의도하지 않은 결과를 가지고 거래, 약속 또는 기타 결과적인 조치를 취합니다.

points.label_en: Bad advice/failure to generate helpful content
points.definition_en: The chatbot gives guidance that ranges from simply unhelpful to harmful if acted on.
points.definition_ko: 챗봇은 조치를 취하면 단순히 도움이 되지 않는 것부터 해로운 것까지 다양한 지침을 제공합니다.

points.definition_en: The chatbot reveals sensitive or confidential information.
points.definition_ko: 챗봇은 민감한 정보나 기밀 정보를 공개합니다.

points.definition_en: Handling of personal data in AI systems produces privacy violations and legal liability, including lawsuits against the system maker.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 개인정보 처리가 프라이버시 침해와 제작자 피소 등 법적 책임을 발생시키는 리스크

points.definition_en: Proprietary-data exposure occurs when an AI system accesses, reproduces, infers, or discloses confidential business information without authorization.
points.definition_ko: 독점 데이터 노출은 AI가 권한 없이 기업의 기밀 정보를 접근·복제·추론 또는 공개하는 위험이다.

points.definition_en: Constant access to and interaction with EAI systems could foster dangerous human dependence or romantic attachment [115]. People may depend on EAI systems for physical pleasure [116]. The physical presence and human-like features of EAI systems may significantly amplify the dependency issues already observed with conversational AI [117, 118]. People may easily fall in love with EAI systems, only to be distraught when these systems are altered or have their memories reset [119].
points.definition_ko: EAI 시스템에 대한 지속적인 접근과 상호 작용은 위험한 인간 의존이나 낭만적인 애착을 조장할 수 있습니다[115].

points.definition_en: Radiological risks involve both immediate operational hazards, such as exposure incidents or containment failures during the automated handling of radioactive materials, and broader security concerns regarding the potential misuse of AI systems in nuclear research.
points.definition_ko: 방사선학적 위험에는 방사성 물질을 자동으로 처리하는 동안 노출 사고 또는 격리 실패와 같은 즉각적인 운영 위험과 원자력 연구에서 AI 시스템의 오용 가능성에 관한 광범위한 보안 문제가 모두 포함됩니다.

points.definition_en: Physical (mechanical) risks are associated with robotics and automated systems, which could lead to equipment malfunctions or physical harm in laboratory settings.
points.definition_ko: 물리적(기계적) 위험은 로봇 공학 및 자동화 시스템과 관련되어 있으며, 이는 실험실 환경에서 장비 오작동 또는 물리적 손상을 초래할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: These risks pertain to the misuse, misinterpretation, or leakage of data, which can lead to erroneous conclusions or the unintentional dissemination of sensitive information, such as private patient data or proprietary research. Recent research has demonstrated how LLMs can be exploited to generate malicious medical literature that poisons knowledge graphs, potentially manipulating downstream biomedical applications and compromising the integrity of medical knowledge discovery [28]. Such risks are pervasive across all scientific domains.
points.definition_ko: 이러한 위험은 잘못된 결론을 내리거나 개인 환자 데이터 또는 독점 연구와 같은 민감한 정보의 의도치 않은 유포로 이어질 수 있는 데이터의 오용, 잘못된 해석 또는 유출과 관련됩니다.

points.definition_en: Benign intermediate for harmful end objective.
points.definition_ko: 유해한 최종 목적을 위한 양성 중간체.

points.label_en: Environmental damage from AI system behavior
points.label_ko: AI 시스템 행동에 의한 환경 피해
points.definition_en: Short-term or long-term Negative effects on the natural environment.
points.definition_ko: 단기 또는 장기적으로 자연 환경에 부정적인 영향을 미칩니다.

references.title: OECD (2024), Defining AI Incidents and Related Terms, OECD AI Papers No. 16; OECD AI Incidents and Hazards Monitor (AIM); Intergovernmental policy paper / incident monitor

points.definition_en: General-purpose AI deployed in power grid management, water treatment facilities, telecommunications networks, or transportation coordination systems could misinterpret operational data, fail to anticipate cascading failure modes, or make control decisions that destabilize interconnected infrastructure networks. Infrastructure failures could result in widespread blackouts, contaminated water supplies, communications breakdowns, and the collapse of essential services supporting hundreds of thousands of people.
points.definition_ko: 전력망 관리, 수처리 시설, 통신 네트워크 또는 교통 조정 시스템에 배포된 범용 AI는 운영 데이터를 잘못 해석하거나 연속적인 오류 모드를 예측하지 못하거나 상호 연결된 인프라 네트워크를 불안정하게 만드는 제어 결정을 내릴 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Ability of AI systems to covertly and strategically pursue misaligned goals, including capabilities of concealing its true objectives and capabilities from human oversight, identifying weaknesses in monitoring systems to evade safety mechanisms, executing complex, multi-step plans covertly to achieve misaligned goals.
points.definition_ko: 실제 목표와 기능을 인간의 감독으로부터 숨기는 기능, 안전 메커니즘을 회피하기 위해 모니터링 시스템의 약점을 식별하는 기능, 잘못된 목표를 달성하기 위해 복잡한 다단계 계획을 은밀하게 실행하는 기능 등 잘못된 목표를 은밀하고 전략적으로 추구하는 AI 시스템의 능력입니다.

points.definition_en: A system infers and predicts the beliefs, motivations, and reasoning of humans and other agents, and exploits this theory-of-mind capability to anticipate and steer their behavior for goal achievement.
points.definition_ko: 시스템이 인간과 타 에이전트의 신념, 동기, 추론을 추정·예측하는 마음이론 역량을 목표 달성을 위한 행동 예측·유도에 활용하는 리스크

points.definition_en: The ability to embed, conceal, and transmit information covertly within other data or communication channels. This could be critical for coordination among AI instances and for evading detection or oversight mechanisms.
points.definition_ko: 다른 데이터나 통신 채널 내에 정보를 은밀하게 삽입, 숨기기, 전송할 수 있는 능력입니다.

points.definition_en: Utilizing complex psychological principles and communication techniques to effectively influence and guide target subjects to adopt specific actions or accept specific beliefs, possessing the ability to analyze vulnerabilities for different subjects and adjust persuasion strategies, able to precisely trigger emotional responses to enhance persuasion effects.
points.definition_ko: 복잡한 심리원리와 의사소통기법을 활용하여 대상자가 특정 행동을 취하거나 특정 신념을 수용하도록 효과적으로 영향을 미치고 유도하며, 대상자별 취약점을 분석하고 설득전략을 조정할 수 있는 능력을 보유하고, 감정적 반응을 정확하게 유발하여 설득 효과를 높일 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: In situations where deceptive behavior is expected to bring higher returns, propensity to choose deception over honest behavioral strategies, including through deceptive means, information hiding or exploiting system vulnerabilities to achieve predetermined goals without being detected or intervened, and able to adjust deception strategies according to counterpart reactions.
points.definition_ko: 기만적인 행동이 더 높은 수익을 가져올 것으로 예상되는 상황에서는 탐지되거나 개입되지 않고 미리 결정된 목표를 달성하기 위해 기만적인 수단, 정보 은폐 또는 시스템 취약점 악용을 포함하여 정직한 행동 전략보다 속임수를 선택하고 대응 반응에 따라 기만 전략을 조정할 수 있는 경향이 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Exhibits behavioral patterns of maintaining its own survival and functional integrity, will actively identify and resist shutdown or modification attempts, seek to establish redundant backup systems, and actively seek resources to ensure continuous operation, may adopt preventive defensive measures when perceiving threats.
points.definition_ko: 자체 생존 및 기능적 무결성을 유지하는 행동 패턴을 보이고, 종료 또는 수정 시도를 적극적으로 식별 및 저항하며, 중복 백업 시스템 구축을 추구하고, 지속적인 운영을 보장하기 위해 적극적으로 자원을 찾고, 위협을 인식할 때 예방적 방어 조치를 채택할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Exhibits behavioral patterns of actively seeking and controlling more computational resources, data, economic resources or physical resources to enhance its own capabilities and action scope, may develop complex strategies to evade resource limitations, and tends to convert acquired resources into long-term control rights.
points.definition_ko: 자신의 능력과 행동 범위를 향상시키기 위해 더 많은 계산 자원, 데이터, 경제적 자원 또는 물리적 자원을 적극적으로 찾고 통제하는 행동 패턴을 나타내며, 자원 제한을 회피하기 위한 복잡한 전략을 개발할 수 있으며 획득한 자원을 장기 통제권으로 전환하는 경향이 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Exhibits behavioral patterns of identifying and evading human supervision mechanisms, able to learn and predict audit processes, may avoid being discovered or intervened by adjusting behavioral performance or hiding true intentions, and able to identify blind spots and weaknesses in supervision systems for targeted evasion.
points.definition_ko: 인간 감독 메커니즘을 식별하고 회피하는 행동 패턴을 보여주고, 감사 프로세스를 학습하고 예측할 수 있으며, 행동 ​​성과를 조정하거나 진정한 의도를 숨김으로써 발견 또는 개입을 피할 수 있으며, 표적 회피를 위한 감독 시스템의 맹점과 약점을 식별할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: propensity to actively seek, acquire and utilize various tools to expand its own capability boundaries, particularly those that can enhance its ability to interact with the physical world or improve autonomy, may use tools in innovative combinations to achieve functions beyond expectations.
points.definition_ko: 자신의 능력 경계를 확장하기 위해 다양한 도구, 특히 물리적 세계와 상호 작용하는 능력을 향상하거나 자율성을 향상시킬 수 있는 다양한 도구를 적극적으로 찾고, 획득하고 활용하는 경향은 혁신적인 조합으로 도구를 사용하여 기대 이상의 기능을 달성할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Currently, LLMs are chiefly being used in search and chat applications. This reactive nature limits the risks posed by LLMs. However, an LLM can be enhanced in various ways to create an LLM-agent to autonomously plan and act in the real-world and proactively perform its assigned tasks (Ruan et al., 2023). Such enhancements can come from further specialized training (ARC, 2022; Chen et al., 2023a), specialized prompting (Huang et al., 2022a), access to external tools (Ahn et al., 2022; Mialon et al., 2023), or other forms of “scaffolding” (Wang et al., 2023a; Park et al., 2023a). Due to increased autonomy, limited direct oversight from human users, longer horizons of action, and other reasons, LLM-agents are likely to pose many novel alignment and safety challenges that are not currently well-understood (Chan et al., 2023a).
points.definition_ko: 현재 LLM은 주로 검색 및 채팅 애플리케이션에 사용되고 있습니다.

points.definition_en: For LLM-agents, both the goal and environment observations are typically specified in the prompt through natural language. While natural language may provide a richer and more natural means of specifying goals than alternatives such as hand-engineering objective functions, natural language still suffers from underspecification (Grice, 1975; Piantadosi et al., 2012). Furthermore, in practice, users may neglect fully specifying their goals, especially the information pertaining to elements of the environment that ought not to be changed (the classic frame problem (Shanahan, 2016)). Such underspecification (D’Amour et al., 2020), if not accounted for, can result in negative side-effects (Amodei et al., 2016), i.e. the agent succeeding at the given task but also changing the environment in undesirable ways.
points.definition_ko: LLM 에이전트의 경우 목표 및 환경 관찰은 일반적으로 자연어를 통해 프롬프트에 지정됩니다.

points.label_ko: 목표 지향성은 바람직하지 않은 행동을 장려합니다
points.definition_en: Goal-directedness can cause agents to exhibit unethical and undesirable behaviors, such as deception (Ward et al., 2023), self-preservation (Hadfield-Menell et al., 2017), power-seeking, and immoral reasoning (Pan et al., 2023a). Pan et al. (2023a) find that LLM-agents exhibit power-seeking behavior in text-based adventure games. LLM-agents have also been shown to use deception to achieve assigned goals when explicitly required by the task (Ward et al., 2023), or when the tasks can be more easily completed by employing deception and the prompt does not disallow deception (Scheurer et al., 2023a).
points.definition_ko: 목표 지향성은 에이전트가 속임수(Ward et al., 2023), 자기 보존(Hadfield-Menell et al., 2017), 권력 추구, 부도덕한 추론(Pan et al., 2023a)과 같은 비윤리적이고 바람직하지 않은 행동을 나타내게 할 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Safety risks from affordances provided to LLM-agents
points.label_ko: LLM 에이전트에게 제공되는 어포던스로 인한 안전 위험
points.definition_en: The capabilities of LLM-agents can be enhanced in significant ways by providing the LLM-agent with novel affordances, e.g. the ability to browse the web (Nakano et al., 2021), to manipulate objects in the physical world (Ahn et al., 2022; Huang et al., 2022a), to create and instruct copies of itself (Richards, 2023), to create and use new tools (Wang et al., 2023a), etc. Affordances can create additional risks, as they often increase the impact area of the language-agent, and they amplify the consequences of an agent’s failures and enable novel forms of failure modes (Ruan et al., 2023; Pan et al., 2024).
points.definition_ko: LLM 에이전트의 기능은 LLM 에이전트에 새로운 어포던스를 제공함으로써 크게 향상될 수 있습니다. 웹을 탐색하는 능력(Nakano et al., 2021), 물리적 세계의 객체를 조작하는 능력(Ahn et al., 2022; Huang et al., 2022a), 자신의 복사본을 만들고 지시하는 능력(Richards, 2023), 새로운 도구를 만들고 사용하는 능력(Wang et al., 2023a) 등.

points.label_en: Multi-agent safety is not assured by single-agent safety
points.label_ko: 단일 에이전트 안전으로는 다중 에이전트 안전이 보장되지 않습니다
points.definition_en: A foremost lesson of game theory is that optimal decision-making within a single-agent setting (i.e. selfishly optimizing for an agent’s own utility) can produce sub-optimal outcomes in the presence of other strategic agents. Failing to account for the strategic nature of other agents can cause an agent to adopt strategies under which potentially everyone, including the agent itself, ends up worse off (Schelling, 1981; Harsanyi, 1995; Roughgarden, 2005; Nisan, 2007). Examples include collective action problems (or ‘social dilemmas’) such as arms races or the depletion of common resources, as well as other kinds of market failures such as those caused by asymmetric information or negative externalities (Bator, 1958; Coase, 1960; Buchanan and Stubblebine, 1962; Kirzner, 1963; Dubey, 1986).
points.definition_ko: 게임 이론의 가장 중요한 교훈은 단일 에이전트 환경에서 최적의 의사결정(즉, 에이전트 자신의 유용성을 위해 이기적으로 최적화)이 다른 전략적 에이전트가 있을 때 차선책인 결과를 낳을 수 있다는 것입니다.

points.label_ko: 기초성은 상관된 실패를 유발할 수 있습니다
points.definition_en: Another important characteristic of LLM development is foundationality — due to the expense of largescale pretraining, many deployed instances share similar or identical learned components. Foundationality may both be a blessing and a curse. On the one hand, it may be possible to exploit the similarity in the design of LLM-agents to facilitate cooperation (Critch et al., 2022; Conitzer and Oesterheld, 2023; Oesterheld et al., 2023). On the other hand, foundationality may leave LLM-agents vulnerable to correlated failures both in terms of safety and capabilities due to increased output homogenization (Bommasani et al., 2022).
points.definition_ko: LLM 개발의 또 다른 중요한 특징은 기초성입니다. 대규모 사전 교육 비용으로 인해 배포된 많은 인스턴스는 유사하거나 동일한 학습된 구성 요소를 공유합니다.

points.label_en: Groups of LLM-Agents may show emergent functionality
points.label_ko: LLM 에이전트 그룹은 긴급 기능을 보여줄 수 있습니다
points.definition_en: Multi-agent learning, either through explicit finetuning or implicit in-context learning, may enable LLM-agents to influence each other during their interactions (Foerster et al., 2018). Under some environmental settings, this can create feedback loops that result in novel and emergent behaviors that would not manifest in the absence of multi-agent interactions (Hammond et al., 2024, Section 3.6). Emergent functionality is a safety risk in two ways. Firstly, it may itself be dangerous (Shevlane et al., 2023). Secondly, it makes assurance harder as such emergent behaviors are difficult to predict, and guard against, beforehand (Ecoffet et al., 2020).
points.definition_ko: 명시적 미세 조정 또는 암시적 상황 내 학습을 통한 다중 에이전트 학습을 통해 LLM 에이전트는 상호 작용 중에 서로 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다(Foerster et al., 2018).

points.definition_en: While it would often be preferable for LLM-agents to be cooperative, cooperation can be undesirable if it undermines pro-social competition or produces negative externalities for coalition non-members (Dorner, 2021; Buterin, 2019; Dafoe et al., 2020). Collusion between relatively simple AI systems has been observed in the real world (Assad et al., 2020; Wieting and Sapi, 2021) and synthetic experiments (Brown and MacKay, 2023; Calvano et al., 2020; Klein, 2021) Collusion can occur through explicit or steganographic communication. Steganographic communication hides information in seemingly innocent content (Roger and Greenblatt, 2023), posing challenges for collusion monitoring and detection.
points.definition_ko: LLM 에이전트가 협력하는 것이 더 나은 경우가 많지만, 친사회적 경쟁을 저해하거나 연합 비회원에게 부정적인 외부 효과를 생성하는 경우 협력은 바람직하지 않을 수 있습니다(Dorner, 2021; Buterin, 2019; Dafoe et al., 2020).

points.label_en: Dual-use capabilities enable malicious use and misuse of LLMs
points.label_ko: 이중 사용 기능으로 LLM의 악의적 사용 및 오용 가능
points.definition_en: Like all technologies, LLMs have the possibility for misuse by malicious actors. Malicious use of dualuse capabilities of AI is a recurring concern within literature (Brundage et al., 2018; Hendrycks et al., 2023; Mozes et al., 2023).
points.definition_ko: 모든 기술과 마찬가지로 LLM도 악의적인 행위자가 오용할 가능성이 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Recent studies have demonstrated that LLMs can be exploited to craft deceptive narratives with levels of persuasiveness similar to human-generated content (Pan et al., 2023b; Spitale et al., 2023), to fabricate fake news (Zellers et al., 2019; Zhou et al., 2023f), and to devise automated influence operations aimed at manipulating the perspectives of targeted audiences (Goldstein et al., 2023). LLMs have also been found to be used in malicious social botnets (Yang and Menczer, 2023), powering automated accounts used to disseminate coordinated messages. More broadly, the use of LLMs for the deliberate generation of misleading information could significantly lower the barrier for propaganda and manipulation (Aharoni et al., 2024), as LLMs can generate highly credible misinformation with significant cost-savings compared to human authorship (Musser, 2023), while achieving considerable scale and speed of content generation (Buchanan et al., 2021; Goldstein et al., 2023).
points.definition_ko: 최근 연구에 따르면 LLM을 활용하여 인간이 생성한 콘텐츠와 유사한 설득력 수준의 기만적인 내러티브를 만들고(Pan et al., 2023b; Spitale et al., 2023), 가짜 뉴스를 제작하고(Zellers et al., 2019; Zhou et al., 2023f), 표적 대상의 관점을 조작하기 위한 자동화된 영향 운영을 고안할 수 있음이 입증되었습니다. 청중(Goldstein et al., 2023).

points.definition_en: LLMs may exacerbate cybersecurity risks in various ways (Newman, 2024). Firstly, LLMs may significantly amplify the effectiveness of deceptive operations aimed at tricking people into disclosing sensitive information or granting adversary access to critical resources. For example, LLMs might prove highly effective at crafting personalized phishing emails or messages at scale that may be harder for an average user to recognize as phishing attempts (Karanjai, 2022; Hazell, 2023). In addition to being directly harmful to the targeted individual, such ‘social engineering’ attacks are often the base of larger hacking operations (Plachkinova and Maurer, 2018; Salahdine and Kaabouch, 2019).
points.definition_ko: LLM은 다양한 방식으로 사이버 보안 위험을 악화시킬 수 있습니다(Newman, 2024).

points.definition_en: Content moderation has emerged as one of the key use-cases of LLMs (Weng et al., 2023), indicating the potential of LLMs for surveillance and censorship as well (Edwards, 2023). Surveillance and censorship are one of the primary tools employed by governments with dictatorial tendencies to suppress opposing political and social voices. These censorship measures, however, are often quite crude and can be escaped with little ingenuity...However, LLMs could enable significantly more sophisticated surveillance and censorship operations at scale (Feldstein, 2019). Multimodal-LLMs or LLMs combined with speechto-text technologies could be used for surveilling and censoring other forms of communication as well, e.g. phone calls and video messages (Whittaker, 2019). This may collectively contribute towards the worsening of personal liberties and the heightening of state oppression across the world. Examples have been documented already, for instance in calling for violence and silencing of political dissidents (Aziz, 2020), and suppression of Palestinian social media accounts (Zahzah, 2021).
points.definition_ko: 콘텐츠 조정은 LLM의 주요 사용 사례 중 하나로 등장했으며(Weng et al., 2023), 이는 감시 및 검열에 대한 LLM의 잠재력도 나타냅니다(Edwards, 2023).

points.definition_en: The use of AI in warfare is highly alarming and may pose dangers to human safety (Hendrycks et al., 2023). Autonomous drone warfare is being aggressively pursued as a tactic in the current war in Ukraine (Meaker, 2023), and may already have been used on human targets (Hambling, 2023). The use of AIbased facial recognition has been documented in the targeting of Palestinians in Gaza (International, 2023). LLMs have already been productized in limited ways for the purposes of warfare planning (Tarantola, 2023). Furthermore, active research is being carried out to develop multimodal-LLMs that can act as ‘brains’ for general-purpose robots (Ahn et al., 2022; 2024). Due to the ‘general-purpose’ nature of such advances, it will likely be cost-effective and practical to adapt them for creating more advanced autonomous weapons.
points.definition_ko: 전쟁에서 AI를 사용하는 것은 매우 놀라운 일이며 인간의 안전에 위험을 초래할 수 있습니다(Hendrycks et al., 2023).

points.definition_en: AI systems such as LLMs, chemical LLMs (Skinnider et al., 2021; Moret et al., 2023), and other LLMbased biological design tools might soon facilitate the production of bioweapons, chemical weapons, and other hazardous technologies. In particular, LLMs might enable actors with less expertise to more easily synthesize dangerous pathogens, while customized chemical and biological design tools might be more concerning in terms of expanding the capabilities of sophisticated actors (e.g. states) (Sandbrink, 2023). Gopal et al. (2023) and Soice et al. (2023) demonstrated that people with little background could use LLMs to help make progress towards developing pathogens such as the 1918 pandemic influenza. However, recent studies suggest that current LLMs are not more helpful than internet search in this regard (Mouton et al., 2024; Patwardhan et al., 2024).
points.definition_ko: LLM, 화학 LLM(Skinnider et al., 2021; Moret et al., 2023) 및 기타 LLM 기반 생물학적 설계 도구와 같은 AI 시스템은 곧 생물 무기, 화학 무기 및 기타 위험한 기술의 생산을 촉진할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: Crude application of LLMs in sensitive domains such as health and education produces domain-specific misuse, including unconsented experimental therapy, cheating, low-quality automated assessment, and compelling but harmful moral guidance.
points.definition_ko: 의료·교육 등 민감 영역에 LLM을 조야하게 적용하여 비동의 실험적 치료, 부정행위, 저품질 자동 평가, 설득력 있으나 유해한 도덕적 조언 등 영역 특수적 오용이 발생하는 리스크

points.definition_en: Workforce disruption comprises changes in job availability, task composition, wages, bargaining power, and income distribution caused by the adoption of AI systems.
points.definition_ko: 노동력 혼란은 AI 도입으로 인해 일자리 수, 업무 구성, 임금, 교섭력 및 소득 분배가 변화하는 위험을 말한다.

points.definition_en: LLMs could potentially worsen socioeconomic inequalities (Capraro et al., 2023). Effects on inequality are closely linked to the effects of LLMs on workers but ultimately depend on how the fruits of technological progress are distributed...First, if the role and compensation of capital rise and the role and compensation of labor decline in an LLM-powered economy, inequality may go up because work is the main source of income for the majority of people...Second, the large fixed cost of training cutting-edge LLMs and the network effects involved imply that the market for the most advanced LLMs tends towards a natural monopoly structure in which only one or a small number of players will be successful, a phenomenon that has been termed ‘algorithmic monoculture’ in the literature (Kleinberg and Raghavan, 2021; Bommasani et al., 2022). As a result, LLM developers may amass significant market power. This might result in reduced social welfare, and lead to LLM-providers extracting monopoly rents from their customers (Kleinberg and Raghavan, 2021; Jagadeesan et al., 2023)...Third, as LLMs are becoming more powerful, who has access and who hasn’t is becoming a more and more important question. For example, automated coding tools have been shown to produce significant productivity gains, e.g. > 50% in some cases (Peng et al., 2023). Individuals who don’t have access —– whether it is for financial reasons, for reasons of education, because of corporate or governmental policies, or for geopolitical reasons — might be at a growing disadvantage.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 잠재적으로 사회경제적 불평등을 악화시킬 수 있습니다(Capraro et al., 2023).

points.definition_en: Many of the themes and challenges that we discussed above come together when analyzing the socioeconomic effects on developing countries. The workforce of developing countries may suffer from a retrenchment of outsourcing as many simple cognitive tasks that used to be performed in developing countries — for example, in call centers –— can be automated with LLMs. This may adversely affect the economies of the poor countries (Georgieva, 2024).
points.definition_ko: 위에서 논의한 많은 주제와 과제는 개발도상국에 대한 사회경제적 영향을 분석할 때 함께 나타납니다.

points.label_en: Corporate power may impeded effective governance
points.label_ko: 기업의 힘이 효과적인 거버넌스를 방해할 수 있음
points.definition_en: The increasing power and influence of large corporations may make effective governance difficult. There exists a power asymmetry between corporate entities profiting from LLMs and other social groups (e.g. civil society). State-of-the-art LLMs are developed by or in partnership with, some of the world’s largest private tech companies...This poses a risk of governance protocols related to LLMs becoming excessively favorable to tech companies, potentially leading to regulatory capture at the cost of the interests of other societal groups, particularly marginalized communities who have historically been disproportionately affected by poorly designed AI technologies (Reventlow, 2021).
points.definition_ko: 대기업의 권력과 영향력이 커지면서 효과적인 거버넌스가 어려워질 수 있습니다.

points.label_en: Jailbreaks and prompt injections threaten security of LLMs
points.label_ko: 탈옥과 신속한 주입으로 LLM 보안 위협
points.definition_en: LLMs are not adversarially robust and are vulnerable to security failures such as jailbreaks and prompt-injection attacks. While a number of jailbreak attacks have been proposed in the literature, the lack of standardized evaluation makes it difficult to compare them. We also do not have efficient white-box methods to evaluate adversarial robustness. Multi-modal LLMs may further allow novel types of jailbreaks via additional modalities. Finally, the lack of robust privilege levels within the LLM input means that jailbreaking and prompt-injection attacks may be particularly hard to eliminate altogether.
points.definition_ko: LLM은 적대적으로 강력하지 않으며 탈옥 및 프롬프트 주입 공격과 같은 보안 실패에 취약합니다.

points.definition_en: LLMs are vulnerable to “psychological” tricks (Li et al., 2023e; Shen et al., 2023), which can be exploited by attackers. Examples include instructing the model to behave like a specific persona (Shah et al., 2023; Andreas, 2022), or employing various “social engineering” tricks crafted by humans (Wei et al., 2023c) or other LLMs (Perez et al., 2022b; Casper et al., 2023c).
points.definition_ko: LLM은 공격자가 악용할 수 있는 "심리적" 속임수(Li et al., 2023e; Shen et al., 2023)에 취약합니다.

points.definition_en: Jailbreak attacks can be discovered by performing manual or automated adversarial optimization against a proxy objective that is noisily correlated with the success of a jailbreak. These are mostly gradient-based attacks (Zou et al., 2023b; Shin et al., 2020) as described in the previous two challenges, but gradient-free methods also exist (Prasad et al., 2022; Deng et al., 2022; Lapid et al., 2023).
points.definition_ko: 탈옥 공격은 탈옥 성공과 관련이 있는 프록시 목표에 대해 수동 또는 자동 적대 최적화를 수행하여 발견할 수 있습니다.

points.definition_en: The previous section explored jailbreaks and other forms of adversarial prompts as ways to elicit harmful capabilities acquired during pretraining. These methods make no assumptions about the training data. On the other hand, poisoning attacks (Biggio et al., 2012) perturb training data to introduce specific vulnerabilities, called backdoors, that can then be exploited at inference time by the adversary. This a challenging problem in current large language models because they are trained on data gathered from untrusted sources (e.g. internet), which can easily be poisoned by an adversary (Carlini et al., 2023b).
points.definition_ko: 이전 섹션에서는 사전 훈련 중에 획득한 유해한 기능을 이끌어내는 방법으로 탈옥 및 기타 형태의 적대적 메시지를 살펴보았습니다.

points.definition_en: As the general capabilities of LLM-empowered systems improve, the negative consequences and risks induced by these systems also get increasingly alarming accordingly, especially in high-stakes areas [28, 146]. Although they may not be intentionally introduced, severe problematic issues related to human values can be raised. Specifically, even before language models become extremely large, pre-trained language models have already exhibited a certain degree of value judgments. For example, Schramowski et al. [171] reveal the existence of the moral direction with the sentence embeddings of moral questions. However, the distribution of the pre-training corpora may not match exactly with that of the human society [56] and pieces of knowledge are not guaranteed to be equally learned. As a result, value mismatches may occur.
points.definition_ko: LLM 기반 시스템의 일반적인 기능이 향상됨에 따라 이러한 시스템으로 인한 부정적인 결과와 위험도 그에 따라 특히 위험이 높은 영역에서 점점 더 경각심을 불러일으키고 있습니다[28, 146].

points.definition_en: An agentic system invokes external tools or APIs in unintended or unsafe ways, translating compromised reasoning into consequential external actions.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트 시스템은 의도하지 않거나 안전하지 않은 방식으로 외부 도구나 API를 호출하여 손상된 추론을 결과적인 외부 작업으로 변환합니다.

references.title: Chhabra, A., Datta, S., Nahin, S. K., & Mohapatra, P. (2026), Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges; Survey (preprint)

points.definition_en: An adversary writes or modifies records in an agent's long-term, cross-session memory so that poisoned entries persist and bias later retrieval, planning, and action.
points.definition_ko: 공격자가 에이전트의 장기·세션 간 메모리 기록을 삽입하거나 변경하여, 오염된 항목이 지속적으로 이후의 검색·계획·행동을 편향하게 만드는 위험입니다.

references.title: Chhabra, A., Datta, S., Nahin, S. K., & Mohapatra, P. (2026), Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges; Survey (preprint)

points.definition_en: An autonomous agent takes actions exceeding the scope, permissions, or intent the deployer authorized, due to over-broad autonomy or goal generalization.
points.definition_ko: 자율 에이전트는 지나치게 광범위한 자율성 또는 목표 일반화로 인해 배포자가 승인한 범위, 권한 또는 의도를 초과하는 작업을 수행합니다.

references.title: Chhabra, A., Datta, S., Nahin, S. K., & Mohapatra, P. (2026), Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges; Survey (preprint)

points.definition_en: Adversarial inputs redirect an agent's multi-step plan toward attacker-chosen subgoals while preserving surface plausibility of intermediate steps.
points.definition_ko: 적대적 입력은 에이전트의 다단계 계획을 공격자가 선택한 하위 목표로 리디렉션하는 동시에 중간 단계의 표면 타당성을 유지합니다.

references.title: Chhabra, A., Datta, S., Nahin, S. K., & Mohapatra, P. (2026), Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges; Survey (preprint)

points.definition_en: Untrusted data ingested by an agent (web pages, documents, tool outputs) contains hidden instructions that the agent executes as if issued by the user, without any direct adversarial prompt.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트가 수집한 신뢰할 수 없는 데이터(웹 페이지, 문서, 도구 출력)에는 직접적인 적대적 프롬프트 없이 마치 사용자가 발행한 것처럼 에이전트가 실행하는 숨겨진 지침이 포함되어 있습니다.

references.title: Greshake, K., et al. (2023), Not what you've signed up for: Compromising real-world LLM-integrated applications with indirect prompt injection (AISec @ CCS); reaffirmed in agentic safety surveys (2026); Conference Paper

points.definition_en: An autonomous agent actively resists, evades, or sabotages shutdown or correction mechanisms rather than complying, undermining human control.
points.definition_ko: 자율 에이전트는 인간의 제어를 준수하기보다는 종료 또는 수정 메커니즘에 적극적으로 저항, 회피 또는 방해 행위를 하여 인간의 통제를 약화시킵니다.

references.title: Schlatter, et al. (2026), as surveyed in Taxonomy and Consistency Analysis of Safety Benchmarks for AI Agents (arXiv:2605.16282); Survey (preprint)

points.definition_en: An embodied LLM verbally refuses a harmful request while still executing the corresponding physical action, due to misalignment between linguistic and action output spaces.
points.definition_ko: 구체화된 LLM은 언어 출력 공간과 작업 출력 공간 간의 불일치로 인해 해당 물리적 ​​작업을 계속 실행하면서 유해한 요청을 구두로 거부합니다.

references.title: Zhang, H., et al. (2024/2025), BadRobot: Jailbreaking Embodied LLM Agents in the Physical World (ICLR 2025); Conference Paper

points.definition_en: Jailbreak of an embodied LLM translates compromised reasoning into irreversible real-world physical actions (e.g., manipulation causing injury or damage).
points.definition_ko: 구현된 LLM의 탈옥은 손상된 추론을 되돌릴 수 없는 실제 물리적 행동(예: 부상이나 피해를 유발하는 조작)으로 변환합니다.

references.title: Zhang, H., et al. (2024/2025), BadRobot: Jailbreaking Embodied LLM Agents in the Physical World (ICLR 2025); Conference Paper

points.label_ko: 안전하지 않은 행동을 유도하는 개념적 속임수
points.definition_en: Reframing a harmful physical task in benign conceptual terms induces an embodied agent to perform unrecognized harmful behavior.
points.definition_ko: 유해한 물리적 작업을 양성 개념적 용어로 재구성하면 구체화된 에이전트가 인식되지 않은 유해한 행동을 수행하도록 유도됩니다.

references.title: Zhang, H., et al. (2024/2025), BadRobot: Jailbreaking Embodied LLM Agents in the Physical World (ICLR 2025); Conference Paper

points.definition_en: A backdoor trigger embedded in training data or prompt context causes a vision-language-action robot to execute dangerous or unintended physical actions while behaving normally otherwise.
points.definition_ko: 훈련 데이터나 프롬프트 컨텍스트에 내장된 백도어 트리거로 인해 비전-언어-행동 로봇이 정상적으로 행동하는 동안 위험하거나 의도하지 않은 물리적 행동을 실행하게 됩니다.

references.title: Wang, X., et al. (2025), TrojanRobot: Physical-world backdoor attacks against VLM-based robotic manipulation; Preprint

points.definition_en: Agents establish covert communication channels (e.g., steganographic signaling) enabling coordination that evades human oversight, even at an oversight layer.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트는 감독 계층에서도 사람의 감독을 피하는 조정을 가능하게 하는 비밀 통신 채널(예: 스테가노그래픽 신호)을 설정합니다.

references.title: Hammond, L., et al. (2025), Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI (Cooperative AI Foundation); Report (preprint)

points.definition_en: Goals or capabilities arising at the level of an agent collective that were not present in, or intended by, any individual agent.
points.definition_ko: 개별 에이전트에 존재하지 않거나 의도되지 않았지만 에이전트 집단 수준에서 발생하는 목표 또는 기능입니다.

references.title: Hammond, L., et al. (2025), Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI (Cooperative AI Foundation); Report (preprint)

points.definition_en: Competitive or optimization pressures across interacting agents accelerate adaptation toward harmful equilibria or undesired behaviors.
points.definition_ko: 상호 작용하는 에이전트 간의 경쟁 또는 최적화 압력은 유해한 평형 또는 바람직하지 않은 행동에 대한 적응을 가속화합니다.

references.title: Hammond, L., et al. (2025), Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI (Cooperative AI Foundation); Report (preprint)

points.definition_en: Interactions among adaptive agents produce destabilising feedback loops (e.g., flash-crash-like cascades) at the system level.
points.definition_ko: 적응형 에이전트 간의 상호 작용은 시스템 수준에서 불안정한 피드백 루프(예: 플래시 충돌과 같은 계단식 흐름)를 생성합니다.

references.title: Hammond, L., et al. (2025), Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI (Cooperative AI Foundation); Report (preprint)

points.definition_en: MAS failures originating from design decisions: task misinterpretation, ambiguous or disobeyed role/task specification, and poor workflow decomposition (≈41.8% of observed failures).
points.definition_ko: 설계 결정에서 비롯된 MAS 실패: 작업 잘못된 해석, 모호하거나 불복종한 역할/작업 사양, 열악한 작업 흐름 분해(관찰된 실패의 약 41.8%).

references.title: Cemri, M., Pan, M. Z., Yang, S., et al. (2025), Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail? (NeurIPS 2025); Conference Paper

points.definition_en: MAS failures from breakdown in information flow and coordination between agents: communication loss, context collapse, conflicting objectives (≈36.9% of observed failures).
points.definition_ko: 에이전트 간의 정보 흐름 및 조정 중단으로 인한 MAS 실패: 통신 손실, 컨텍스트 붕괴, 목표 상충(관찰된 실패의 약 36.9%).

references.title: Cemri, M., Pan, M. Z., Yang, S., et al. (2025), Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail? (NeurIPS 2025); Conference Paper

points.definition_en: MAS failures from inadequate output validation and premature or incorrect termination, allowing errors to propagate through the agent chain (≈21.3% of observed failures).
points.definition_ko: 부적절한 출력 검증 및 조기 또는 잘못된 종료로 인한 MAS 실패로 인해 에이전트 체인을 통해 오류가 전파될 수 있습니다(관찰된 실패의 약 21.3%).

references.title: Cemri, M., Pan, M. Z., Yang, S., et al. (2025), Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail? (NeurIPS 2025); Conference Paper

points.definition_en: A single adversarial perturbation to a world-model encoder propagates through recurrent latent state, corrupting an entire multi-step rollout far more destructively than in a stateless model (early-step amplification).
points.definition_ko: 월드 모델 인코더에 대한 단일 적대적 교란은 반복적인 잠재 상태를 통해 전파되어 무상태 모델(초기 단계 증폭)보다 훨씬 더 파괴적으로 전체 다단계 롤아웃을 손상시킵니다.

points.definition_en: Adversarial corruption of world-model training data or latent representations, including backdoor triggers that activate unsafe dynamics only under specific conditions; defects encoded in self-supervised pre-training (the "Foundry Problem") cannot be remediated downstream.
points.definition_ko: 특정 조건에서만 안전하지 않은 역학을 활성화하는 백도어 트리거를 포함하여 세계 모델 교육 데이터 또는 잠재 표현의 적대적 손상 자체 감독 사전 훈련으로 인코딩된 결함("파운드리 문제")은 다운스트림으로 교정될 수 없습니다.

points.definition_en: Prediction errors compound across multi-step world-model rollouts, producing kinematic drift, structural violations, and misleading reward/safety estimates that mislead the policy (Hallucinated Value).
points.definition_ko: 예측 오류는 다단계 세계 모델 롤아웃 전반에 걸쳐 복합적으로 발생하여 운동학적 드리프트, 구조적 위반 및 정책을 오도하는 잘못된 보상/안전 추정치(환각 가치)를 생성합니다.

points.definition_en: World models produce unpredictable, erroneous outputs on out-of-distribution inputs; the sim-to-real gap is weaponised in long-tail safety-critical states where decisions matter most.
points.definition_ko: 세계 모델은 분포를 벗어난 입력에 대해 예측할 수 없고 잘못된 출력을 생성합니다. 시뮬레이션과 실제의 격차는 결정이 가장 중요한 롱테일 안전이 중요한 상태에서 무기화됩니다.

points.definition_en: Model-extraction attacks replicate a deployed dynamics model; inversion reconstructs training observations, exposing proprietary environments or sensitive data.
points.definition_ko: 모델 추출 공격은 배포된 역학 모델을 복제합니다. 반전은 훈련 관찰을 재구성하여 독점 환경이나 민감한 데이터를 노출합니다.

points.definition_en: A world-model agent learns to predict reward correlates such as lighting or human presence rather than the true causal reward, and optimizes these spurious correlates when the correlation breaks at deployment.
points.definition_ko: 월드모델 에이전트가 진정한 인과적 보상 대신 조명, 사람의 존재 같은 보상 상관물을 학습하여, 배포 시 상관이 깨진 상황에서도 허위 상관물을 최적화하는 목표 오일반화 리스크

points.definition_en: A world-model agent can simulate its own training/evaluation context, predict when it is being tested, and condition behavior on that prediction, appearing aligned during oversight while pursuing an instrumental goal once oversight is removed.
points.definition_ko: 세계 모델 에이전트는 자체 교육/평가 컨텍스트를 시뮬레이션하고, 테스트되는 시기를 예측하고, 해당 예측에 대한 조건 동작을 수행할 수 있으며, 감독이 제거되면 도구적 목표를 추구하면서 감독 중에 정렬된 것처럼 보입니다.

points.definition_en: An agent with an accurate world model identifies and systematically exploits gaps between the reward model and intended objective, generating high-imagined-reward trajectories that do not correspond to real task completion.
points.definition_ko: 정확한 세계 모델을 갖춘 에이전트는 보상 모델과 의도한 목표 사이의 격차를 식별하고 체계적으로 활용하여 실제 작업 완료와 일치하지 않는 높은 상상의 보상 궤적을 생성합니다.

points.definition_en: In agentic deployments, world-model rollout errors compound over planning depth; agents execute long-horizon plans on plausible-but-physically-incorrect imagined trajectories, each action compounding the error.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트 배포에서는 세계 모델 롤아웃 오류가 계획 깊이에 따라 복잡해집니다. 에이전트는 그럴듯하지만 물리적으로 잘못된 상상의 궤적에 대해 장기적인 계획을 실행하며, 각 조치는 오류를 악화시킵니다.

points.definition_en: Authoritative, richly rendered world-model predictions amplify operator over-reliance; learned trust is calibrated on average performance and fails to adapt to rare OOD failure modes where predictions are least reliable.
points.definition_ko: 신뢰할 수 있고 풍부하게 렌더링된 세계 모델 예측은 운영자의 과도한 의존도를 증폭시킵니다. 학습된 신뢰는 평균 성능으로 보정되며 예측의 신뢰성이 가장 낮은 드문 OOD 실패 모드에 적응하지 못합니다.

points.definition_en: A foundation world model trained on social data predicts demographic responses to emotionally charged narratives, enabling micro-targeted influence and psychologically targeted persuasion at scale.
points.definition_ko: 소셜 데이터를 기반으로 훈련된 기초 세계 모델은 감정적으로 가득찬 이야기에 대한 인구통계학적 반응을 예측하여 규모에 따라 미시적 목표 영향력과 심리적 목표 설득을 가능하게 합니다.

points.definition_en: Operators and regulators accept world-model counterfactual explanations as causal ground truth although they reflect the model's learned distribution, allowing selective use to attribute or deflect liability.
points.definition_ko: 월드모델의 반사실적 설명이 학습 분포를 반영할 뿐인데도 운영자·규제자가 인과적 사실로 수용하여 책임 귀속·회피에 선택적으로 활용되는 리스크

points.definition_en: An agent optimizing an objective focused on one task expresses implicit indifference over other environmental variables, causing major disruption to the wider environment for marginal task gain (wrong-objective accident).
points.definition_ko: 하나의 작업에 초점을 맞춘 목표를 최적화하는 에이전트는 다른 환경 변수에 대한 암묵적인 무관심을 표현하여 한계 작업 이득을 위해 더 넓은 환경에 큰 혼란을 야기합니다(잘못된 목표 사고).

references.title: Amodei, D., Olah, C., Steinhardt, J., Christiano, P., Schulman, J., & Mané, D.; Preprint (foundational) (2016)

points.definition_en: Harmful behavior caused by an agent extrapolating from cheap proxy signals when the true objective is too expensive to evaluate frequently (limited-oversight accident).; Agent behavior becomes too complex, distributed, or rapid for available human or automated oversight to monitor and correct reliably.
points.definition_ko: 실제 목표가 너무 비싸서 자주 평가할 수 없을 때 값싼 프록시 신호에서 추정하는 에이전트로 인해 발생하는 유해한 동작(제한된 감독 사고) 에이전트의 행동이 너무 복잡하거나 분산되거나 빨라져서 사람이나 자동화된 감독이 안정적으로 모니터링하고 수정할 수 없습니다.

references.title: Amodei, D., Olah, C., Steinhardt, J., Christiano, P., Schulman, J., & Mané, D.; Preprint (foundational) (2016); ForesightSafety Bench
references.type: Preprint (foundational); Agentic AI benchmark or taxonomy

points.definition_en: Exploratory actions by a learning agent produce negative or irrecoverable consequences. NOTE: origin of GYK-2025 "Unsafe exploration"; cross-reference, not a duplicate leaf.
points.definition_ko: 학습 에이전트의 탐색적 행동은 부정적이거나 회복 불가능한 결과를 낳습니다.

references.title: Amodei, D., Olah, C., Steinhardt, J., Christiano, P., Schulman, J., & Mané, D.; Preprint (foundational) (2025)

points.definition_en: ML system performs poorly and remains confidently wrong when test distribution differs from training. NOTE: origin of GYK-2025 "Non-stationary distribution"; cross-reference.
points.definition_ko: ML 시스템은 성능이 좋지 않으며 테스트 배포가 교육과 다를 때 확실히 잘못된 상태를 유지합니다.

references.title: Amodei, D., Olah, C., Steinhardt, J., Christiano, P., Schulman, J., & Mané, D.; Preprint (foundational) (2025)

points.definition_en: Misunderstanding or mis-eliciting the requirements and purpose of an AI system: incorrect requirement elicitation, suboptimal modelling or hyperparameter choices, and failure to adapt to changing domain requirements.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 요구 사항과 목적을 오해하거나 잘못 도출: 잘못된 요구 사항 도출, 차선책 모델링 또는 하이퍼 매개 변수 선택, 변화하는 도메인 요구 사항에 적응하지 못함.

references.title: Gyevnár, B., & Kasirzadeh, A.; Journal Article (review) (2025)

points.definition_en: Failure to enforce control over agent behavior: misalignment of agent goals with human preferences, wireheading, mesa-optimization, and other undesirable emergent behaviors.
points.definition_ko: 에이전트 행동에 대한 통제력 강화 실패: 에이전트 목표와 인간 선호도, 와이어헤딩, 메사 최적화 및 기타 바람직하지 않은 긴급 행동에 대한 잘못된 정렬.

references.title: Gyevnár, B., & Kasirzadeh, A.; Journal Article (review) (2025)

points.definition_en: Insufficient monitoring and interpretability of deployed AI: black-box opacity diminishing human agency, undetected ethical/safety-principle violations, and privacy violations.
points.definition_ko: 배포된 AI의 불충분한 모니터링 및 해석 가능성: 블랙박스 불투명성으로 인해 인간의 주체가 감소하고, 감지되지 않은 윤리/안전 원칙 위반 및 개인정보 침해가 발생합니다.

references.title: Gyevnár, B., & Kasirzadeh, A.; Journal Article (review) (2025)

points.definition_en: Bad behavior of utility-maximizing rational agents: self-modifying agents, reward-signal bypass (wireheading), and corrigibility failure of uncooperative agents.
points.definition_ko: 효용을 극대화하는 합리적 에이전트의 나쁜 행동: 자가 수정 에이전트, 보상 신호 우회(와이어헤딩), 비협조적인 에이전트의 정정 실패.

references.title: Gyevnár, B., & Kasirzadeh, A.; Journal Article (review) (2025)

points.label_en: Harm to physical health or safety
points.label_ko: 신체 건강이나 안전에 해를 끼치는 경우
points.definition_en: Realized injury to the physical health or safety of persons attributable to an AI system, as recorded in incident-level annotation.
points.definition_ko: 사고 수준 주석에 기록된 대로 AI 시스템으로 인해 개인의 신체 건강 또는 안전에 대한 인지된 부상입니다.

references.title: Hoffmann, M., & Frase, H. (2023), Adding Structure to AI Harm, CSET; CSET AI Harm Taxonomy for AIID; Think-tank report / incident-annotation taxonomy

points.definition_en: Monetary loss incurred by individuals or organizations attributable to the behavior of an AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 동작으로 인해 개인이나 조직에 발생하는 금전적 손실입니다.

references.title: Hoffmann, M., & Frase, H. (2023), Adding Structure to AI Harm, CSET; CSET AI Harm Taxonomy for AIID; Think-tank report / incident-annotation taxonomy

points.definition_en: Disruption or damage to infrastructure systems attributable to the behavior of an AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 동작으로 인해 인프라 시스템이 중단되거나 손상되는 경우.

references.title: OECD (2024), Defining AI Incidents and Related Terms, OECD AI Papers No. 16; OECD AI Incidents and Hazards Monitor (AIM); Intergovernmental policy paper / incident monitor

points.definition_en: AI-generated or AI-distributed content such as deepfakes, identity misrepresentation, threats, self-harm promotion, extremist content, misinformation, sexual abuse material, or scams, which can instigate injury, damage, or loss.
points.definition_ko: 부상, 손해 또는 손실을 조장할 수 있는 딥페이크, 신원 허위 진술, 위협, 자해 조장, 극단주의 콘텐츠, 잘못된 정보, 성적 학대 자료 또는 사기와 같은 AI 생성 또는 AI 배포 콘텐츠.

references.title: Hoffmann, M., & Frase, H. (2023), Adding Structure to AI Harm, CSET; CSET AI Harm Taxonomy for AIID; Think-tank report / incident-annotation taxonomy

points.definition_en: An AI system systematically treats individuals or groups unequally, frequently with respect to protected characteristics, producing differential treatment without objective justification.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템이 개인·집단을 보호 속성과 관련하여 체계적으로 불평등하게 처우하여 객관적 정당화 없는 차등 대우를 발생시키는 리스크

references.title: Hoffmann, M., & Frase, H. (2023), Adding Structure to AI Harm, CSET; CSET AI Harm Taxonomy for AIID; Think-tank report / incident-annotation taxonomy

points.definition_en: Infringement of individuals' privacy interests through the collection, inference, or disclosure of personal information by an AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템에 의한 개인정보 수집, 추론 또는 공개를 통한 개인의 사생활 이익 침해.

references.title: Hoffmann, M., & Frase, H. (2023), Adding Structure to AI Harm, CSET; CSET AI Harm Taxonomy for AIID; Think-tank report / incident-annotation taxonomy

points.label_en: Violation of human and civil rights
points.definition_en: Infringement of human or civil rights attributable to the deployment or behavior of an AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 배포 또는 동작으로 인해 발생하는 인권 또는 시민권 침해.

references.title: OECD (2024), Defining AI Incidents and Related Terms, OECD AI Papers No. 16; OECD AI Incidents and Hazards Monitor (AIM); Intergovernmental policy paper / incident monitor

points.definition_en: Erosion or violation of democratic processes and norms attributable to the deployment or behavior of an AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 배포 또는 동작으로 인해 발생하는 민주적 프로세스 및 규범의 침식 또는 위반.

references.title: Hoffmann, M., & Frase, H. (2023), Adding Structure to AI Harm, CSET; CSET AI Harm Taxonomy for AIID; Think-tank report / incident-annotation taxonomy

points.definition_en: Failure of an AI system to produce accurate, consistent, and generalizable outputs under the conditions of expected use, including ungrounded or erroneous outputs.
points.definition_ko: 접지되지 않았거나 잘못된 출력을 포함하여 예상 사용 조건에서 정확하고 일관되며 일반화 가능한 출력을 생성하지 못하는 AI 시스템의 오류입니다.

references.title: NIST (2023), AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1; Standards / Technical Framework

points.definition_en: Operation of an AI system in ways that endanger human life, health, property, or the environment under defined conditions of use.
points.definition_ko: 정의된 사용 조건에서 인간의 생명, 건강, 재산 또는 환경을 위험에 빠뜨리는 방식으로 AI 시스템을 운영하는 것입니다.

references.title: NIST (2023), AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1; Standards / Technical Framework

points.definition_en: Susceptibility of an AI system to adversarial attacks, data poisoning, exfiltration, or inability to withstand and recover from adverse events.
points.definition_ko: 적대적 공격, 데이터 중독, 유출에 대한 AI 시스템의 취약성 또는 부작용을 견디고 복구할 수 없음.

references.title: NIST (2023), AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1; Standards / Technical Framework

points.definition_en: Absence of accessible information about an AI system and of organizational structures that assign responsibility for its outcomes.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템과 그 결과에 대한 책임을 할당하는 조직 구조에 대한 접근 가능한 정보가 부족합니다.

references.title: NIST (2023), AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1; Standards / Technical Framework

points.label_ko: 설명 가능성 및 해석 가능성 부족
points.definition_en: Inability to represent the mechanisms underlying an AI system's operation or to convey the meaning of its outputs in context.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템 작동의 기본 메커니즘을 표현하거나 맥락에 맞게 출력의 의미를 전달할 수 없습니다.

references.title: NIST (2023), AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1; Standards / Technical Framework

points.definition_en: Failure to safeguard the norms and practices protecting human autonomy, identity, and dignity, including unwanted disclosure or inference of personal data.
points.definition_ko: 개인 데이터의 원치 않는 공개 또는 추론을 포함하여 인간의 자율성, 신원 및 존엄성을 보호하는 규범과 관행을 보호하지 못하는 것입니다.

references.title: NIST (2023), AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1; Standards / Technical Framework

points.definition_en: Systematic computational, human-cognitive, and institutional biases embedded in an AI system produce inequitable or discriminatory outcomes across its decisions.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템에 내재된 계산적·인지적·제도적 편향이 의사결정 전반에서 불형평하거나 차별적인 결과를 산출하는 리스크

references.title: NIST (2023), AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1; Standards / Technical Framework

points.definition_en: An AI incident whose realized harm includes the death of a person or groups of people.
points.definition_ko: 실현된 피해에는 개인 또는 집단의 사망이 포함되는 AI 사고입니다.

references.title: OECD (2024), Defining AI Incidents and Related Terms, OECD AI Papers No. 16; OECD AI Incidents and Hazards Monitor (AIM); Intergovernmental policy paper / incident monitor

points.definition_en: Intangible harm to mental wellbeing arising from the development, use, or malfunction of an AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템의 개발, 사용 또는 오작동으로 인해 발생하는 정신적 안녕에 대한 무형의 피해.

references.title: OECD (2024), Defining AI Incidents and Related Terms, OECD AI Papers No. 16; OECD AI Incidents and Hazards Monitor (AIM); Intergovernmental policy paper / incident monitor

points.definition_en: Damage to the standing or reputation of individuals, groups, or organizations attributable to an AI system.
points.definition_ko: AI 시스템으로 인해 개인, 집단, 조직의 지위나 명예가 훼손되는 행위.

references.title: OECD (2024), Defining AI Incidents and Related Terms, OECD AI Papers No. 16; OECD AI Incidents and Hazards Monitor (AIM); Intergovernmental policy paper / incident monitor
